title: The File of Fatima - Episode 1: Illuminations Before Starting
speaker: Sheikh Abdul-Halim al-Ghizzi

content.text: Overview: Episode 1

In the inaugural episode of the File of Fatima, Sheikh al-Ghizzi outlines his investigative methodology, rejecting the manipulated game of chains of narration (Asanid) in favor of forensic analysis of historical contexts (Qara'in). He establishes a profound Quranic principle of complicity, **demonstrating that those who validate the oppressors of the Ahlulbayt bear the guilt of their crimes, with the blood of Fatima smeared on their clothes until the Day of Resurrection.**

Drawing from highly sensitive and often censored texts across both Sunni and Shia traditions—including Sahih Muslim, Ibn Qutaybah's Al-Imamah wa al-Siyasah, al-Juwayni's Fara'id al-Simtayn, a suppressed letter in Bihar al-Anwar, and the foundational Kamil al-Ziyarat—the Sheikh **exposes a systematic campaign of burying the truth**. He conclusively proves through these diverse historical fragments that the Prophet's daughter did not merely die of grief, but was brutally oppressed, usurped, and murdered following a violent assault on her home, **setting the stage for a detailed examination of her martyrdom**.
Segment 1: Illuminations Before Embarking

Sheikh al-Ghizzi introduces the "File of Fatima" series, setting three foundational premises: first, **his intent is not to provoke** but to present buried truths from both Shia and Sunni historical texts; second, he highlights the paradox of the Muslim Ummah abandoning the Prophet's only surviving child despite the Quranic command to love his near kin; and third, he asserts that **true Shiism is defined solely by the Infallible Ahlulbayt**, not by any fallible scholar or cleric.

Peace be upon Fatima, the Chosen (al-Mustafa), and Al-Murtada her husband, and the Infallibles from her children. Peace be upon the Fatimids in every time and place, her offspring, her Shia, and her lovers, and the mercy of Allah and His blessings.

Points I loved to point out before I take up the papers of this file. The first point: It was my desire that the episodes of this program be long, so that I could research in them every small and big detail relating to this topic from near or far. But it occurred to my mind that this program should be translated into the most important languages spoken by the majority of the Shia of the Ahlulbayt in the world... I will try to be brief, but a brevity that does not compromise and does not subtract from the important demands around which the papers of this file revolve.

As for the second point: The speech in these episodes is not a provocation to anyone, nor a love for incitement or for noise. **It is a clarification of truths**. These truths are present in the books of the Shia of Fatima, and in the books of others! **The Fatimi File: papers from the bellies of the books of hadith and the books of history.** The whip of the oppressors has fallen upon it, or the forgery of the pens of historians and hadith narrators from the worshippers of dirhams and from those who eat the crumbs of the tables of sultans.

The atrocity of the crime... and it was registered against an unknown perpetrator! Thus history judged the oppression of Fatima: either denial, or casting doubt, or forgery, or distortion. So that the Fatimi File would be absent in the world of oblivion, and placed in the archive of heedlessness, where the dust of disloyalty to Muhammad, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, accumulates upon it.
When the Seal of the Prophets, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, used to stand in his noble mosque while preaching among his companions and saying:
May Allah curse whoever deprives the hired worker of his wage... And I am your hired worker! 'Say, I do not ask of you for it any reward except love for the near of kin.'

And I ask myself before I ask others: What person in this world is the closest to the Messenger of Allah? Is there anyone closer in kinship and stronger in blood-tie and connection than Fatima? Fatima is the daughter of Muhammad. And Muhammad departed from the world and he had no one in this world—neither a father, nor a mother, nor a child—except Fatima! The speech here is about Fatima. About the oppression of Fatima, about the tragedy of the daughter of Muhammad.

The third point: It is my conviction that I clarify, it is not a proof against anyone, and I do not want to impose my opinion on anyone, nor do I represent any entity among the entities. **Shiism (al-Tashayyu') represents nothing except the Ahlulbayt.** A perfect religion cannot be represented by the deficient (al-Naqis), no matter what he reaches of virtue, righteousness, knowledge, and jurisprudence. The authoritative proof of the people of knowledge is only an incidental proof and a relative proof, to the extent of their closeness to the Ahlulbayt. As it came in the noble signed decree (Tawqi'):
And as for the occurring events, return in them to the narrators of our hadith, for they are my proof over you, and I am the proof of Allah over them.

There is no one on earth who represents Shiism! And if there are found among the scholars, the religious authorities, the jurists, and the hadith scholars, then they represent that matter **a relative representation**. The complete, absolute authoritative proof is for Muhammad and the family of Muhammad.
Segment 2: Partners in the Crime - The Quranic Principle of Complicity

Sheikh al-Ghizzi answers the pivotal question: "Why open the File of Fatima?" He derives the answer directly from the Quran. **Citing verses from Surah Al-Baqarah and Surah Al Imran**, he demonstrates how Allah held the Jews of Medina responsible for killing the prophets, even though those murders occurred 500 years before they were born. Imam al-Sadiq explains that because the Jews of Medina loved and approved of their ancestors' actions, Allah deemed them direct accomplices. The Sheikh applies this Quranic principle to the Ummah: **anyone who defends, loves, or approves of the killers of the Ahlulbayt is an active partner in their murder**.

I do not want to wander far in these points. And I ask a question: Why do I open this file? Why the speech in the lines of the pages of this file? The answer comes from the orchards of the Book and the Progeny. In the Noble Book, here we go to Surah Al-Baqarah. And in the 91st verse:
Say, 'Then why did you kill the prophets of Allah before, if you are [indeed] believers?'

Did the Jews of Medina kill any prophet from the prophets? What do our Imams, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon them all, say? And this is the first volume of **Tafsir al-Burhan** by Sayyid Hashim al-Bahrani. The narration is from our **Imam al-Sadiq**, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him. The Imam says:

This section of the Quran was only revealed regarding a group of Jews who were in the time of Muhammad. They did not kill the prophets of Allah with their hands, nor were they in their time. Rather, their early ancestors who were before them killed them... So Allah made them of them, and added to them the action of their early ancestors because they followed them and took them as masters.

And we do not go far, in Surah Al Imran. In the 183rd verse: "Say, 'There have already come to you messengers before me with clear proofs... So why did you kill them, if you should be truthful?'" The address is also to the Jews who were in the time of Muhammad.

The narration in the noble al-Kafi. And this is the second volume of the volumes of the noble al-Kafi. The narration is from our Imam al-Sadiq, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him. The Imam says:

May Allah curse the Qadariyyah. May Allah curse the Kharijites. May Allah curse the Murji'ah. May Allah curse the Murji'ah... Indeed these say that our killers are believers! So our blood is smeared on their clothes until the Day of Resurrection!

What do these Murji'ah say? That the killers of the Ahlulbayt, Yazid ibn Muawiyah, is a believer, and his caliphate is legitimate! So we leave the talk about Fatima! Which clothes are smeared with the blood of Fatima and the family of Fatima? "That our killers are believers," these Murji'ah say thus, "So our blood is smeared on their clothes until the Day of Resurrection."
Then after that, the Imam points to the verse. The Imam says:
There was between the killers and the addressed five hundred years, so Allah bound them to the killing because of their being pleased with what they did!

The exact same thing, the Murji'ah say "our killers are believers." The killers of the Ahlulbayt are believers. The blood is there; the blood of the Ahlulbayt is smeared on their clothes until the Day of Resurrection. And that is not strange. The narrations state that the murdered one comes on the Day of Resurrection having been killed by one man, but the killers are gathered in dozens. Why? Because anyone who heard of the killing and rejoiced in it, he is a partner in his killing!
Segment 3: The Principle of Complicity and the Manifesto of the Mahdi

Sheikh al-Ghizzi deepens the principle of complicity by quoting Imam al-Baqir from Sheikh al-Saduq's 'Iqab al-A'mal. The Imam states that anyone who remains willfully ignorant of the Ahlulbayt's oppression is a partner to their oppressors. The Sheikh then recites the heartbreaking manifesto of Imam al-Mahdi, delivered by Al-Nafs al-Zakiyyah before his reappearance, which cries out for help against the continuous oppression of the Ahlulbayt since the Prophet's death.

A clear and explicit narration narrated by our Sheikh al-Saduq in his book **'Iqab al-A'mal**. The narration is from Jabir al-Ju'fi, from our **Imam al-Baqir**, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him. What does our Imam al-Baqir say?

Whoever does not know the evil of what was brought upon us of our oppression, the taking away of our right, and what we were afflicted with, then he is a partner to whoever brought it upon us in what we were subjected to!

**This is an issue of extreme importance**! This is the speech of the Infallible Imam. **The statement here is about a nation that claims that it is the nation of Muhammad, a nation that heard of the Ahlulbayt. Meaning the one who does not know the extent of the oppression that befell the Ahlulbayt—while it is possible for him to know—he is a partner to the one who oppressed the Ahlulbayt**! This word requires the believing human to pause and deeply to reflect upon it for a long time.

Our Imam of the Time, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him. This is the 52nd volume of Bihar al-Anwar. And these are lines from the manifesto that our Imam of the Time sends with the Pure Soul (al-Nafs al-Zakiyyah). The messenger of the Proof says to the people of Mecca:

We are the Household of Mercy, and the mine of the Message and the Caliphate. And we are the progeny of Muhammad and the lineage of the prophets. And indeed we have been oppressed, and persecuted, and conquered, and our right was extorted from us since our Prophet was taken until this day of ours! So we seek your help, so help us!

This is the manifesto of the Imam of our time! And the broadest title, the greatest title in this oppression is the oppression of the mother of al-Hassan and al-Hussein: the Fatimi Oppression. This is a brief answer to a question I posed: why do we open the Fatimi File? The answer is in these luminous words.

As for how we deal with the papers of the Fatimi file. I do not want to research here upon the method of the historians or the hadith scholars, so I get lost in this game! In the game of sources and chains of narration which was fabricated by those who fabricated it! We are searching for this crime just as investigators search for crimes! We search for contexts (Qara'in). And from the known rules recognized in criminology: there is no such thing as a perfect crime. And from the rules also: that the criminal circles the location of his crime.
Segment 4: The Game of Chains of Narration and the Deception of al-Bukhari

Sheikh al-Ghizzi demonstrates the **"game of chains of narration"** by comparing the exact same hadith in Sahih Muslim and Sahih al-Bukhari. In Sahih Muslim, Umar explicitly admits that Imam Ali and al-Abbas viewed Abu Bakr and Umar as "liars, sinful, treacherous, and dishonest." **However, Sahih al-Bukhari completely censors these specific words while keeping the rest of the hadith and the exact same chain of transmission**.

This is Sahih al-Bukhari. And this is Sahih Muslim, and they are the most trusted books among the people [the Sunnis]. The narration in Sahih Muslim talks about a dialogue between al-Abbas the uncle of the Prophet, and the Master of Legatees [Ali], and Umar ibn al-Khattab. Umar says, addressing al-Abbas and Ali:

So Abu Bakr said: The Messenger of Allah said: We do not leave inheritance; what we leave is charity. So you both viewed him [Abu Bakr] as a liar, sinful, treacherous, and dishonest! And Allah knows that he is truthful, pious, rightly-guided, and a follower of the truth.
Then Umar continues:
Then Abu Bakr died, and I became the successor of the Messenger of Allah and the successor of Abu Bakr. So you both viewed me as a liar, sinful, treacherous, and dishonest!

This narration is an authentic narration in Sahih Muslim, and the one who transmits it is Umar. So Ali viewed Abu Bakr according to this narration as a liar, sinful, treacherous, and dishonest! And he viewed Umar likewise.

If you went to Sahih al-Bukhari... Al-Bukhari transmitted the exact same hadith, from the exact same narrator, from Malik ibn Aws, with the exact same chain of narration, with the exact same text, **except that he removed from the hadith these words which I pointed to!** Which are: the opinion of Ali and al-Abbas regarding Abu Bakr and Umar!

This is the "most authentic" of hadith books! Is this not deception (Tadlis)? Hiding of facts! So what is the use of the issue of chains of narration, and what is the use of the issue of sources if the issue is like this?! Then this is a game! It is said about al-Bukhari that he had 600,000 hadiths! If he had 600,000 hadiths and he only chose 2761, then how many truths disappeared in those hadiths?!

So if it was not a deception from al-Bukhari, and we said that the narrations reached al-Bukhari in this form, while they are present in other sources in another form, what does this mean? It means that relying on chains of narration alone, even with good intention, does not lead us to the truths! It leads us to deceived reports.
Segment 5: Ibn Qutaybah and the Confrontation at Fatima's Door

Sheikh al-Ghizzi turns to the renowned Sunni historian Ibn Qutaybah al-Dinawari's book, Al-Imamah wa al-Siyasah. He reads a harrowing account of Umar's mob arriving at Fatima's door, her agonizing cry to the Prophet, and the explicit threat to behead Imam Ali. The Sheikh highlights the profound theological implications of Fatima refusing to return the obligatory Islamic greeting (Salam) to Abu Bakr and Umar.

And therefore, when the talk about the oppression of Fatima comes in the books of the people, they hasten to deny it! For example: this book is Al-Imamah wa al-Siyasah by Ibn Qutaybah al-Dinawari. And he is from the scholars of the people, and from their hadith narrators, and from their historians. This book is explicit and clear regarding the oppression of Fatima. Page 22, page 23:

Then Umar stood up, and a group walked with him until they came to the door of Fatima. So they knocked on the door. When she heard their voices, she called out at the top of her voice: 'O Father! O Messenger of Allah! What have we met after you from Ibn al-Khattab and Ibn Abi Quhafah (Abu Bakr)!'
He continues:

But Umar remained, and with him a group. So they brought Ali out and took him to Abu Bakr. They said to him: 'Pledge allegiance.' He said: 'If I do not do it, then what will you do?' They said: 'Then, by Allah besides whom there is no god, we will strike your neck!'
Ibn Qutaybah also narrates:

So Umar said to Abu Bakr: 'Let us go to Fatima, for we have angered her.' So they both set out together, and they sought permission to enter upon Fatima, but she did not give them permission. So they came to Ali and spoke to him, so he entered them upon her. When they sat near her, she turned her face to the wall. They greeted her with Salam, but she did not return the Salam to them.

Returning the Salam to a Muslim is obligatory! And Fatima is infallible. Why did she not greet them? Because returning the Salam is obligatory upon the Muslim, even if he is in disagreement with him. **She did not return the Salam to them**. Then Ibn Qutaybah writes:

She said: 'I implore you by Allah, did you not hear the Messenger of Allah saying: The pleasure of Fatima is from my pleasure, and the anger of Fatima is from my anger...?' They said: 'Yes, we heard it from the Messenger of Allah.' She said: 'Then I make Allah and His angels witness that you both have angered me and did not please me! And if I meet the Prophet, I will surely complain to him about you both!'

This is the speech of Ibn Qutaybah. They denied it, and we do not care about their denial as I clarified a short while ago. This is a game: the game of researching in sources and chains of narration. **This is a source from their sources, clear and explicit**.
Segment 6: The Explicit Prophecy of the Murder of Fatima

Sheikh al-Ghizzi presents another profound piece of forensic evidence from the Sunni scholar al-Juwayni in his book Fara'id al-Simtayn. The Sheikh reads a devastating hadith narrated by Ibn Abbas, where the Prophet prophesies the exact calamities that will befall Fatima, and explicitly curses her oppressors and the one who strikes her.

The other source-they said that this book was falsely attributed to Ibn Qutaybah al-Dinawari. As for this second book, they said its author is a Shiite, that he had become a Rafidhi! This is Fara'id al-Simtayn by the hadith scholar al-Hamawini or al-Juwayni al-Khurasani.
The narration with his chain of transmission, he narrates it from Sa'id ibn Jubayr from Ibn Abbas. The Prophet began to speak about the oppression of his Ahlulbayt, until he said:

It is as if I am with her, and humiliation has entered her house, her sanctity has been violated, her right has been usurped, her inheritance has been denied, her side has been broken, and her fetus has been miscarried! And she is calling out: 'O Muhammadah!' but she is not answered... And she will be the first to join me from my Ahlulbayt. So she will come to me sorrowful, distressed, oppressed, usurped, and murdered (Maqtulah)!

A clear, explicit statement! The book is from the books of the people (the Sunnis). The hadith is not from the Shia, it is from Sa'id ibn Jubayr from Ibn Abbas. The Messenger of Allah says, at that moment what does he say?
O Allah, curse whoever oppressed her! And punish whoever usurped from her, and humiliate whoever humiliated her! And abide forever in Your Fire whoever struck her side until she miscarried her child!
And Sahih al-Bukhari is not far from these atmospheres. In Sahih al-Bukhari, page 1190, Hadith 6725 and 6726:

So Abu Bakr said to them: I heard the Messenger of Allah... saying: We do not leave inheritance; what we leave is charity... He said: So Fatima abandoned him, and she did not speak to him until she died!

We do not expect from al-Bukhari—who deleted those words and deleted many words—to bring us the full story as al-Juwayni mentioned it. We search for contexts here and there. These contexts, when gathered, the picture will appear clear and it will manifest evident and plain.
Segment 7: The Suppressed Document - Umar's Letter to Muawiyah

Sheikh al-Ghizzi unveils a chilling and historically suppressed document found in the 30th volume of Bihar al-Anwar. The document is a **long letter written by Umar ibn al-Khattab to Muawiyah ibn Abi Sufyan**, extracted from the lost second volume of Dala'il al-Imamah. In this letter, **Umar explicitly confesses to the horrifying details of the attack on Fatima's house**.

**There is a document present, but no one mentions it**. This is the thirtieth volume of Bihar al-Anwar. From the volumes that were prevented from being printed! And the one who prevented its printing was one of our Maraji' (religious authorities). Everyone knows that Bihar al-Anwar was printed without the volumes from the twenty-ninth volume to the thirty-fourth volume.

This is a very important document, page 210, Hadith number 151. Sheikh al-Majlisi says: "Some of the virtuous scholars in Mecca... informed me that he extracted it from the second volume of the book **Dala'il al-Imamah**." Dala'il al-Imamah by the hadith scholar al-Tabari the Imami (Shia). This letter is a detailed letter from Umar ibn al-Khattab to Muawiyah ibn Abi Sufyan, telling him about the events that occurred. Among what he says, he says:

...and I took the whip and I struck! And I said to Khalid ibn al-Walid: 'You and our men, come forward in gathering the firewood!' So I said: 'I will surely set it on fire'—meaning set fire to the house. So she said—meaning Fatima—'O enemy of Allah, and enemy of His Messenger, and enemy of the Commander of the Believers!' And Fatima struck her hands on the door to prevent me from opening it. So I pushed it while it was difficult for her. So I struck her hands with the whip, and it pained her.
He continues his confession to Muawiyah:

But I remembered the grudges of Ali... So I kicked the door, while she had stuck her insides to the door to barricade it! I heard her... and she let out a cry that I thought had turned the top of Medina to its bottom! And she said: 'O Father! O Messenger of Allah! Thus is it done to your beloved and your daughter! Ah! Take me to you, for by Allah, the one in my insides has been killed, O Muhammad!'
And the final harrowing detail:

And I heard her in labor pains while she was leaning against the wall. So I pushed the door and entered. She came at me with a face whose sight I feared, so I slapped her a slap on her cheeks from outside the veil (Khimar), and her earring broke and scattered to the ground!

This text is part of a long, wide letter written by Umar ibn al-Khattab to Muawiyah, according to this narration which our Sheikh al-Majlisi narrated in Bihar al-Anwar. **This is among the texts that were hidden! Among the texts that are not mentioned, not on the pulpits, nor are they mentioned on satellite channels, nor are they mentioned in public gatherings**!
Segment 8: The Conclusive Proof from Kamil al-Ziyarat - Death by Beating

Sheikh al-Ghizzi concludes the first episode by addressing critics who accused him of fabricating the claim that Imam al-Sadiq stated Fatima died specifically from being beaten. **To silence them, he reads an undeniable, explicit hadith from the foundational Shia text Kamil al-Ziyarat**. The text explicitly states that Fatima will be beaten while pregnant, her home will be invaded, she will miscarry due to the strikes, and she will definitively "die from that beating."

As for Kamil al-Ziyarat... I spoke about how in the reports from our Imam al-Sadiq, peace be upon him, it states that Fatima, prayers of Allah be upon her, was beaten and died from the beating. And it reached me that some individuals denied this matter! And they said that I am the one who fabricated it, and that no such thing exists!

This is Kamil al-Ziyarat. And the narration here, the narration is narrated by our Imam al-Sadiq from the Messenger of Allah, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family. The narration from Hammad ibn Uthman, from Abu Abdullah (Imam al-Sadiq), peace be upon him:

When the Prophet, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, was taken on the Night Journey (Mi'raj) to the heaven, it was said to him: 'Indeed, Allah the Blessed and Exalted will test you in three things...' And as for the third: 'What your Ahlulbayt will face after you of killing.'
The explicit proof follows:

And as for your daughter, she will be oppressed, deprived, her right which you designated for her will be usurped by force, and she will be beaten while she is pregnant! And her sanctuary and her home will be entered upon her without permission! Then humiliation and abasement will touch her, and she will find no protector. And she will miscarry what is in her womb from the beating! And she will die from that beating!

What is the difference between this narration and the narration of the opponents in Fara'id al-Simtayn which I read a short while ago: "So she will come to me sorrowful, distressed, oppressed, usurped, and murdered"? This narration [in **Kamil al-Ziyarat**] is from Hammad ibn Uthman from our **Imam al-Sadiq**, peace be upon him, from the Messenger of Allah. **Fatima died from the beating! Fatima was murdered, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon her.**

So Fatima dies from that beating. And the question here: **Then how severe was that beating because of which she died**?! How was Fatima beaten? And where was Fatima beaten?! These details will come to us, God willing the Exalted, in the next episode.

title: The File of Fatima - Episode 2: The Game of Chains of Narration and the Deception of the Sahihs
speaker: Sheikh Abdul-Halim al-Ghizzi

content.text: Overview: Episode 2

In the second episode of the File of Fatima, Sheikh al-Ghizzi dismantles the Sunni methodological reliance on the **game of sources and chains of narration** (Asanid). While acknowledging the rational necessity of seeking trustworthy narrators—as commanded by the Ahlulbayt—he exposes how this system has been weaponized as an **illusion to bury historical truths**. Through a meticulous forensic investigation, the Sheikh highlights severe contradictions and deliberate censorship within the "most authentic" Sunni books, primarily Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim. He brings forward shocking examples of the **Caliphs' ignorance of foundational Quranic vocabulary** and basic Islamic jurisprudence, revealing how al-Bukhari intentionally amputated hadiths to protect their image. The episode culminates in the tragic realization that the original **prayer of Muhammad was completely lost** among the Companions, underscoring the absolute necessity of uncovering the suppressed tragedy of Fatima's murder through **circumstantial evidence** rather than manipulated chains.
Segment 1: Validating the Source - The Rational Need for Trustworthy Narrators

Sheikh al-Ghizzi begins the second episode of "The File of Fatima" by outlining the upcoming schedule and themes, including a deep dive into the Book of Sulaym ibn Qays. He revisits his previous assertion that modern historical and hadith methodologies—which he calls the "game of sources and chains of narration"—are used as toys to bury truths. However, he clarifies that this is not a dismissal of the fundamental human and rational need for reliable sources. He cites numerous Shia narrations from **Al-Kafi** and **Rijal al-Kashshi** where the Imams specifically commanded their followers to only accept knowledge from "trustworthy and reliable" (Thiqah Ma'mun) individuals. He concludes that while the Ahlulbayt established a high standard for truth-seeking, this methodology has been practically weaponized by opponents to suppress the tragedy of Fatima.

Peace be upon Fatima, the Chosen (al-Mustafa), and Al-Murtada after her, and the Infallibles from her children and her sons. Peace be upon the Fatimids in every time and place, from her offspring, her Shia, and her lovers, and the mercy of Allah and His blessings.
The Fatimi File, Episode Two. Our episodes in this file will follow one after another today, and likewise tomorrow.

The second episode: I will try to clarify an important matter in this episode. Tomorrow, the speech will be about the **most important document regarding the oppression of the daughter of the Messenger of Allah, and it is the Book of Sulaym ibn Qays.** The Book of Sulaym ibn Qays is among the most important documents and sources that talk about the history of the **Great Sedition (al-Fitnah al-Kubra)** that occurred after the departure of the Messenger of Allah, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family. So tomorrow, the talk is about Sulaym ibn Qays and the texts mentioned in this noble book regarding the oppression of the mother of al-Hassan and al-Hussein.

As for the talk of today, in this second episode, it is a clarification and a confirmation of what passed in the first episode, so that I complete the speech regarding the demands whose mention preceded and do not return to them a second time in the coming episodes.

When I said that relying on sources and chains of narration is a "game" and that I will not rely on them in the Fatimi File, this speech is **not a negation of the importance of sources and chains of narration, ever!** Confirming the authenticity of sources, the strength of sources, and the trustworthiness of chains of narration is a **primordial, human, and rational issue** before it enters into the discussions of science or legal matters. But there is a difference between the issue being correct, logical, and rational, and the practical reality. When I said that sources and chains of narration are this "game," it is according to the existing practical reality.

The first illumination: I take texts from the hadith of the Ahlulbayt, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon them all. A narration from the noble **Al-Kafi** by our Sheikh al-Kulayni, may Allah the Exalted's pleasure be upon him. Ahmad ibn Ishaq asks the Imam: "With whom should I deal, from whom should I take [knowledge], and whose word should I accept?" So he said:

Al-Umari is my trustworthy one (Thiqati); whatever he delivers to you from me, then from me he delivers. And whatever he says to you from me, then from me he says. So listen to him and obey, for he is the Trustworthy, the Reliable (al-Thiqah al-Ma'mun).
The noble signed decree (Tawqi') issued from the Sacred Precinct (al-Nahiyah al-Muqaddasah), narrated in **Rijal al-Kashshi** from al-Qasim ibn al-'Ala:
For there is no excuse for anyone of our allies (Mawali) in doubting what our trustworthy ones narrate from us, for they have known that we consult them with our secret and we charge them with it.

Another narration also narrated in Rijal al-Kashshi. From Ali ibn al-Musayyib, he said: I said to al-Rida, peace be upon him: "My apartment is far... from whom should I take the features of my religion?" He said:
From Zakariya ibn Adam al-Qummi. The Reliable (al-Ma'mun) over the religion and the world.
And I conclude the speech with the known noble decree issued from the Sacred Precinct, known as the Decree of Ishaq ibn Yaqub:
And as for the occurring events, return in them to the narrators of our hadith, for they are my proof over you and I am the proof of Allah.

And we have in the books of hadith from the Household of Infallibility very, very many texts in this meaning. When I said that the issue of chains of narration and sources is a "game," it is not intended by the judgment of the intellect and logic... **The methodology the Ahlulbayt want is that we investigate the truth, we investigate truthfulness and trustworthiness, and we investigate the sources.**
Segment 2: The Quranic Methodology - Investigating the "Untrustworthy" and the Deception of Appearances

Sheikh al-Ghizzi balances his previous point: while the intellect and the Ahlulbayt command us to rely on trustworthy sources, this does not mean we categorically reject all information from untrustworthy ones. He cites the Quranic verse of Tabayyun (investigation) regarding the Fasiq (al-Walid ibn Uqba) to show that the divine command is to investigate circumstantial evidence, not to blindly dismiss the news. Furthermore, using a verse from Surah Al-Baqarah about false witnesses deceiving judges, he demonstrates that "apparent trustworthiness" in the science of Rijal (narrator evaluation) is often misleading. Therefore, the true methodology—especially when investigating the murder of Fatima—is to act as a forensic investigator gathering circumstantial evidence (Qara'in) rather than blindly trusting the superficial labels of chains of narration.

But that does not mean that the person who is described with a lack of trustworthiness, we reject his news entirely! And even the person who is described with trustworthiness, we do not accept the news in everything he says, unless he is authenticated... The reliable and trustworthy one, if he informs us, it does not mean that we suffice merely with his trustworthiness. This might be sufficient sometimes. But at other times we need contexts (Qara'in).
The Quranic methodology and the Infallible methodology are one methodology. When we go to the Noble Book, we go to Surah Al-Hujurat, to the sixth verse:
O you who have believed, if there comes to you a disobedient one (Fasiq) with information, investigate (Fatabayyanu).

Meaning, do not accept his news, and likewise do not reject his news; investigate and search for contexts, make sure. He might be truthful, and he might be a liar. The verse is regarding the story of al-Walid ibn Uqba... and al-Walid ibn Uqba is a true Fasiq, history testifies to that and practical reality testifies to that.
So we go to Surah Al-Baqarah. And we read this verse, which is the 188th verse. The address is to those who have believed:

And do not consume one another's wealth unjustly or send it [in bribery] to the rulers in order that [they might aid] you [to] consume a portion of the wealth of the people in sin, while you know [it is unlawful].

There are several facets to the verse... The fourth facet, and it is what I want here. Which is that the claimant brings witnesses... these witnesses, according to their apparent state, according to their social standing, according to their religious status among the people, are such that the judge—based on the available information he has—accepts their testimonies! And the judge has means to investigate. The judge's means to investigate might be better than the means of investigation of the scholars of Rijal (narrator evaluation)! Because the scholar of Rijal sits in his house and gathers information from books.

And despite that, the judge investigates, and he reaches the knowledge that these witnesses are just ('Udul), their descriptions are accepted. **But in reality, they are not just; they are bearing false witness!** And the wealth is transferred to whom? To this false claimant.

So therefore, the issue that a person is brought and it is said "this one is trustworthy"—he might be trustworthy according to reality, and he might be trustworthy according to the apparent state of affairs! ... Does anyone claim that the scholars of Rijal who write the descriptions of men in their books know the inner secrets of people? No one says that. They rely on the appearances of matters, and the appearances of matters do not reveal the absolute realities.

As I said, I, in this file, search—just as investigators search in crimes—I search for contexts (Qara'in). The search here is not in a jurisprudential issue or in a theological issue. **The search here is regarding a crime! The search here is regarding the incident of killing; Fatima was murdered!** We search for contexts, for witnesses, and for every small and big thing that leads us to the truth of this matter, so that we do not fall into the exact same thing that those who are far from the Ahlulbayt fell into. Those who, in their best states, registered the crime against an unknown perpetrator! And closed the file, and buried it among many files on the shelves of oblivion.
Segment 3: The Game of Chains of Narration and the Deception in the Sahihs

Sheikh al-Ghizzi proves why relying strictly on Sunni chains of narration is a "game" by demonstrating deliberate censorship in their most authentic books. First, he recalls Ibn Qutaybah's record of Fatima's intense anger and her vow to curse Abu Bakr in every prayer. Then, he drops a bombshell from Sahih Muslim, where Umar explicitly confesses that Imam Ali viewed both Abu Bakr and Umar as "liars, sinful, treacherous, and dishonest." Finally, he shows how Sahih al-Bukhari transmits the exact same hadith but intentionally deletes these damning descriptions to protect the Caliphs, proving that "authentic" sources were systematically sanitized to bury the truth.

When I spoke about the game of sources and chains of narration... because the people who deny this oppression, when we argue against them and say that it was mentioned in your books—and I mentioned models of that: Al-Imamah wa al-Siyasah, Fara'id al-Simtayn, and I mentioned models from al-Bukhari which speak about Fatima abandoning Abu Bakr until she died, and not speaking to him.
When we read for example in Al-Imamah wa al-Siyasah... when Abu Bakr and Umar entered upon Fatima, she said:
Then I make Allah and His angels witness that you both have angered me and did not please me! And if I meet the Prophet, I will surely complain to him about you both!

So Abu Bakr said, according to Al-Imamah wa al-Siyasah by Ibn Qutaybah: "I seek refuge in Allah the Exalted from His anger and your anger, O Fatima." Then Abu Bakr wept heavily until his soul almost departed. While Fatima was saying—addressing Abu Bakr:
By Allah, I will surely invoke Allah against you in every prayer I pray!

What grievance was in the heart of Fatima when she swears this...? The Fatima who does not pray for herself says: "By Allah, I will surely invoke Allah against you in every prayer I pray!" If Fatima, whose pleasure is from the pleasure of Allah, and whose anger is from the anger of Allah, is praying against him in every prayer she prays! And she died murdered, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon her.

When the speech is like this, the talk comes that this source is not authentic, and this book is falsely attributed to Ibn Qutaybah... Therefore, I cut the path from the first time and went in a straight line. I said: if this is the basis in the research... I went to the two most important sources they have: **Sahih al-Bukhari** and **Sahih Muslim**. If the speech is about sources, then these are the most authentic sources. And if the speech is about chains of narration, then these are the most authentic chains of narration.

But what does reality say? I read to you in Sahih Muslim... The narration talks about a dialogue between al-Abbas the uncle of the Prophet, and the Master of Legatees [Ali], and Umar ibn al-Khattab. Umar says, addressing al-Abbas and Ali:

So you both viewed him [Abu Bakr] as a liar, sinful, treacherous, and dishonest! ... Then Abu Bakr died, and I became the successor of the Messenger of Allah and the successor of Abu Bakr. So you both viewed me as a liar, sinful, treacherous, and dishonest!

This is the opinion of Ali regarding Abu Bakr! And Ali believes regarding Umar the same: a liar, sinful, treacherous, and dishonest! This narration is in Sahih Muslim. This is an authentic source and the chain is authentic! And who is the one speaking? Umar ibn al-Khattab.

I am not now in the process of explaining this narration, but when I go to Sahih al-Bukhari... The exact same hadith found in Sahih Muslim... **These descriptions which came in Sahih Muslim—that Ali believed Abu Bakr was a liar, sinful, treacherous, and dishonest, and he believed the exact same about Umar—this speech was deleted from the exact same narration, which has the exact same chain of narration, in Sahih al-Bukhari!**

I mentioned this narration as an example of the distortion and deception (Tadlis) by which the theory of sources and chains of narration falls! If these are the most authentic sources, and these are the most authentic chains of narration, then what is their use in delivering the truth?!

And the issue does not stop at this example... Ubaydullah (the narrator) said: "So I informed Abdullah ibn Abbas. He said: 'Do you know who the other man was?' I said: 'No.' He said: 'It is Ali!'" If Aisha, the wife of the Prophet, does not transmit the truth completely, then what do you think of the other Companions?
Segment 4: Ignorance of the Quran and the Censorship of "Abba"

Sheikh al-Ghizzi provides another glaring example of al-Bukhari's deliberate censorship to protect the image of the Caliphs. He recounts the well-documented historical incident where Umar ibn al-Khattab was reciting Surah 'Abasa and did not know the meaning of the common Arabic word "Abba" (pasture/fodder). Instead of seeking knowledge, Umar dismissed the attempt to understand the Quran as "over-burdening" (Takalluf). While major Sunni sources like Tafsir al-Tabari narrate the full, embarrassing story, al-Bukhari completely amputates the context, leaving only a meaningless fragment: "We have been forbidden from over-burdening." The Sheikh points out that even Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani, in his commentary Fath al-Bari, was forced to bring the full story from other sources just to explain what Bukhari's truncated hadith actually meant, fully exposing the cover-up.

This program is not a research or a study in Sahih al-Bukhari or in Sahih Muslim. But to clarify the issue, I will bring another model, another example. The Noble Quran. Let me go to Surah 'Abasa wa Tawalla:
And fruit and pasture (Wa Fakihatan wa Abba). As an enjoyment for you and your grazing livestock.

I believe any human who knows a simple level of Arabic reads the verses and finds... the fruit is for the human, and the Abba (pasture/fodder) is for the animal. The verses are clear, the verses are evident.

Let us see, for example, this is **Tafsir al-Tabari**. And it is from the oldest exegeses of the people. When he comes to this verse "And fruit and pasture," he transmits this narration from Anas ibn Malik:

Umar recited 'Abasa wa Tawalla' until he came to this verse, 'And fruit and pasture (Abba).' He said: 'We already know the fruit, so what is the Abba?' Then he said: 'Indeed, this is over-burdening (Takalluf)!'

Now it is clear from the verse that comes after it: "And fruit and pasture. As an enjoyment for you and your grazing livestock." And this is clear Arabic. Furthermore, what did the Arabs own in their lives? A camel, a tent, sheep, food they ate, and fodder for their animals! **If the Caliph does not even know the simplest of these present vocabulary words!**

A second narration: "He said: We already know the fruit, so what is the Abba? Then he said: By your life, O son of al-Khattab, indeed this is over-burdening!" And what is the over-burdening? Meaning, to know the meanings of the words used among the people and the daily vocabulary is "over-burdening"! **If the level of knowledge he possesses is at this horizon, then what about the very deep truths in the Book of Allah?!**

Let us go to al-Bukhari! How did he transmit the hadith? To see how it is the "most authentic of sources" and how they are the "most authentic of chains"! Al-Bukhari, how did he transmit the hadith? From Anas he said:
We were with Umar, and he said: We have been forbidden from over-burdening (Takalluf)!

**He amputated the story from its root!** He did not mention, as came in the original hadith, that Umar recited 'Abasa wa Tawalla until he reached His saying "And fruit and pasture (Abba)"... He cut all of this speech! And he only brought the final word: "We have been forbidden from over-burdening!"
Therefore, what did the author of Fath al-Bari do? What was he forced to do? And this is Fath al-Bari explaining the hadiths of Sahih al-Bukhari... What does Ibn Hajar say? He says:
And this is more appropriate to complete the hadith which al-Bukhari extracted!

Meaning he knows that the hadith of al-Bukhari is amputated! And he is now explaining... So is al-Bukhari ignorant of the hadiths? They say that he is the "Commander of the Believers in Hadith"! ... What is this called? Amputating hadiths! Deleting hadiths! **And the truth that the investigator reaches is that the hadiths which are amputated are always the hadiths in which there is a praise for Ali and the Ahlulbayt, and the hadiths in which there is a detraction of Abu Bakr and Umar are amputated!**
Segment 5: Ignorance of Tayammum and the Deletion of the Fatwa

Sheikh al-Ghizzi provides another stark example of Sunni hadith manipulation by comparing accounts of Umar's fatwa on Tayammum (dry ablution). Sahih Muslim and numerous other authentic texts record that when a man asked Umar what to do if he lacked water while ritually impure, Umar explicitly commanded him, "Do not pray!" Ammar ibn Yasir had to publicly correct the Caliph using the Prophet's instruction. However, Sahih al-Bukhari deliberately deletes Umar's un-Islamic fatwa from the text. The Sheikh also rebukes Ibn Hajar's absurd attempt to justify Umar's ignorance of explicit Quranic verses by rebranding his mistake as a valid "Ijtihad" (independent reasoning) in the presence of the Prophet.
Let us bring another example... This is Sahih Muslim. Page 141, Chapter of Tayammum, Hadith 832.
A man came to Umar and said: 'I became in a state of major ritual impurity (Junub) and I did not find water.' So he (Umar) said: 'Do not pray!'
The fatwa was issued clear and explicit; he did not tell him to perform Tayammum. He said: "Do not pray."

So Ammar—Ammar ibn Yasir was sitting there—said: "Do you not remember, O Commander of the Believers, when you and I were in a military detachment and we became Junub and did not find water? As for you, you did not pray. As for me, I rolled in the dirt and prayed. So the Prophet said: It would have been sufficient for you to strike your hands on the earth..."

Naturally, this speech is clear, it reveals the Caliph's ignorance of the religious rulings. But the strange thing... this is the first volume of Fath al-Bari by Ibn Hajar. When he reaches this narration, what does Ibn Hajar say? He says: "And this is a famous school of thought of Umar!" Meaning if a person becomes Junub, he does not pray!
The strange thing in that is the Quranic verses are clear and explicit! ... And in Surah al-Ma'idah, verse 6:
...and find no water, then seek clean earth and wipe over your faces and hands with it.
Then he (Ibn Hajar) comments with a strange commentary! He says:

And it is derived from this hadith the occurrence of the Ijtihad (independent reasoning) of the Companions during the time of the Prophet, and that the Mujtahid has no blame upon him if he exerts his effort!
And when did Umar exert his effort?! ... **First, if the Prophet is present, there is no meaning to Ijtihad! And there is no Ijtihad against the text! And there is no Ijtihad against the Quran!**

Let us go to al-Bukhari to see how he transmitted the report! Al-Bukhari transmitted the report with the exact same chain... Of course, he cut the speech! The speech of Umar who said to the man "Do not pray"! **He deleted the speech of Umar from the text of the narration when he said to the man: "Do not pray."**

The exact same hadith that Muslim transmitted. But when al-Bukhari transmitted it, he distorted it! He deleted the speech of Umar from the text of the narration... And you notice the extent of ignorance of the Quran, the extent of ignorance of the religious rulings. And this does not concern us now, this distortion upon distortion, and across history distortion multiplies.
Segment 6: The Corrupted Prayer of Abu Hanifa and the Conversion of the Sultan

To further demonstrate how deeply distortion and bizarre rulings run in Sunni jurisprudence, Sheikh al-Ghizzi recounts a famous historical incident documented by the Sunni historian Ibn Khallikan in Wafayat al-A'yan. The story describes how the Hanafi Sultan Mahmud ibn Subuktigin was doubting his school of thought and asked the scholars to demonstrate a valid prayer according to both the Shafi'i and Hanafi schools. The Shafi'i scholar al-Qaffal al-Marwazi demonstrated the Hanafi prayer by wearing dog skin, smearing himself with feces, performing ablution with wine, praying backwards, reciting in Persian instead of Arabic, skipping bowing, and finally concluding the prayer by breaking wind instead of saying the Salam. When the Hanafis protested, their own books were checked, and al-Qaffal's horrifying demonstration was proven to be entirely valid according to Abu Hanifa's jurisprudence! The Sheikh notes that even this book suffers from modern censorship to hide these embarrassing details.

Therefore, it is not strange that we find this book Wafayat al-A'yan by Ibn Khallikan. Biography number 713. Mahmud ibn Subuktigin is the name of the person whose biography is written in this entry... And the Imam of the Two Sanctuaries, Abu al-Ma'ali Abd al-Malik al-Juwayni... mentioned:

That the mentioned Sultan Mahmud ibn Subuktigin was upon the madhhab (school of thought) of Abu Hanifa... So a doubt fell into his mind. He gathered the jurists from both groups... The agreement was that they would pray two Rak'ahs in his presence upon the madhhab of Imam al-Shafi'i... and upon the madhhab of Abu Hanifa...
So al-Qaffal al-Marwazi prayed... according to the madhhab of al-Shafi'i. And he said: This is a prayer that Imam al-Shafi'i... does not permit anything less than it.

Then he prayed two Rak'ahs upon what Abu Hanifa... permits. So he wore the tanned skin of a dog! ... and he smeared a quarter of it with impurity! And he performed ablution with date wine (Nabidh al-Tamr).
He wore the tanned skin of a dog... And the flies and mosquitoes gathered on him! ... And his ablution was a reversed, inverted ablution.

Of course, here it is written "Then he faced the Qiblah." By the right of Al-Hussein, I read in an old copy... "Then he turned his back to the Qiblah (Istadbara al-Qiblah)!" ... This is distortion upon distortion! This is a distortion in the prayer, and a distortion in the book, distortion upon distortion!

And he entered the state of prayer... without intention in the prayer. And he said the Takbir (Allahu Akbar) in Persian! ... According to the copy I memorize: 'He entered the state of prayer without intention, and said the Takbir in Persian, so he said Khoda Buzurg, then he did not recite Al-Fatihah, he recited a verse and translated it into Persian!'

And of course, do not be surprised that he did not recite Al-Fatihah! Because narrations are present that the Caliph Umar prayed and did not recite Al-Fatihah... Then he pecked two pecks like the pecking of a rooster! Meaning in the prostration, without separation and without bowing! And he performed the Tashahhud, and after that... he broke his wind (Ahdatha)! He let out a sound at the end of it without the intention of Salam!

And he said: 'O Sultan, this is the prayer of Abu Hanifa!' ... So the Hanafis denied that this was the prayer of Abu Hanifa. So al-Qaffal ordered the books of Abu Hanifa to be brought. ... So the prayer upon the madhhab of Abu Hanifa was found to be exactly as al-Qaffal had demonstrated! So the Sultan turned away from the madhhab of Abu Hanifa and held onto the madhhab of al-Shafi'i...

And this incident is historically well-known as the reason for the conversion of Mahmud ibn Subuktigin. The Sultan is Sunni, the book is Sunni, and the scholars are Sunni, and the Shia have no involvement in that! ... You notice this distortion? Distortion leads to distortion, and distortion upon distortion!
Segment 7: The Lost Prayer of Muhammad and the Final Tears of the Prophet

Sheikh al-Ghizzi delivers a devastating critique of the early Ummah using Sahih al-Bukhari, showing how the Companions completely lost the original prayer of the Prophet. He cites Imran ibn Husayn, who rejoiced at praying behind Imam Ali in Basra because it reminded him of Muhammad's true prayer, leading to the obvious question: What were they praying during the previous caliphates? Furthermore, Anas ibn Malik laments that everything from the Prophet's time, including the prayer, had been completely lost and corrupted. Because the "most authentic" Sunni books are riddled with such censorship and bizarre headings to hide this reality, the Sheikh reaffirms his rejection of the "game of chains." He powerfully concludes the episode with heartbreaking narrations detailing the Prophet's final, tearful warnings about the impending burning of Fatima's door, her being slapped, and his divine declaration: "By Allah, O Fatima, I will not be pleased until you are pleased."

And that is not a strange thing at all. Let us go to al-Bukhari! And I take everything I transmit from al-Bukhari and Muslim—authentic sources and authentic chains—to prove the correctness of my statement "the game of sources and chains of narration." Let us read these two narrations. Page 142...
I prayed with Ali, may Allah be pleased with him, in Basra. This man reminded us of a prayer we used to pray with the Messenger of Allah!

**The question here is: Then what were the people praying?!** ... And the Umayyads had not ruled yet, nor the Abbasids! Now the caliphate of Uthman had ended, and the caliphate of Ali began, and these are the Companions, and this Imran ibn Husayn from the Companions says: 'He prayed with us the prayer of Muhammad.' So what were the people praying then?
I go to page 104. Let us read the two narrations from Sahih al-Bukhari... Hadith 529: From Anas he said:
I do not recognize anything from what was in the time of the Prophet! ... It was said: 'The prayer?' He said: 'Have you not lost what you lost in it?!'

Al-Bukhari distorts here too! Under what title does he place these hadiths? Under the title: 'Chapter of losing the prayer from its time' ... This is the distortion of headings! The hadiths talk about one thing, and the headings are about something else entirely!
The narration after it... I heard al-Zuhri, from al-Zuhri saying:
I entered upon Anas ibn Malik in Damascus while he was weeping... He said: 'I do not know anything of what I caught up with except this prayer, and this prayer has been lost!'

And this Anas ibn Malik himself is also from the distorters! When the Commander of the Believers asked him to testify in Kufa, and asked him to testify to the Pledge of Ghadir... he said: 'I have grown old and forgotten.' ... So the Emir said to him: 'May Allah strike you with an exposing mark (Fadihah).' ... He was struck with vitiligo (Baraṣ) in everything...
**So what is the value of sources if the prayer was lost?! And if the hadiths were distorted? And if the facts were altered, and if the meanings were changed?**

This is the reason for which the Imams say: 'The right path is in opposing them (Al-Sawab fi Khilafihim).' ... In the reality of the situation, in the reality of matters, Umar made it clear to us in Sahih Muslim... what is Ali's opinion of Abu Bakr and Umar? A liar, treacherous, a traitor!

So by Allah upon you! After these details... is this not a game?! This is a game! They play it whenever they wish! ... Therefore, I said that I will not adopt this method, and this theory: researching the authenticity of chains and the authenticity of sources. **I will search in contexts (Qara'in). I will search in every part of the speech...**

I conclude the episode with the words of the Greatest Prophet... This narration is narrated from Imam al-Kadhim. And the book in my hands is **Awalim al-Ulum** by Sheikh Abdullah ibn Nurullah al-Bahrani...

And know, O Ali! Indeed, I am pleased with whomever my daughter Fatima is pleased with!... O Ali! Woe to whoever oppresses her! And woe to whoever extorts her right! And woe to whoever violates her sanctity! And woe to whoever burns her door! And woe to whoever harms her husband! ... By Allah, O Fatima, I will not be pleased until you are pleased! Then I will not be pleased until you are pleased!
These are the last words the lips of the Seal of the Prophets overflowed with! ... This word, let us embroider it upon our hearts: 'By Allah, O Fatima, I will not be pleased until you are pleased!'
The final word, and I ask you for supplication. Transmitted by Sheikh al-Saduq in **Al-Amali** from the Commander of the Believers, peace be upon him, he said:

While Fatima, Al-Hassan, Al-Hussein, and I were with the Messenger of Allah, he turned to us and wept! ... He said: 'I weep over your being struck on the crown, on the top of your head! And Fatima's cheek being slapped!'

So his eyes were tearing up for what thing? For the slapping of Fatima on her cheek. And she was struck on her cheek, and slapped on her eye... **It exceeded the limit with the slapping of the cheek; may the hand of tyranny and aggression be paralyzed**. I ask you all for supplication. I will meet you upon the love of the mother of Al-Hassan and Al-Hussein, with the third episode tomorrow. In the safety of Allah.

title: The File of Fatima - Episode 3: The Documents of the Great Crime and the Book of Sulaym ibn Qays
speaker: Sheikh Abdul-Halim al-Ghizzi

content.text: Overview: Episode 3

In the third episode of the File of Fatima, Sheikh al-Ghizzi deepens his forensic investigation into the **Great Crime** committed against the daughter of the Prophet. Opening with the profound theological framework of the **Ziyarat al-Jami'ah li-A'immat al-Mu'minin**, he establishes the Ummah's systematic **betrayal and covenant-breaking**. The episode synthesizes undeniable historical fragments from both Sunni and Shia sources—including Al-Imamah wa al-Siyasah, Fara'id al-Simtayn, Kamil al-Ziyarat, and the suppressed letters in Bihar al-Anwar—to prove that Fatima was brutally **murdered by beating**. The Sheikh exposes the horrifying details of the assault, the tearing of the Prophet's deed to Fadak, and her absolute refusal to return the obligatory Islamic greeting to her oppressors, signifying their excommunication from the fold of faith. The investigation culminates in a meticulous reading of the **Book of Sulaym ibn Qays**, the "Alphabet of the Shia," which explicitly details the massive gathering of firewood to burn the house, the fatal strikes by Umar and Qunfudh, and the crushing of her rib that led to her tragic martyrdom.
Segment 1: The Comprehensive Ziyarat and the Treachery of the Ummah

Sheikh al-Ghizzi opens the third episode by reciting excerpts from the Ziyarat al-Jami'ah li-A'immat al-Mu'minin, a powerful supplication directed to the Imams that explicitly details the historical betrayal of the Ummah. The Ziyarat outlines how the people abandoned the Prophet's covenant on his deathbed, attacked the House of Prophethood, usurped the rights of Ali, and severely oppressed Fatima to the point of breaking her heart. The Sheikh uses this text to frame the episode, establishing that the tragedy of Fatima was not an isolated incident, but the central crime in a broader, systematic dismantling of the Prophet's legacy and a total violation of his commands.

Peace be upon Fatima, the Chosen (al-Mustafa), and Al-Murtada her husband, and the Infallibles from her children. Peace be upon the Fatimids in every time and place, her offspring, her Shia, and her lovers, and the mercy of Allah and His blessings.

The Fatimi File, Episode Three. I open my speech with what came in the **Ziyarat al-Jami'ah li-A'immat al-Mu'minin** (The Comprehensive Ziyarat for the Imams of the Believers). And it is other than the Ziyarat al-Jami'ah al-Kabirah found in Mafatih al-Jinan... Among what came in this noble Ziyarat, while we address the Household of Infallibility:

O my Masters! O Family of the Messenger of Allah! Indeed I seek nearness to Allah, Majestic and High, by opposing those who betrayed you, and broke their pledge to you, and rejected your Guardianship (Wilayah), and denied your status, and cast off the noose of your obedience, and abandoned the causes of your love, and sought nearness to their pharaohs by disassociating from you, and turning away from you!
The text of the Ziyarat continues, exposing the origins of the betrayal:

And that is because of what the corrupt, oppressive tyrants, and the envious transgressors paved for them! The people of covenant-breaking, treachery, opposition, and deceit! And the hearts filled with the filth of polytheism, and the bodies packed with disbelief, who concealed hypocrisy, and devoted themselves to the ties of discord!
And the immediate aftermath of the Prophet's passing:

So when the Chosen One (Al-Mustafa) passed away... they snatched the opportunity, and seized the chance, and violated the sanctity! And they betrayed him on his deathbed, and hastened to break the allegiance, and oppose the confirmed covenants... So the bedouins and the remnants of the [pagan] parties rushed to the House of Prophethood and the Message, and the landing place of Revelation and the angels... Until they broke the covenant of the Chosen One regarding his brother, the Banner of Guidance...
And regarding the pure Lady of Light:

And they wounded the liver of the Best of Creation by oppressing his daughter, persecuting his beloved, humiliating his dear one, and minimizing his flesh and the piece of his liver! And they abandoned her after him, and belittled her worth, and made lawful her sacred things, and cut off her kinship, and denied her brotherhood...
And the Ziyarat addresses the continuous slaughter of the Infallibles:

O my Masters! O my Masters! Would that the Chosen One could see you, while the arrows of the Ummah are sunk into your livers! And their spears are aimed at your throats! And their swords are lapping up your blood! ... So are there any trials, O my Masters, except those that clung to you? And any calamities except those that enveloped you? And any tragedies except those that singled you out?

This is Fatima. And her children, the family of Fatima, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon them all. And those are her enemies: their names are clear, their persons are known, and all the evidence points to them with clarity and evidence.

Episode Three. And the speech in it is about another aspect of the Fatimi File. The oppression of Fatima. The Great Crime: the murder of the daughter of the Prophet, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family. I try in every episode of the episodes of the Fatimi File to take up an aspect of her oppression, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon her.
Segment 2: The Boycott and the Final Confrontation on the Deathbed

Sheikh al-Ghizzi revisits the foundational Sunni text Al-Imamah wa Al-Siyasah by Ibn Qutaybah, highlighting the terrifying reality that Umar and his men remained at Fatima's door even after others fled in tears. He then moves to a detailed account from Bihar al-Anwar detailing Abu Bakr and Umar's attempt to visit the bedridden Fatima. The Sheikh emphasizes her physical inability to turn her own face due to the severe injuries from the assault, her absolute refusal to return their obligatory Islamic greeting (Salam), and her devastating vow to curse them in every prayer. He explains that these varying historical texts act like different Quranic verses, each providing a unique angle to form a complete picture of the tragedy.

In this episode, we take up some of the documents and some of the texts. I mentioned in the first episode, and likewise in the second episode, what came in Al-Imamah wa al-Siyasah by Ibn Qutaybah... I will read paragraphs—the important paragraphs in the text which Ibn Qutaybah brought.
So when the people heard her voice and her weeping, they turned back crying, and their hearts were about to crack and their livers to split!
Not all of them! A portion of the group. Who remained? "And Umar remained, and with him a group." This is by the text of Al-Imamah wa al-Siyasah by the scholar of the opponents, Ibn Qutaybah.

In another place, when he came to visit her—and she did not desire it, but the Commander of the Believers permitted them to visit al-Zahra... "When they sat near her, she turned her face to the wall. **He greeted her, but she did not return the Salam to them.**"
A red line under this phrase! "He greeted her, but she did not return the Salam to them." It is clear and very axiomatic in the teachings of Islam that returning the Salam to a Muslim is obligatory!

And another line, while she is addressing Abu Bakr: "By Allah, I will surely invoke Allah against you in every prayer I pray! **By Allah, I will surely invoke Allah against you in every prayer I pray!**" The Fatima who does not pray for herself says: "By Allah, I will surely invoke Allah against you in every prayer I pray!"

In **Bihar al-Anwar**. And this is the 43rd volume of Bihar al-Anwar by our Sheikh al-Majlisi, may Allah the Exalted's pleasure be upon him. A narration containing detail which Sheikh al-Majlisi transmits from **Ilal al-Shara'i'** by our Sheikh al-Saduq. I excerpt from it the point of need.

So Ali, peace be upon him, went out and gave them permission. So when their eyes fell upon her, he greeted her, but she did not return [the greeting] to them. And she turned her face away from them, so he shifted and faced her, until she did that several times. And she said: 'O Ali, bring the garment' after she became tired. And she said to the women around her: 'Turn my face.'

Meaning **she could not turn her face from the severity of the sickness! The sickness that afflicted her from the wounds and from the beating that fell upon her** when the people attacked her house...

What did al-Zahra say? ... "So she turned to Ali, peace be upon him, and said: 'I will not speak a single word to them until I ask them about something they heard from the Messenger of Allah...'" Until she asked:

'I implore you by Allah, do you remember that the Messenger of Allah... said: Fatima is a part of me, and I am from her. Whoever harms her has harmed me...?' They said: 'O Allah, yes.' So she said: 'Praise be to Allah.' Then she said: 'O Allah, I make You witness, so bear witness, O those who are present with us, that they have indeed harmed me in my life and at my death! By Allah, I will not speak a single word to you both until I meet my Lord and complain to Him about what you both have done to me and committed against me!'

So Umar said: "Amazing, O people, how you put him in charge of your affairs! And you are an old man who has become senile (Kharafta), submitting to the anger of a woman and rejoicing at her pleasure! And what is it if a woman becomes angry?!" And he stood up and left.
Segment 3: The Theological Weight of Refusing the Salam

Sheikh al-Ghizzi references a commentary he wrote years ago for the book Awalim al-Ulum to explain the profound theological implications of Fatima refusing to return Abu Bakr and Umar's greeting. He draws a direct parallel from Sahih al-Bukhari regarding Ka'b ibn Malik, who was boycotted by the Prophet and all Muslims for failing to support the Prophet in the Battle of Tabuk. During that boycott, the Prophet explicitly refused to return Ka'b's Salam because Ka'b had momentarily exited the circle of faith. The Sheikh deduces that Fatima's absolute refusal to return the Salam to Abu Bakr and Umar—until the day she died—was not a mere personal grudge, but a divine excommunication, indicating that they had exited the fold of faith and died as disbelievers under her enduring wrath.

Here I go to another book, which is **Awalim al-Ulum** (The Worlds of Sciences) by Sheikh Abdullah ibn Nurullah al-Bahrani. And it serves as an addendum (Mustadrak) to Bihar al-Anwar. In the second volume of Awalim al-Zahra... I wrote a commentary regarding what came in Al-Imamah wa al-Siyasah when it came in this text: "He greeted her, but she did not return the Salam to them."

I said: "Secondly, it came in Sahih al-Bukhari... I heard Ka'b ibn Malik narrating when he stayed behind from Tabuk, and the Messenger of Allah forbade speaking to us. 'And I would come to the Messenger of Allah'—this is Ka'b ibn Malik—'and I would greet him with Salam. So I would say to myself: Did he move his lips to return the Salam or not?'"

Knowing that Ka'b ibn Malik was one of the three who were left behind... And the Prophet, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, had ordered the Muslims not to speak to them at all, and not to return the Salam to them! Why? And that was only because they had exited from the circle of truth and faith when they did not support the Messenger of Allah!
**And had they died while the Messenger of Allah, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, was angry with them, they would have died as disbelievers!**

But Ka'b ibn Malik and his two companions, mercy encompassed them, and Allah and His Messenger turned in forgiveness toward them. **But as for those whom the Great Truthful Lady, prayers of Allah be upon her, departed from this world while she was angry and wrathful toward them, and she did not return the Salam to them at all until she was martyred!**
This is a very important issue and a very important indication: that they greeted her, and she did not return the Salam to them!
Segment 4: The Convergence of Sunni and Shia Texts on the Murder by Beating

Sheikh al-Ghizzi synthesizes explicit texts from both Sunni and Shia traditions to conclusively prove that Fatima was murdered. He recalls the prophecy in the Sunni text Fara'id al-Simtayn, where the Prophet states Fatima will die "murdered" and curses the man who strikes her side and causes her miscarriage. He then pairs this with the highly authentic Shia text Kamil al-Ziyarat, where Imam al-Sadiq transmits the Prophet's words that Fatima will "die from that beating." The Sheikh concludes that her severe bedridden state—which prevented her from even turning her head away from her oppressors—was the direct, agonizing result of this fatal assault.

The speech continues by gathering contexts (Qara'in) from here and there, so that the picture becomes complete and the full painting becomes clear. Another document... what came in Fara'id al-Simtayn. And it is from the books of the people (Sunnis) and not from the books of the Shia.
So she will come to me sorrowful, distressed, oppressed, usurped, and murdered (Maqtulah)!

Fatima was murdered! This is the speech of the Messenger of Allah. The Messenger of Allah says at that moment: "O Allah, curse whoever oppressed her! And punish whoever usurped from her, and humiliate whoever humiliated her! And abide forever in Your Fire whoever struck her side until she miscarried her child!"

Upon this exact same context is what came in **Kamil al-Ziyarat**, and it is from the most trusted books of the Imamiyyah (Shia) by our Sheikh Ibn Qulawayh, may Allah the Exalted's pleasure be upon him. The narration is from Hammad ibn Uthman from Abu Abdullah (Imam al-Sadiq), prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him. And he transmits the speech from the Messenger of Allah, until the hadith says:
And she will miscarry what is in her womb from the beating, and she will die from that beating!

A short while ago we read [that] when they entered upon her, she was on the bed, and she began to turn her face from one side to the other. And from the severity of the exhaustion, from the severity of the illness, she could not turn her face while she was on the pillow! She asked the women close to her to turn her face, fleeing from looking at the faces of the two men! "And she will miscarry what is in her womb from the beating."

So Fatima, as in the text of Fara'id al-Simtayn from the books of the opponents: "murdered" (Maqtulah), she died murdered. And in Kamil al-Ziyarat from the books of her Shia and her lovers, the narration clarified that she is killed, she dies from the beating, she was beaten until she died! **How was Fatima murdered? She was murdered by beating**, as our Imam al-Sadiq, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him, said.
Segment 5: The Suppressed Confession and the Multiple Attackers

Sheikh al-Ghizzi returns to the suppressed 30th volume of Bihar al-Anwar, containing Umar's letter to Muawiyah. He emphasizes a crucial detail: the firewood gathered was not merely symbolic, but a massive amount intended to burn the entire house down with its inhabitants inside. Umar explicitly confesses to whipping Fatima's hands, kicking the door into her pregnant stomach, and slapping her face so violently that her earring broke. The Sheikh then introduces a new piece of evidence from Al-Ihtijaj by al-Tabarsi, where Imam al-Hassan directly confronts Al-Mughirah ibn Shu'bah, accusing him of also striking Fatima until she bled. This reveals the horrifying reality that Fatima was subjected to a coordinated assault by multiple attackers, and the beating was so severe it caused her to bleed through her garments.

I believe that these documents which I passed over, whether in the first episode or in this third episode, have become clear. In Bihar al-Anwar, in the thirtieth volume also, I read what came in the letter that Umar ibn al-Khattab wrote and sent to Muawiyah. Umar in his letter to Muawiyah says:
...and I took [the whip] and I struck! And I said to Khalid ibn al-Walid: 'You and our men, come forward in gathering the firewood!'

There is an image that sometimes arises that the firewood was a small amount and was placed at the door of Fatima only! ... **The firewood that was gathered was to burn the house! And this is clear from all the texts.** There is no text that says the firewood was a little gathered at the door only. We will read the texts, and you will witness that all the texts say the gathered firewood was to burn the house!
Umar's letter continues:

So I pushed it while it was difficult for her. So I struck her hands with the whip, and it pained her... So I kicked the door, while she had stuck her insides to the door to barricade it!... And she said: 'O Father! O Messenger of Allah! Thus is it done to your beloved and your daughter! Ah! Take me to you, for by Allah, the one in my insides has been killed...'
He goes on:

And I heard her in labor pains while she was leaning against the wall. So I pushed the door and entered. She came at me with a face whose sight I feared, so I slapped her a slap on her cheeks from outside the veil (Khimar), and her earring broke and scattered to the ground!

Of course, there were those who were with him, and that will come in the texts. It will come to us in the texts that the one who squeezed al-Zahra was not only Umar. Qunfudh comes in the texts, and he is from the followers of Umar. And Al-Mughirah ibn Shu'bah also comes in the texts! For example, in **Al-Ihtijaj** by Sheikh al-Tabarsi. A long argumentation between our Imam al-Hassan and his boasting against Muawiyah... And Imam al-Hassan, while he is speaking about his mother... says:
And you are the one who struck Fatima the daughter of the Messenger of Allah, until you made her bleed, and she cast what was in her womb!

Notice, a beating to the point of bleeding! How severe would this beating be? Especially since Fatima was wrapped and covered in her clothes! A beating that is from behind the clothes and leads to bleeding, how severe must it be?

The picture becomes complete from here and there. According to the text in Umar's letter to Muawiyah, he speaks about the fact that he directly undertook this matter. And here in the book Al-Ihtijaj, our Imam al-Hassan clarifies that Al-Mughirah struck her until he made her bleed, and she cast what was in her womb.
Segment 6: The Torn Deed, the Final Will, and the Secret Grave

Sheikh al-Ghizzi presents a shocking account from the 53rd volume of Bihar al-Anwar, where Imam al-Sadiq details how Umar demanded the written deed (Sahifah) for Fadak that the Prophet had given to Fatima, spat on it, and tore it to pieces in front of the public. He then reads from Fatima's heartbreaking final will, recorded in Irshad al-Qulub. In it, she explicitly forbids the Ummah that oppressed her from attending her funeral. She recounts her own tragedy in this will: the gathering of highly flammable firewood (Jazl), the whipping of her arm, the kicking of the door onto her pregnant body, the breaking of her earring, and the murder of her unborn son, Muhsin. The segment ends with the Ummah's desperate attempt to exhume her secret grave for political optics, which was violently halted by a furious Imam Ali.

This attack on the Messenger of Allah, the attack on the family of the Messenger of Allah is not strange. This is the 53rd volume of Bihar al-Anwar; there is a text in it that perhaps many have not heard of. This narration is narrated by Al-Mufaddal ibn Umar, from our Imam al-Sadiq, prayers of Allah be upon him:

And the saying of Umar... 'Bring your document which you mentioned your father wrote for you!' And she brought out the document. And he took it from her, and unfolded it before the witnesses from Quraysh, the Muhajirun, the Ansar, and the rest of the Arabs, and he spat on it, and tore it to pieces!

Meaning there is a document that the Greatest Prophet wrote for al-Zahra regarding Fadak and the 'Awali; they belong to al-Zahra. This is a very important issue: that the Messenger of Allah wrote a document for Fatima, but how was this document dealt with? It was taken, unfolded before the people, spat upon, and torn up! So the one who does this matter, does that which happened to al-Zahra, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon her.

There is another text, which is the will of the daughter of the Messenger of Allah... when she willed that she did not want the people to pray over her or attend her funeral. The narration is transmitted from Irshad al-Qulub, quoted by the author of Al-Awalim. What does the daughter of Muhammad say?

Do not let an Ummah that broke the covenant of Allah, and the covenant of my father the Messenger of Allah regarding the Commander of the Believers Ali, pray over me! And they wronged me of my right, and took my inheritance, and tore up my document which he wrote for me from my father regarding the ownership of Fadak...
She continues in her will:

So they gathered thick firewood (Hatab al-Jazl) at our door, and they brought fire to burn it, and burn us! I stood at the frame of the door, and implored them by Allah and by my father to leave us alone and support us. So Umar took the whip from the hand of Abu Bakr's freedman and struck my arm with it! The whip curled around my arm until it became like a bracelet. And he kicked the door with his foot and forced it back onto me while I was pregnant! So I fell on my face while the fire was blazing and scorching my face! And he struck me with his hand until my earring scattered from my ear! And labor pains came to me, and I miscarried Muhsin, murdered without a crime! So is this an Ummah that should pray over me?!

For this reason, Fatima refused to let this Ummah pray over her. And for this reason, Fatima wanted her grave hidden, so that no one from these people would visit it. Yet despite that, they searched for her grave and wanted to exhume it! ... So Abu Bakr said: 'Bring trustworthy Muslims to exhume these graves until you find her grave so we can pray over her!'
This reached the Commander of the Believers, so he came out of his house furiously, his face red:
So Ali advanced, swearing by Allah: 'If one stone is searched from these graves, I will put the sword to the last of this Ummah!' So the people turned and fled.
Segment 7: The Ultimate Document - The Book of Sulaym ibn Qays

Sheikh al-Ghizzi unveils the most critical document in Shia history regarding the tragedy: The Book of Sulaym ibn Qays. He establishes its unparalleled authenticity by quoting Imams al-Sajjad and al-Sadiq, who referred to it as the "alphabet of the Shia" and a "secret from the secrets of the family of Muhammad." He then reads the harrowing, detailed account of the assault: Umar's order to gather massive amounts of firewood to burn the entire house, his setting the door on fire and forcing it open, his striking of Fatima's side with his sword scabbard, and his whipping of her arm.
