The text details Imam Ali's fierce but restrained defense, the subsequent mob attack led by Qunfudh and Khalid ibn al-Walid, and the crushing of Fatima's rib that led to her miscarriage. The Sheikh concludes by revealing Imam Ali's bitter confirmation that Umar later exempted Qunfudh from taxes specifically as a reward for the fatal beating he delivered to Fatima.

We now move to a document that is among the most important documents telling us about Fatima: Sulaym ibn Qays, may Allah the Exalted's pleasure be upon him, and his book Al-Saqifah, the "Alphabet of the Shia" (Abjad al-Shi'ah), the **Book of Sulaym ibn Qays**.

A narration from our Imam al-Sajjad, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him. Aban ibn Abi Ayyash said in the opening of the book of Sulaym that they read this book to Imam al-Sajjad for three days. What did our Imam al-Sajjad say?
Sulaym spoke the truth, may Allah have mercy on him. This is all our hadith; we know it!
What does our Imam al-Sadiq, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him, say?

Whoever of our Shia and our lovers does not have the Book of Sulaym ibn Qays al-Hilali, then he has nothing of our affair! And he knows nothing of our causes! It is the Alphabet of the Shia (Abjad al-Shi'ah), and it is a secret from the secrets of the family of Muhammad, peace be upon them.

The Book of Sulaym ibn Qays is from the Four Hundred Foundations (al-Usul al-Arba'mi'ah). Sheikh al-Nu'mani... says: "There is no disagreement among all the Shia... that the Book of Sulaym ibn Qays al-Hilali is a Foundation (Asl) from the greatest books of the Foundations." ... And these Two Truthful Ones, al-Kulayni and Ibn Babawayh al-Qummi (Sheikh al-Saduq), transmitted from this Great Foundation.
I browse through this very highly esteemed document: the Book of Sulaym ibn Qays... On page 585 of the second volume. A long narration, I excerpt from it the point of need.

So Umar became angry and said: 'What do we have to do with women?' Then he ordered people around him to carry firewood. So they carried the firewood, and Umar carried with them! And they placed it around the house of Ali, Fatima, and their two sons, peace be upon them.
The text continues with Umar calling for fire and setting the door ablaze:

Then he pushed it and entered. So Fatima, peace be upon her, confronted him and cried out: 'O Father! O Messenger of Allah!' So Umar raised the sword while it was in its scabbard, and he struck her side with it! And she cried out: 'O Father!' So he raised the whip and struck her arm with it! So she called out: 'O Messenger of Allah! How evil are the successors Abu Bakr and Umar after you!'
Imam Ali leaps to defend her, but restrains himself due to the Prophet's covenant. Umar calls for help, and the mob storms the house:

So the cursed Qunfudh set out and attacked, he and his companions, without permission. And Ali, peace be upon him, rushed to his sword, but they beat him to it and overpowered him by numbers!... And Fatima, peace be upon her, intercepted between them and him at the door of the house. So the cursed Qunfudh struck her with the whip! When she died, there was a mark on her arm like a bracelet from his strike.
The report continues and says:

And Umar had sent to him: 'If Fatima gets between you and him, strike her!' So Qunfudh, may Allah curse him, forced her to the frame of the door of her house, pushed her, and broke a rib from her side! And she miscarried a fetus from her womb! So she remained bedridden until she died, may Allah's prayers be upon her, a martyr from that!
Another text also, I will read it from the Book of Sulaym ibn Qays... Sulaym asks Imam Ali why Umar did not fine Qunfudh like he fined all his other governors. Imam Ali replies:
He thanked him for a strike he struck Fatima, peace be upon her, with the whip, so she died, and on her arm was its mark like a bracelet!

These are excerpts from the **Book of Sulaym ibn Qays**, the most important document, the most important source, the most important chain, and the most important evidence regarding this crime that occurred upon the daughter of the Messenger of Allah, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family.

title: The File of Fatima - Episode 4: The Atmospheres of Terror and the Raining Whips
speaker: Sheikh Abdul-Halim al-Ghizzi

content.text: Overview: Episode 4

In the fourth episode of the File of Fatima, Sheikh al-Ghizzi focuses on reconstructing the violent **atmospheres of Medina** to fully contextualize the Great Crime. He demonstrates that Abu Bakr's caliphate was not established by peaceful consensus, but through sheer **military coercion and state-sponsored terror**. Drawing from Tarikh al-Tabari and other Sunni and Shia texts, the Sheikh details how Umar deployed thousands of armed Bedouin mercenaries to severely beat the people of Medina and drag them to pledge allegiance. He further exposes the hypocrisy of the Saqifah by showing how Umar used the argument of "kinship" to seize power while denying that exact right to Imam Ali. Shifting to the moral character of the attackers, the Sheikh proves through Sunni sources that Al-Mughirah ibn Shu'bah—one of the primary men who struck Fatima—was a **known adulterer** saved from stoning by Umar's rigged justice system. The episode culminates in a harrowing synthesis of Shia texts detailing the fatal, multi-pronged assault on Fatima's house, revealing that Umar explicitly commanded the mob to **"Strike Fatima!"** causing whips to rain down upon the beloved daughter of the Prophet.
Segment 1: The Atmosphere of Terror - The Dragging of Ali

Sheikh al-Ghizzi begins the fourth episode by shifting the focus to the general atmosphere of Medina during the time of the assault. To understand the crime against Fatima, he argues, one must understand the environment in which it occurred. He starts by revisiting a terrifying scene from the Book of Sulaym ibn Qays, describing the moment Imam Ali was dragged violently from his home to Abu Bakr. The Sheikh highlights the sheer military force deployed against the family of the Prophet, noting that Abu Bakr was surrounded by heavily armed men with drawn swords, ready to execute anyone who opposed the new regime.

Peace be upon Fatima, the Chosen (al-Mustafa), and Al-Murtada after her, the Infallibles from her children. Peace be upon the Fatimids in every time and place, her offspring, her Shia, and her lovers, and the mercy of Allah and His blessings.
The Fatimi File, Episode Four. I am still browsing and turning the pages of this file. In this episode, my speech will be in three directions.

The first direction: We will wander through the texts to see the atmosphere in which the oppression of Fatima occurred, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon her. We will take a general look at what came in the texts that are in our hands, and the texts are many. I will select and focus on the most important of these texts which, in their entirety, draw a clear picture for us of the general atmosphere that surrounded that crime which occurred.

The first text I take up is from the **Book of Sulaym ibn Qays**. And this text, in the past episode, I read it to your hearing. In this episode, I take from it the point of need which relates to giving us a snapshot or a picture of the atmosphere that was surrounding the event of the crime.
Page 587 from the second volume of the **Book of Sulaym ibn Qays**, may Allah the Exalted's pleasure be upon him... On page 587 of this important document, the document of Al-Saqifah:
Then Ali, peace be upon him, was taken... being violently dragged (Yu'tallu 'Atlan)!
Meaning he was being pulled with force, with violence, and with severity by a large group of people. The picture becomes clear to us little by little.

...until he was brought to Abu Bakr, while Umar was standing with the sword over his head! ... And Khalid ibn al-Walid, Abu Ubaydah ibn al-Jarrah, Salim the freedman of Abu Hudhayfah, Mu'adh ibn Jabal, Al-Mughirah ibn Shu'bah, Usayd ibn Hudayr, and Bashir ibn Sa'd. And the rest of the people were sitting around Abu Bakr with their weapons!

You notice the picture? Ali is dragged violently, pulled severely, until he is brought to Abu Bakr, while Umar is standing with the sword over his head... Meaning there is a large group that came with Ali dragging him, and they are absolutely armed. And there is another group present with Abu Bakr, and all of them are armed too!
Segment 2: Home Invasions and the Reign of Terror in Medina

Sheikh al-Ghizzi draws from the 28th volume of Bihar al-Anwar to paint a terrifying picture of the post-Saqifah atmosphere in Medina. He describes how Umar formed armed gangs to systematically raid the homes (Kabs) of the Muhajirun and Ansar who refused to pledge allegiance, arresting them and dragging them to the mosque by force. While Umar targeted regular dissidents with a small group, the texts explicitly state he marched on the house of Fatima with a "massive group" (Jam' Kathir) with the intent to burn it down with her entire family inside. The Sheikh then reads the account of the companion Al-Bara' ibn 'Azib, who witnessed Abu Bakr and Umar's armed mob terrorizing the streets, striking anyone who passed by and physically forcing their hands onto Abu Bakr's in a coerced pledge of allegiance.

We add another point, and another picture so that the painting becomes complete in all its dimensions. This is **Bihar al-Anwar**. And this is the twenty-eighth volume of Bihar al-Anwar... I take these lines from this hadith, from this report. From Abdullah ibn Abd al-Rahman he said:

Then Umar tied his lower garment tightly and began roaming the streets of Medina, calling out: 'Indeed, Abu Bakr has been pledged allegiance to, so come to the allegiance!' ... Umar knew that a group was hiding in their houses. So he would target them with a group [of men], and invade their homes (Yakbisuhum).

Meaning he enters their houses by force, and arrests them. This invasion (Kabs)—meaning it was not only the house of al-Zahra that was invaded! But the greatest issue was in the house of al-Zahra, prayers of Allah be upon her.

You notice, meaning Medina was in a state of severe crisis; the circumstances were escalated to the furthest limit... This is an allegiance by force (Ikrah); otherwise why are they in their houses and brought to the mosque by force?

Until days passed, he approached with a large group (Jam' Kathir)... to the house of Ali ibn Abi Talib, and demanded him to come out, but he refused. So Umar called for firewood and fire, and said: 'By the One in Whose hand is Umar's soul, you will surely come out, or I will burn it down upon whoever is in it!'

Burn the house, not the door as some imagine! Burn the house upon whoever is in it, and with whoever is in it! And who is in it? Fatima, her husband, and her sons, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon them all.

I want to focus here on this issue, on the issue of the "large group." There is a massive group... The issue is not limited to one or two people, one individual or two. **There is a massive mob that came to the house of Fatima, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon her.**
In the same volume, the twenty-eighth volume of **Bihar al-Anwar**. And the hadith is transmitted from the Companion, Al-Bara' ibn 'Azib... What does Al-Bara' say?

Then I did not stay long until I saw Abu Bakr, Umar, and Abu Ubaydah approaching among the people of the Saqifah, while they were girded with San'ani waist-wrappers (Uzur San'aniyyah)... no one passed by them except that they struck him (Khabatuh)!

"Except that they struck him," meaning either they arrest him, or beat him, or force him to pledge allegiance! They strike severely. Al-Khabt (striking) in the language of the Arabs is striking with the sword... This is terror, terrorism to the people.
So if they recognized him, they extended his hand to the hand of Abu Bakr, whether he wished that or refused! So I lost my mind out of grief.
You notice the atmosphere. When we study the atmosphere, then we can imagine how the event occurred. The atmosphere is very important in knowing events and facts.
Segment 3: The Armed Suppression of the Twelve Companions

The Sheikh demonstrates how Abu Bakr's caliphate was enforced through sheer military intimidation rather than consensus. He quotes Sayyid al-Khoei's Mu'jam Rijal al-Hadith, detailing how twelve prominent Companions publicly rejected Abu Bakr's rule, forcing him to step down from the pulpit in defeat. In response, Umar drew his sword—ironic, the Sheikh notes, given Umar's well-documented history of fleeing battles like Khaybar and Hunayn—and threatened to behead any further dissenters. Citing Al-Sirat al-Mustaqim, the Sheikh reveals the staggering scale of this intimidation: Umar's allies, including Khalid ibn al-Walid, Salim, and Mu'adh, marched into the Prophet's mosque with thousands of heavily armed men, explicitly threatening to crush the skull of anyone from Imam Ali's camp who dared to speak out.

Let us read what came in **Mu'jam Rijal al-Hadith** wa Tafsil Tabaqat al-Ruwat by Sayyid al-Khoei, may Allah the Exalted's pleasure be upon him. And Al-Barqi mentioned at the end of his Rijal... that twelve men objected to Abu Bakr standing in the place of the Prophet, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family. He said: So Abu Bakr descended from the pulpit...

So when the following Friday came, Umar drew his sword. Umar, in history, in the history of the Prophet's battles, we have not read a stance for Umar... like the stances we read in history, for example in the Battle of Khaybar, when the banner was given to Abu Bakr, so he returned... And likewise Umar, when he was given the banner on the second day, returned fleeing! He fled from the battlefield. And the fleeing of the people is known... But the circumstances now are different, and Umar draws his sword!

So when the following Friday came, Umar drew his sword. Then he said: 'I will not hear a man say the likes of what she said... except I will strike his neck!' Then he set out, he, Salim, Mu'adh ibn Jabal, and Abu Ubaydah, with their swords drawn, until they brought Abu Bakr out and made him ascend the pulpit.
You notice the whole issue is weapons, force, men, coercion, threat, terrorism! These are the atmospheres that were prevailing in Medina.

This is another text from the book **Al-Sirat al-Mustaqim** ila Mustahaqqi al-Taqdim. By the Imami theologian Zayn al-Din al-Bayadi al-'Amili... After the conversations and discussions between a group of Companions who objected to the caliphate of Abu Bakr:

So Khalid came to them and said: 'Banu Hashim have coveted it!' And Salim came with a thousand men. And Mu'adh with a thousand men. So they went out to the mosque with their swords drawn! ... And Ali, peace be upon him, was sitting among a group of his companions. So Umar said: 'If any of you speaks with what he spoke yesterday, I will take that which contains his eyes!'
You notice the large groups of people? And these narrations did not transmit the truth entirely to us, they transmitted a facet to us. The issue is bigger than that.
Segment 4: The Mercenaries - Deploying the Bedouins Against Medina

Sheikh al-Ghizzi brings forward a chilling historical account cited by the great investigator Sayyid Ja'far Murtada al-'Amili, originally from the book of Sheikh al-Mufid. The text details how Umar seized the opportunity of a large group of Bedouins (A'rab) entering Medina for supplies. Knowing that the Quran describes the Bedouins as "the most severe in disbelief and hypocrisy," Umar hired them as armed mercenaries, offering them food and allowances on the condition that they enforce Abu Bakr's allegiance. Armed with wooden clubs and wrapped in San'ani waist-wrappers, these Bedouins flooded the streets, severely beating the inhabitants of Medina and dragging them to pledge allegiance by force. The Sheikh highlights this maximum use of terror to explain the brutal atmosphere in which the assault on Fatima's house took place.

I move to another text, mentioned by the investigating scholar Sayyid Ja'far Murtada al-'Amili, in his book Al-Sahih min Sirat al-Nabi al-A'zam... He transmits from the book of **Sheikh al-Mufid**, may Allah the Exalted's pleasure be upon him, his book on the history of the Battle of the Camel.

That a group of Bedouins (A'rab) entered Medina to buy provisions (Yumtaru) from it... So Umar sent for them, summoned them, and said to them: 'Take your portion and allowance in exchange for [enforcing] the allegiance to the Caliph of the Messenger of Allah! And whoever refuses, strike his head and his forehead!'
Bedouins! And the Bedouins are the most severe in disbelief and hypocrisy, by the explicit text of the Quran!

So by Allah, I saw the Bedouins having tightened their belts, and wrapped themselves in San'ani waist-wrappers... And they took wood (Khashab) in their hands, and went out until they severely beat the people (Khabatu al-Nasa Khabtan)! And they brought them, coerced, to the allegiance!

You notice the atmospheres? There are people, a group that attacked the house of Ali. And a group sitting with weapons upon them around Abu Bakr... And this is another text... meaning the use of force to the maximum limit, the use of coercion to the maximum limit. You notice the pictures? These are the atmospheres of Medina! So it is not strange, then, that the crime occurred. **These are the atmospheres that surrounded Fatima, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon her.**
Segment 5: The Armed Suppression of the Twelve Companions

The Sheikh demonstrates how Abu Bakr's caliphate was enforced through sheer military intimidation rather than consensus. He quotes Sayyid al-Khoei's Mu'jam Rijal al-Hadith, detailing how twelve prominent Companions publicly rejected Abu Bakr's rule, forcing him to step down from the pulpit in defeat. In response, Umar, Uthman, and others marched him back to the pulpit surrounded by heavily armed men from their tribes. Citing Al-Sirat al-Mustaqim, Tafsir al-Burhan, and Al-Ihtijaj, the Sheikh reveals the staggering scale of this intimidation: Umar's allies, including Khalid ibn al-Walid, Salim, and Mu'adh, marched into the Prophet's mosque with four thousand heavily armed mercenaries, explicitly threatening to crush the skull of anyone from Imam Ali's camp who dared to speak out.

There is another text in the book **Al-Khisal** by our Sheikh al-Saduq. A long narration, which is the discussion of the twelve Companions with Abu Bakr, a strong, logical, scientific, and very precise discussion. Until the narrator says at the end of this report: "That Abu Bakr sat in his house for three days" after the discussion. Because it was a strong discussion, and in front of the people, and Abu Bakr was silenced on the pulpit; he could not answer!

So when the third day came, which was Friday, Umar ibn al-Khattab, Talhah, Uthman ibn Affan, Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf, Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas, and Abu Ubaydah ibn al-Jarrah came to him, with each one of them having ten men from their tribes, drawing their swords! So they brought him out of his house and onto the pulpit... So a speaker among them said: 'By Allah, if any of you returns and speaks the likes of what he spoke... we will put our swords into him!' So they sat in their homes and no one spoke after that.

These are the atmospheres of Medina! These are the atmospheres of the "Rightly-Guided Caliphate"! These are the atmospheres that surrounded the calamity of Fatima, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon her. So it was not strange that her reaction was not to accept anyone attending her funeral, not to accept anyone of them praying over her, and not to accept anyone knowing her grave.
This text came in the third volume of **Tafsir al-Burhan** by Sayyid Hashim al-Bahrani... The text says:

So Uthman ibn Affan entered upon him [Abu Bakr] with a thousand men! ... And Mu'adh ibn Jabal came with a thousand men! ... And Salim the freedman of Hudhayfah came with a thousand men! And they continued to gather until they became four thousand men! And they came drawing their swords, with Umar leading them, until they stopped at the mosque of the Messenger of Allah, while the Commander of the Believers was among a group of his companions. So Umar said: 'O companions of Ali! Today, not a man among you will speak what he spoke yesterday, or we will take that which contains his eyes!'

You notice how much the number is? Four thousand! Where did this number come from? From those Bedouins who are the most severe in disbelief and hypocrisy... And it will come to us according to al-Tabari and other than al-Tabari that the Bedouins surrounding Medina are the ones who supported the "Rightly-Guided Caliphate", and they are the ones who provided this human cover for the coercion to pledge allegiance and the terrifying of the people in Medina that occurred.

This exact same content was transmitted by Sheikh al-Tabarsi, the mercy of Allah be upon him, in his book **Al-Ihtijaj**... "So men continued gathering to them, man by man, until four thousand men had gathered. So they went out drawing their swords, with Umar ibn al-Khattab leading them..."
Segment 6: The Explicit Confessions from Tarikh al-Tabari and the Bedouin Mercenaries

Sheikh al-Ghizzi turns to the prominent Sunni historian al-Tabari to confirm the armed occupation of Medina. He reads the explicit admission that the Bedouin tribe of Aslam flooded the streets in such massive numbers that the alleys became choked. Umar famously rejoiced, stating he was only certain of victory once he saw the tribe of Aslam arrive to enforce the allegiance. The Sheikh points out that this proves the people of Medina had largely refused to pledge allegiance, necessitating this overwhelming deployment of foreign mercenaries to crush dissent.
In Tarikh al-Tabari. And this is the second volume of Tarikh al-Tabari... Let us read this paragraph:
Abu Bakr ibn Muhammad al-Khuza'i narrated to me that Aslam... approached with its entirety until the alleys—the alleys of Medina—became choked with them!

This is al-Tabari, and it is not I who is saying it, and it is not a Shiite book! "That Aslam approached", meaning the tribe of Aslam, the Bedouins of Aslam, "approached with its entirety until the alleys became choked with them", meaning the alleys of Medina from their great numbers! These thousands upon thousands, four thousand... Medina was small, these thousands upon thousands that entered Medina... "so they pledged allegiance to Abu Bakr."
So Umar used to say: 'It was not until I saw Aslam that I became certain of victory!'

This means that the people of Medina generally had not [pledged allegiance]. It passed upon us how Umar had a group with him raiding homes, and how Abu Bakr and Umar went out forcing people to pledge allegiance to Abu Bakr, striking the people and then wiping their hands on Abu Bakr's hand, as passed upon us, coerced, whether they wished or refused.

These quotes, the quotes of al-Tabari, are not quotes, for example, of al-Majlisi in Bihar al-Anwar, and they give a clear picture... **When did Umar become convinced of victory? As the narration of al-Tabari says: when the Bedouins came with this multitude!**
Segment 7: The Logic of Saqifah, the Argument of Kinship, and the Threat to Sa'd ibn Ubadah

Sheikh al-Ghizzi analyzes the internal debate at Saqifah as recorded in Tarikh al-Tabari. When the Ansar propose shared power, Umar swiftly rejects it, arguing that the Arabs would only accept leadership from the Prophet's tribe, Quraysh. The Sheikh points out the glaring hypocrisy: Umar uses the argument of "kinship" to seize power, yet vehemently denies this exact same, vastly stronger claim for Imam Ali. The segment ends with a terrifying display of the mob's brutality as they nearly trample the bedridden Ansar leader Sa'd ibn Ubadah. When his followers plead for his life, Umar commands them to kill him, showcasing the ruthless violence that paved the way for Abu Bakr's rule.

In page 515 of the same book, from al-Tabari, the speech is regarding what took place inside the Saqifah of Banu Sa'idah. After the Ansar said: "An emir from us, and an emir from you," an emir from the Ansar and an emir from the Muhajirun, what did Umar reply to them? "So Umar said: 'Impossible (Hayhat)!...'" Notice the argumentation of Umar; pay attention to it.

So Umar said: 'Impossible! Two cannot gather in a single horn! By Allah, the Arabs will not agree to make you their commanders while their Prophet is from other than you! But the Arabs do not refuse to give their leadership to those among whom the Prophethood resided... And in this, we have the manifest proof and the clear authority against whoever among the Arabs refuses! Who disputes us the authority of Muhammad and his emirate, while we are his close allies and his tribe?!'

So is it not more appropriate that this speech be [used] against Umar himself, and Abu Bakr himself? Who is closer to Muhammad? Quraysh in its entirety? Or is Banu Hashim closer? Or is Ali closer?! And this is the exact same evidence that the Shia use to argue, and the opponents reject it out of stubbornness!

He says: "Whoever disputes us in that is advancing a falsehood! Who disputes us the authority of Muhammad and his emirate while we are his close allies and his tribe?" This speech of Umar is based on the argument of kinship. So this speech is also turned against Umar: there is someone closer than you! Therefore, whoever disputes the closest one, these descriptions apply to him!

And the speech continues until he says: "That Aslam approached with its entirety until the alleys became choked with them! ... So people approached from every side pledging allegiance to Abu Bakr, and they almost trampled Sa'd ibn Ubadah!" So people from the companions of Sa'd said: "Be careful of Sa'd!" because he was sick, he was thrown on the ground. "Be careful of Sa'd, do not trample him!"

So Umar said: 'Kill him! May Allah kill him!' Then Umar stood over his head... He said: 'I intended to trample you until your limbs break!' So [Sa'd] grabbed Umar by his beard and said: 'By Allah, if you cut a single hair from it, you would not return except with your front teeth missing!'

So Abu Bakr said: "Gently, O Umar! Gentleness is more effective here!" And this is the policy of Abu Bakr, the policy of gentleness... As for in other situations, it passed upon us how he commanded and executed setting fire to the house of Ali!
Segment 8: The Rigged Shura and the Threat of Beheadings

Sheikh al-Ghizzi jumps ahead to the end of Umar's life to show that this culture of military coercion never changed. He quotes Tarikh al-Tabari detailing Umar's infamous six-man Shura (electoral council). Far from a democratic consultation, Umar ordered Suhayb to stand over the Companions with drawn swords and behead any minority dissenters—even if the split was four to two. If there was a three-to-three tie, Umar rigged the outcome by commanding them to side with Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf (who he knew hated Ali) and to execute the opposing three. The Sheikh concludes that this terrifying environment completely validates Imam Ali's grievances in the famous Shaqshaqiyyah Sermon and perfectly contextualizes the violent atmosphere in which Fatima was murdered.

These atmospheres remained continuous. Meaning now, in the exact same Tarikh al-Tabari, if we go to the topic of the Shura in the exact same volume, page 750, 751. When Umar was subjected to the assassination operation and founded what is called the Shura.

And he said to Suhayb: 'Lead the people in prayer for three days, and bring in Ali, Uthman, Al-Zubayr, Sa'd, and Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf... and bring Abdullah ibn Umar, but he has no share in the matter! Stand over their heads, so if five gather and agree upon a man, and one refuses, strike his head!'
Why? Where is the Shura [consultation]? But this is the method that existed; this is the culture that was dominating the atmospheres!

Stand over their heads, so if five gather and agree upon a man, and one refuses, strike his head with the sword! And if four agree and choose a man from among them, and two refuse, then strike the heads of both of them! And if three agree on a man from among them, and three on another man... then be with those among whom is Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf—and he knew that Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf hated Ali!—and kill the rest!

This is an excellent Shura! This is a Shura in the best manner! This speech I did not transmit from the book Al-Kafi; this is the book of al-Tabari, their book! And this speech is the speech of Umar. These atmospheres are the atmospheres that were dominating Medina.

Therefore, when we read the Shaqshaqiyyah Sermon [of Imam Ali], we find these meanings clear in it. Those who deny the Shaqshaqiyyah Sermon, if they had fairness, and if they had knowledge of these details, they would have known that what came in the Shaqshaqiyyah Sermon is far less than the reality that Medina was living! **In the likes of these atmospheres was the oppression of al-Zahra. And in the likes of these atmospheres, the crime of the murder of the daughter of Muhammad, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, occurred.**
Segment 9: The Adultery of Al-Mughirah and the Corrupted Justice of Umar

Sheikh al-Ghizzi transitions to the second focus of the episode: exposing the moral character of Al-Mughirah ibn Shu'bah, one of the primary perpetrators who struck Fatima. To prove Imam al-Hassan's accusation that Al-Mughirah was a known adulterer saved by Umar from being stoned, the Sheikh reads directly from prominent Sunni historical texts, including Ibn Khallikan's Wafayat al-A'yan and Tarikh al-Tabari. The texts detail how four witnesses caught Al-Mughirah committing adultery in Basra. However, during the trial, Umar explicitly coached the fourth witness, Ziyad ibn Abih, to soften his testimony. Umar then used this technicality to acquit Al-Mughirah with a cry of "Allahu Akbar," whipping the three truthful witnesses instead. Later, Umar privately admitted to Al-Mughirah that he knew the first witness was telling the absolute truth.

The second direction that I want to take up is also in these exact same atmospheres... I mentioned in the past episode a hadith and speech I transmitted from the book **Al-Ihtijaj** by our Sheikh al-Tabarsi, regarding Imam al-Hassan's argumentation that took place between him and Muawiyah... Imam al-Hassan addresses Al-Mughirah:

And as for you, O Mughirah ibn Shu'bah, you are an enemy to Allah, a discarder of His Book, a denier of His Prophet, and you are the adulterer! And stoning became obligatory upon you, and the pious, righteous, just witnesses testified against you! So he [Umar] acquitted you, and repelled the truth with falsehoods, and honesty with deceptions... And you are the one who struck Fatima the daughter of the Messenger of Allah, until you made her bleed, and she cast what was in her womb!

I will go to the books of the people! ... This is Wafayat al-A'yan (Obituaries of Eminent Men) by Ibn Khallikan, Volume Six... the hadith about the incident of adultery of Al-Mughirah ibn Shu'bah which Imam al-Hassan pointed to.

They testified before Umar. The first witness, the second witness, the third witness. The fourth witness, who was he? It was Ziyad ibn Abih... When Ziyad came for the testimony, Umar said something to him; because of this speech that Umar said, he lightened the testimony!

When he saw him approaching, what did Umar say to Ziyad ibn Abih? **'I see a man... through whose tongue Allah will not disgrace a man of the Muhajirun!'** Because if he confessed with the exact same testimony of the previous witnesses... then at that time there is no escape from stoning Al-Mughirah! And Umar does not want to stone Al-Mughirah!

So Umar, may Allah be pleased with him, said: 'Allahu Akbar!' He rushed to them and struck them! He stood over Abu Bakrah and struck him with eighty lashes, and struck the rest. And the saying of Ziyad pleased him! And he dropped the legal punishment (Hadd) from Al-Mughirah!

Thus were the Islamic penal codes applied in the Islamic State, in this manner! ... "Then Umm Jamil met Umar ibn al-Khattab, may Allah be pleased with him, during the season... So Umar said to him: 'Do you know this woman, O Mughirah?' He said: 'Yes, this is Umm Kulthum bint Ali!'" Look at the mockery! How could he say about this adulterous woman, what does he say? 'This is Umm Kulthum bint Ali.' "So Umar said to him: 'Are you feigning ignorance with me?! By Allah, I do not think Abu Bakrah lied about you!'" Meaning Abu Bakrah was truthful.

This is Al-Mughirah ibn Shu'bah! And this story here is not only found in Wafayat al-A'yan at all, it is present in most books of history. This is Tarikh al-Tabari... And the exact same words are found in the book Al-Kamil fi al-Tarikh... And this exact same speech is present in very many sources, and these are all the books of the people!
Segment 10: The Killing of Muhsin and the Rain of Whips

Sheikh al-Ghizzi concludes the episode by entering the third direction: exploring texts that detail the direct, physical causes of Fatima's martyrdom and the miscarriage of Muhsin. Drawing from Awalim al-Ulum, Dala'il al-Imamah, and other Shia sources, the Sheikh recounts the horrific climax of the assault. He highlights Qunfudh striking her with the scabbard of the sword, the blazing firewood (Jazl), the door being kicked into her pregnant stomach, and the nail of the door piercing her chest. The segment reaches its devastating peak with Umar's explicit command to the mob: "Strike Fatima!" resulting in whips raining down upon the beloved daughter of the Prophet. The Sheikh closes the episode with a heartfelt, emotional tribute to her enduring spiritual status.

The third direction, which is the final direction in this episode. Models of texts that speak about the oppression of the mother of Al-Hassan and Al-Hussein, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon her. And this is the second volume of **Awalim al-Ulum** by our Sheikh al-Bahrani... The narration, as I said in Awalim al-Ulum, is transmitted from **Dala'il al-Imamah**:

So when the Messenger of Allah... passed away, and what happened on the day the people entered her house occurred... and what befell her from the kick of the man... she miscarried by it Muhsin, a fully formed boy! And that was the root of her illness and her death, prayers of Allah be upon her.
And a second narration on page 45 from the same volume:

And the cause of her death was that Qunfudh, the freedman of Umar, struck her with the scabbard of the sword by his order! So she miscarried Muhsin, and she became severely ill from that. And she did not permit anyone who had harmed her to enter upon her.
Another narration also transmitted from **Al-Bihar**, also from Al-Mufaddal ibn Umar from our **Imam al-Sadiq**:

And their gathering of Jazl and firewood... at the door to burn the house of the Commander of the Believers, Fatima, Al-Hassan, Al-Hussein, Zaynab, Umm Kulthum, and Fidhdhah. And their setting the fire on the door. The coming out of Fatima... And his rebuking her, and his saying: 'Enough, O Fatima! Muhammad is not present... And Ali is nothing but one of the Muslims, so choose if you wish his coming out for the allegiance to Abu Bakr, or burning you all!' ... And Umar struck her with the whip on her arm until it became like a black bracelet! And he kicked the door with his foot until it hit her belly while she was pregnant with Muhsin for six months, and her miscarrying him!
And in another narration transmitted from Ilm al-Yaqin fi Usul al-Din... "So Umar ordered Qunfudh to strike her with his whip!"
So Qunfudh struck her with the whip on her back and her sides until he exhausted her, and it left a mark on her noble body!

This line should have many red lines placed under it: "So Qunfudh struck her with the whip on her back and her sides until he exhausted her, and it left a mark on her noble body!" **That one made her bleed, and this one kicked her, and this one exhausted her, and this one slapped her!** "And that beating was the strongest cause in the miscarriage of her fetus."
And another narration on page 581 from Al-Jannah al-'Asimah: "Umar took a sword from Khalid ibn al-Walid and began striking with its scabbard on her shoulder until she became wounded!"
And another narration on page 582:

And the nail of the door penetrated her chest! And Fatima cried out: 'O Father! O Messenger of Allah! Look at what we have encountered after you from the son of al-Khattab and the son of Abu Quhafah!' So Umar turned to those around him and said: 'Strike Fatima!' So the whips rained down upon the beloved of the Messenger of Allah and his flesh!

Until... hear this speech! This is not my speech, page 582 from the book Awalim al-Ulum... **"So Umar turned to those around him and said: 'Strike Fatima!' So the whips rained down upon the beloved of the Messenger of Allah and his flesh."**

And this is a small part of the oppression of Fatima. The Fatimi File is still continuing. I said we gather contexts from here and there until the crime becomes clear. And we have seen some contexts in the books of the opponents, clear and explicit.

Fatima, O daughter of Muhammad! I testify that whoever connects with you has connected with the Messenger of Allah! And whoever cuts you off, O mother of Al-Hassan, has cut off the Messenger of Allah! And whoever shuns you, O mother of Al-Hussein, has shunned the Messenger of Allah! And whoever harms you, O mother of Zaynab, has harmed the Messenger of Allah!

title: The File of Fatima - Episode 5: Fabrications, Fadak, and the Slander on the Pulpits
speaker: Sheikh Abdul-Halim al-Ghizzi

content.text: Overview: Episode 5

In the fifth episode of the File of Fatima, Sheikh al-Ghizzi explores how the Great Crime was foundationally built upon a **web of fabrications and lies**. He examines the systemic denial of Fatima's rights, particularly the usurpation of **Fadak**, proving through her historic Fadakiyyah Sermon and Imam Ali's devastating debates that the state's actions fundamentally contradicted the Quran. The episode then uncovers a deeply suppressed historical reality: that the state-sponsored oppression culminated in the **public slander of Fatima's chastity on the pulpits of the Ummah**. Tracing this horrific legacy from the Saqifah through the Umayyad and Abbasid eras, the Sheikh highlights the sadistic mockery of the Ahlulbayt by rulers like Al-Mansur and Al-Mutawakkil. The investigation concludes by showing how Sunni historians deliberately censored these atrocities, leaving the truth to be preserved in the steadfast poetry of the Shia and the foundational jurisprudence of scholars like Sheikh al-Mufid.
Segment 1: The First Paper - A Web of Fabrications and Lies

Sheikh al-Ghizzi opens the fifth episode with a recitation from the Ziyarat of Lady Fatima, emphasizing her titles as the oppressed, the usurped, the one whose rib was broken, and whose child was murdered. He then introduces the primary theme of the episode: the foundational layer of the Great Crime was built on lies and fabrications. He argues that the entire post-Saqifah era required a systematic distortion of truth—from altering the meaning of Ghadir Khumm, to inventing hadiths like "My Companions are like stars" and "We Prophets do not leave inheritance," to falsely claiming that Prophethood and Caliphate cannot exist in one household. He explains that one lie inevitably birthed another, culminating in the ultimate lie: the denial of Fatima's explicit rights.

Peace be upon Fatima, the Chosen (al-Mustafa), and Al-Murtada her husband, and the Infallibles from her children and her sons. Peace be upon the Fatimids in every time and place, from her offspring, her Shia, and her lovers, and the mercy of Allah and His blessings.

I begin my speech in this episode with a segment from the Ziyarat of the Great Truthful Lady, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon her. And this is Mafatih al-Jinan, and a segment from one of her noble Ziyarats:

O Allah, send prayers upon Muhammad and his Household. And send prayers upon the Chaste, the Pure, the Truthful, the Infallible, the God-fearing, the Spotless, the Pleased, the Pleasing, the Virtuous, the Rightly-Guided, the Oppressed (al-Mazlumah), the Subjugated (al-Maqhurah), the one whose right was Usurped (al-Maghsubati haqquha)... the one whose inheritance was Denied, the one whose rib was Broken, the one whose husband was Oppressed, the one whose child was Murdered!

"The one whose child was murdered" is a reference to Muhsin here. Otherwise, all the children of Fatima from the Infallibles have been murdered, except the Pure Remnant, the Imam of our Time, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him.

The Fatimi File... what is the first paper of its papers? **The first paper of the papers of the Fatimi File, if I wanted to title it, its title is: Fabrications and Lies!** As he, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, said: "Sayings will multiply after me." Fabrications and lies against the Messenger of Allah, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family.

First: distorting the meaning of the Allegiance of Ghadir. Second: fabricating this meaning, "My Ummah does not unite upon an error." And the question is: Is the Ummah infallible? The axiomatic answer is: No! ... Another fabrication: "We the assembly of Prophets are not inherited from," or "do not leave inheritance." A clear fabrication in opposition to the Book [the Quran] and in opposition to the Messenger of Allah. "Prophethood and Caliphate do not gather in one house," to other than that of the fabrications, sayings, and falsehoods.

From here began the first lines of the Fatimi File. It began from fabrications, distortions, and lies... **And lying, if it occurs, gives birth to another lie!** And so on in a continuous chain of lies; lying gives birth... until it filled the horizons of the Easts and the Wests in the world of truth! And until the facts were lost, the facts were obliterated, and the facts were distorted!
Segment 2: Fadak - The Divine Gift and the Torn Deed

Sheikh al-Ghizzi transitions to the second direction of the episode: the issue of Fadak. Rather than delving into exhaustive historical and jurisprudential debates, he relies on Imam Ali's definitive statement in Nahj al-Balaghah that Fadak was in their hands, but "the souls of a people coveted it." Using texts from Awalim al-Ulum, the Sheikh details how Fadak was acquired peacefully, making it the exclusive property of the Prophet. Following the divine command to "Give the relative his right," the Prophet summoned Imam Ali to write a formal deed (Kitab) transferring Fadak to Fatima. The Sheikh reiterates the tragic fate of this document: it was forcibly taken from Fatima's hands, spat upon, and torn apart.

The second direction that I wanted to point to is a quick pass over the issue of Fadak... I believe that the issue of Fadak is clear among the clear things, and clear things do not require us to pause long at them. The word of the Master of Legatees, a brief word which came in his letter to Uthman ibn Hunayf, his governor over Basra:
Yes, Fadak was in our hands from all that the sky shaded, so the souls of a people coveted it, and the souls of another people generously let go of it.

This word is sufficient for us, and we do not need prolonging or broadening. But despite that, I pause at the issue of Fadak without entering into many details... I began my speech in the first direction [about] lies and fabrications, and among the instances of these lies and fabrications is the issue of Fadak.

Some narrations and hadiths passed us by in the previous episodes pointing to the fact that the Messenger of Allah... had written a deed (Kitab) for al-Zahra regarding Fadak. But this deed was spat upon... We pass over this narration. The narration is from our Imam al-Baqir, transmitted here from I'lam al-Wara by Sheikh al-Tabarsi. He says: Al-Baqir, peace be upon him, said:

So he made a treaty with them that he would spare their blood. So the orchards of Fadak became purely and exclusively for the Messenger of Allah, because no horses or camels were spurred for it. So Gabriel descended and said: 'Indeed, Allah, Mighty and Majestic, commands you to give the relative his right (Wa ati dhal-qurba haqqahu).' So he said: 'O Gabriel, and who are my relatives, and what is her right?' He said: 'Fatima.' ... So the Messenger of Allah summoned Fatima, and wrote a deed for her!

This is another narration confirming the meanings of the previous narrations that spoke about a written deed from the Messenger of Allah. And the strange thing is, many and numerous narrations speak about a deed from the Messenger of Allah, but this deed is not mentioned! **What is mentioned is the deed that Abu Bakr wrote, and which Umar took from her hand, and kicked her in her side... and to which she said to him: "You tore my deed, may Allah tear your belly!"**

A second narration also in the same source... transmitted from the book Al-Khara'ij wa al-Jara'ih... "So he called for Adim 'Ukazi (a piece of tanned leather from 'Ukaz) to write a deed upon it... and he summoned Ali ibn Abi Talib and said: 'Write for Fatima a gift from the Messenger of Allah.'"
The texts are clear: exclusive to the Messenger of Allah, he gifted it to Fatima, and he wrote a deed for her regarding that.
Segment 3: The Fadakiyyah Sermon and the Quranic Defense

Sheikh al-Ghizzi reads powerful excerpts from Lady Fatima's historic Fadakiyyah Sermon, where she publicly dismantled Abu Bakr's fabricated hadith ("We Prophets do not leave inheritance"). Using a barrage of explicit Quranic verses regarding the inheritance of Prophets Solomon, David, and Zechariah, she exposed the glaring contradiction in the Caliph's claim. The Sheikh highlights her brilliant, devastating argument: either Abu Bakr is claiming to know the Quran better than the Prophet and Imam Ali, or he is outright abandoning the Book of Allah to usurp her rights. She concludes with a terrifying warning that they will meet at the Resurrection, where Allah is the Judge and Muhammad is the claimant against him.
Therefore, Al-Zahra, peace be upon her, in her sermon... I excerpt a segment from her sermon while she addresses the Companions, the companions of her father. She says:

O Muslims! Will I be overcome regarding my inheritance?! This is Fatima the daughter of Muhammad, and Muhammad just bade you farewell two or three days ago! O son of Abu Quhafah [Abu Bakr]! Is it in the Book of Allah that you inherit from your father, and I do not inherit from my father?! You have surely brought a monstrous thing!
Al-Zahra continues, directly challenging his fabrication with the words of Allah:

Did you intentionally abandon the Book of Allah and cast it behind your backs? When it says: 'And Solomon inherited David.' And He said regarding what He narrated of the story of Yahya bin Zakariya when he said: 'So grant me from Yourself an heir, who will inherit me and inherit from the family of Jacob.' And He said: 'And blood relatives are more entitled to one another in the Book of Allah.'

All these verses explicitly state this clear, manifest meaning: that this hadith [Abu Bakr's claim] contradicts the Book of Allah! "We the assembly of Prophets are not inherited from"... this hadith is intended to mean the issue of financial inheritance! Preventing financial inheritance! And preventing financial inheritance contradicts these verses.
She continues with her devastating argument:

And you claimed that I have no status and no inheritance from my father, and there is no kinship between us! Did Allah single you out with a verse from which He excluded my father?! Or do you say that the people of two different religions do not inherit from one another? Am I and my father not from the people of one religion?! Or are you more knowledgeable in the specifics and generalities of the Quran than my father and my cousin?!
And her terrifying conclusion:

So take it (Fadunakaha), haltered and saddled! It will meet you on the Day of your Gathering! For what an excellent Judge is Allah, and the claimant is Muhammad! And the rendezvous is the Resurrection! And at the Hour, the falsifiers will lose!
And the speech of Fatima continues in her sermon when Abu Bakr emphasizes the issue of inheritance... So Fatima said:

Glory be to Allah! My father, the Messenger of Allah, was never one to turn away from the Book of Allah, nor opposed to its rulings! Rather, he used to follow its traces and pursue its chapters. Do you combine treachery with fabricating falsehood against him?!
Segment 4: The Political Hypocrisy and Aisha's Demand for Inheritance

She said to him: 'Then give me my inheritance from the Messenger of Allah!' So he said to her: 'Did you and Malik ibn Aws al-Nasri not come and testify that the Messenger of Allah does not leave inheritance?! Until you both prevented Fatima her inheritance, and invalidated her right!'

Because she was among the witnesses—Aisha—who testified that the Messenger of Allah had said this statement. **So how do you demand today an inheritance from the Prophet?! You are the one who testified that the Prophet said he leaves no inheritance**, and Fatima was prevented due to your testimony and the testimony of those who testified with you!
We have a narration transmitted by Al-Fadl ibn Shadhan. That after Aisha came, what did Uthman say to her? ... He said:

Are you not the two who testified before Abu Bakr... that the Prophet said: 'We the assembly of Prophets do not leave inheritance, what we leave is charity.' If you testified to the truth, then you have invalidated your own claim against yourselves! And if you both testified falsely, then upon the one who testifies falsely is the curse of Allah, the angels, and all people!

So she said to him: "O Na'thal! By Allah, you have resembled the Messenger of Allah to Na'thal the Jew!" So he said to them both: "Allah presents an example of those who disbelieved: the wife of Noah and the wife of Lot." So they both went out from his presence.
Segment 5: The Symbolic Borders of Fadak and the Imamate

Meaning, Fadak is a very important paper in the Fatimi File! **And Fadak is not an orchard; it is the Imamate, it is the Caliphate, it is the Islamic government! Fadak is a title and a symbol.**

So Abu Bakr was silenced. Then Umar said: "O Ali, leave us from your speech! For we cannot match your argumentation. So if you bring just witnesses, otherwise it is spoils (Fay') for the Muslims; neither you nor Fatima have any right to it."

So the Commander of the Believers said: "O Abu Bakr, do you read the Book of Allah? ... Tell me about the saying of Allah, Mighty and Majestic: Indeed, Allah only intends to keep away the impurity from you, O People of the House, and to purify you with a thorough purification. Regarding whom was it revealed? Regarding us or regarding other than us?" He said: "Rather, regarding you."

He said: "So if witnesses testified against Fatima the daughter of the Messenger of Allah with an immorality (Fahishah), what would you do with her?" He said: "I would establish the legal punishment (Hadd) upon her, just as I would establish it upon the women of the Muslims!"

He said: 'Then you would be, before Allah, among the disbelievers!' He said: 'And why?' He said: 'Because you would have rejected the testimony of Allah for her regarding purity, and accepted the testimony of people against her!'
So the people grumbled, and they objected, and looked at one another, and said: "By Allah, Ali ibn Abi Talib has spoken the truth!" And he returned to his home.

After that, the report mentions... "So Abu Bakr sent for Umar and summoned him. Then he said to him: 'Did you see the gathering of Ali with us on this day? By Allah, if we sit another sitting like it, our affair will be ruined for us! So what is the view?'"

So Umar said: 'The view is that you order his killing.' He said: 'So who will kill him?' He said: 'Khalid ibn al-Walid.' ... Abu Bakr said: 'Attend the mosque. Stand beside him in the prayer. When I say the Salam (the conclusion of the prayer), strike his neck!'

Asma bint Umays heard this... She said to her maidservant: "Go to the house of Ali and Fatima and convey my greetings to them both. And say to Ali: Indeed, the chiefs are conferring to kill you, so leave [the city]; indeed, I am to you of the sincere advisors."

So she came, and the Commander of the Believers said to her: "Indeed, Allah will stand between them and what they intend." Then he stood up, prepared for prayer, attended the mosque, and prayed behind Abu Bakr. And Khalid ibn al-Walid prayed beside him, and with him was the sword. So when Abu Bakr sat for the Tashahhud... he regretted what he had said, and feared civil strife, and recognized the severity of Ali and his might!

So he remained pondering, not daring to say the Salam, until the people thought he had forgotten. Then he turned to Khalid and said: 'O Khalid, do not do what I ordered you! Peace be upon you, and the mercy of Allah and His blessings.'

So the Commander of the Believers said: "O Khalid, what did he order you with?" He said: "He ordered me to strike your neck." He said: "Would you have done it?" He said: "Yes, by Allah, had he not said to me do not kill him before the Salam, I would have killed you!"

He said: **So Ali grabbed him and slammed him into the ground!** So the people gathered upon him... So the people said: "O Abu al-Hassan! Allah, Allah, by the right of the owner of the grave [the Prophet]!" So he let him go. Then he turned to Umar, grabbed him by his collar, and said: "O son of Sahhak! By Allah, were it not for a covenant from the Messenger of Allah, and a Book from Allah that preceded, you would have known which of us is weaker in supporters and fewer in number!"

Also, Sheikh al-Saduq narrated this report in more than one source. And this is the book **Al-Khisal**. In the Chapter of the Seven, Sheikh al-Saduq brings a narration... "And as for slandering the chaste woman, indeed they slandered Fatima, peace be upon her, on their pulpits!"

These texts, what do they say? They say Fatima was slandered on their pulpits! How was Fatima slandered on their pulpits? The slanderer (Qadhif) is the fabricator. You fabricate by mentioning immoralities (Fawahish), by mentioning bad deeds, by mentioning major sins, whatever they may be... Fatima the pure, the purified! **This is another page from the pages of the Fatimi File that are folded away and not published!** This is the speech of the Imams, and these are the most reliable sources.

The issue began from fabrications, when Fatima was belied in her claim that Fadak was hers. And this is the strongest slander! The Prophet... when he was asked: "Does the believer commit adultery?" This is the speech of the Caliph of the Muslims, the "Commander of the Believers" Abu Ja'far al-Mansur! This is his speech.

As for Al-Mutawakkil... Al-Mutawakkil's story is long in his enmity to the Ahlulbayt, and in his enmity to al-Zahra. I pause here with Al-Mutawakkil for a bit, in order to clarify a truth among the truths, which is the truth of distortion (Tahrif).

But when the speech reaches the cursing and slandering of Fatima, he summarizes the speech in this way! **Why are the facts hidden? Why is the oppression of Fatima and the Ahlulbayt always hidden and lost?!**

In the book **Al-Kamil fi al-Tarikh** by Ibn al-Athir... "And Al-Mutawakkil had an intense hatred for Ali ibn Abi Talib, peace be upon him, and his Ahlulbayt! And he would target anyone he heard of who allied with Ali and his family by taking their wealth and their blood!"

And among his drinking companions was Ubadah the effeminate (Al-Mukhannath). He used to tie a pillow on his stomach under his clothes, uncover his bald head, and dance before Al-Mutawakkil while the singers sang: 'The bald, large-bellied one has come, the Caliph of the Muslims!'

In his gatherings of drunkenness and rowdiness, thus he mocked Ali! "He did that one day while Al-Muntasir was present. So Al-Muntasir gestured to Ubadah, threatening him, so he fell silent out of fear of him." So Al-Muntasir said:

O Commander of the Believers! Indeed, the one whom this dog Ubadah is mocking, and from whom the people are laughing, is your cousin, and the elder of your household, and through him is your pride! So eat his flesh yourself if you wish, but do not feed this dog and his likes from it!

So Al-Mutawakkil said to the singers: "Sing all together: The young man is jealous for his cousin; the head of the young man is in his mother's private parts!" These are the palaces of the Caliphs of the Muslims!

I point here to examples of the poetry of those who used to attend the gathering of Al-Mutawakkil and his likes. I will mention the poetry said by the Shia poets, among them Al-Hussein ibn Al-Hajjaj, in response to them... He addresses Ali:
O owner of the white dome in Najaf, Whoever visits your grave and seeks healing there, is healed!
And in another poem addressing Al-Hashimi (Ibn Sukkara), what does Al-Hussein ibn Al-Hajjaj say?

So you found no cure to benefit yourself with, Except by seeking to attack the daughter of Yasin [the Prophet]! For your speech regarding Al-Zahra Fatima, Is the speech of a man infatuated with Nasb (enmity to the Ahlulbayt)! The Chief of Women tomorrow in the Gathering, will be served, By the people of Paradise with the wide-eyed houris.

This is the meaning to which the words of the Imams pointed: that they slandered al-Zahra on their pulpits! And in their gatherings of drunkenness, and among their drinking companions, and they said what they said! And this is a small page from the oppression of Fatima.
I said one day in an intimate converse (Munajah) with Fatima, these are lines from it I wrote in days past. I say:

O Mother of Al-Hassan! O Mother of Al-Hussein! And I return, O Mother of the Pure Ones, Letting my conscience speak! I might lie in the claim of truth, For I know myself, I love burdens. I might go east, or go west, I am a sinful human, swept by all desires... I might lie in all things... Except in my love for you, O Mother of the Pure Ones! For it has my heart, and my mind, and in it is all my life!

This is my hope in you, O Mother of Al-Mahdi! O Fatima al-Zahra! O daughter of the Messenger of Allah! O Mother of Al-Hassan and Al-Hussein! Aid me and reach me, prayers of Allah be upon you. I am a friend to whoever allies with you, and an enemy to whoever opposes you, O piece of Muhammad, and his soul that is between his sides!

And to you, O Fatimids, continuous peace between us and you. **Al-Hujjah ibn Al-Hassan al-Askari, the Imam of our Time, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him, is the heart of Fatima, the core of the heart of Fatima**. I ask you all for supplication. In the safety of Allah.

title: The File of Fatima - Episode 6: The Personality of Umar ibn al-Khattab
speaker: Sheikh Abdul-Halim al-Ghizzi

content.text: Overview: Episode 6

In the sixth episode of the File of Fatima, Sheikh al-Ghizzi applies a profound epistemological principle—knowing a person's true nature through **prolonged association (Mu'asharah)**—to the primary architect of the assault on Lady Fatima: Umar ibn al-Khattab. Relying almost entirely on authentic Sunni historical and hadith sources, the Sheikh meticulously builds a psychological and behavioral profile of the Caliph. The investigation uncovers a deeply ingrained, lifelong pattern of **extreme physical violence, unyielding harshness, and a blatant disregard for the sanctity of private homes and women**. From his aggressive disrespect toward the Prophet and the Companions, to his violent suppression of mourning women and his terrifying midnight beatings, the evidence demonstrates that his brutal attack on the house of Revelation was not an anomaly, but the ultimate manifestation of his established character. The episode concludes by contrasting this state-sponsored cruelty with the profound theological sorrow captured in the elegies of the great Shia scholars, warning against those who attempt to deny the historical reality of the oppression of the Prophet's daughter.
Segment 1: The Epistemology of Character - Knowing Men Through Action

Sheikh al-Ghizzi opens the sixth episode by addressing a profound epistemological question: How do we truly know a person's character? He explains that the most reliable method—recognized by both Islamic jurisprudence and natural human psychology—is prolonged association (Mu'asharah). Through repetitive observation of a person's actions, their true nature moves from theoretical observation to absolute certainty (Badihiyyat). He uses the universally accepted courage of Imam Ali as an example of "meaning-based consensus" (Tawatur Ma'nawi), where countless diverse reports all point to the exact same truth. The Sheikh states that this epistemological framework is essential for understanding the primary figure responsible for the Great Crime against Fatima: Umar ibn al-Khattab.

Peace be upon Fatima, the Chosen (al-Mustafa), and Al-Murtada after her, and the Infallibles from her children and her sons. Peace be upon the Fatimids in every time and place, from her offspring, her Shia, and her lovers, and the mercy of Allah and His blessings.

The Fatimi File, Episode Six. How are we able to know a human being? When we want to know a person, there are several methods to know that person whom we want to know. **Among these methods, and the best of methods, is association ('Ishrah / Mu'asharah)!** Living with the person closely makes us know that person through this method more than the other methods.

You read, O believers, in the jurisprudential books and in the manuals of practical law, that among the methods and means of knowing the justice ('Adalah) of a Marja' of emulation, for example, or knowing the justice of the Imam of congregational prayer... one of these methods to know the hidden realities of people and their natures is association. When you associate with a person and accompany him in travel and in residence, in hardship and in ease... then you will be knowledgeable of the natures of that person.

When we know a truthful person through association, you associate with a person and reach the conclusion that he is truthful. How did you reach this conclusion? What is the mechanism present in association? What is the secret that association reveals the truth of this human being to us?

There is no strange secret in association. The pictures simply repeat. Now, how do we attain axiomatic truths (Badihiyyat)? ... People know that the sun in the morning rises from the east. And this is an axiomatic issue that does not require study, and does not require thought. **How did this information become axiomatic? It is repetition!** Since a human opens his eyes to this world, he sees that the sun every morning emerges from the east. The repetition of the picture leads to transforming it into an axiom.

And this is the secret, this is the mechanism present in association. When we associate with a person, we conclude that he is trustworthy, that he is truthful, that he is stingy, that he is generous... Do we have, for example, a microscope? Did we perform an anatomy on this human being? Never! The issue is the repetition of the picture. The picture of truthfulness repeats from this man, and we arrive at this definitive conclusion.

This exact same process is what is called **Meaning-Based Consensus (Tawatur Ma'nawi)**. The scholars agree... there is an agreement that Tawatur is a definitive matter. And Tawatur is of two types: that which is verbal (Lafthi), and that which is meaning-based (Ma'nawi).

**There is no doubt among anyone of the Muslims that the bravest of people in the time of the Messenger of Allah was Ali!** The courage of Ali is unquestionable. When we want to discuss the courage of Ali from this aspect... is it placed in the category of conjecture (Zanniyyat) or in the category of certainties (Yaqiniyyat)? The courage of Ali is placed in the category of certainties! Why? Because it is from the known consensus.

There are different incidents, but they ultimately point to one meaning! And this is what is called meaning-based consensus. When history speaks to us... about the courage of Ali, for example, on the night of sleeping in the bed of the Messenger of Allah; about the courage of Ali in his migration when he brought out the Fatimas in broad daylight; about the courage of Ali in Badr, and in Uhud, in Al-Ahzab, in Khaybar, and so on. These repeated transmissions, and from different people, for one meaning and one content, lead to the attainment of certainty!
Segment 2: The Aggression Against the Prophet - Sahih al-Bukhari and Tafsir al-Tabari

Sheikh al-Ghizzi transitions from the epistemological concept of "meaning-based consensus" to examining the established character of Umar ibn al-Khattab. He sets out to prove through Sunni sources that Umar's violent assault on Fatima was entirely consistent with his well-documented history of aggression—even toward the Prophet himself. He cites Sahih al-Bukhari, where Umar violently yanked the Prophet's shirt to stop him from praying over Abdullah ibn Ubayy, leaving a physical mark on the Prophet's neck. He then cites Tafsir al-Tabari and Hilyat al-Awliya', detailing an incident where Umar, irritated by a Quranic reminder, smashed a date branch on the ground, sending dates flying directly into the Prophet's face.

In the past episodes, the details of the crime passed upon us. And how the assault upon the daughter of Muhammad, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, happened, and who were the champions of this assault. **And the first repeated name is Caliph Umar.** The Caliph Umar ibn al-Khattab is the repeated name who was the champion of this assault.

Let us cast a look at the nature of Umar ibn al-Khattab. And I here do not want to be exhaustive, and I will rely on the books of the people, on the books of the opponents, to determine the specifications and nature of the personality... Because some might say—and it is a saying we do not believe stems from good intention—that it is unlikely for Caliph Umar to do what he did of striking al-Zahra, assaulting her, kicking her, whipping her, and other than that.

**The personality was known for harshness, cruelty, and roughness!** And these sayings, it is not I who say them; rather the books of the people testify to that. Therefore, I will wander among the books of hadith, and the books of history and biographies, to draw a picture of the nature of this man.
This is **Sahih al-Bukhari**... Hadith number 1269. The text of the hadith: From Ibn Umar [Abdullah ibn Umar]:

When Abdullah ibn Ubayy died, his son came to the Prophet... He said: 'O Messenger of Allah, give me your shirt so I may shroud him in it, and pray over him, and seek forgiveness for him.' So the Prophet gave him his shirt. ... When the Prophet wanted to pray over him... Umar, may Allah be pleased with him, pulled him (Jadhabah)! Umar pulled him and said: 'Has not Allah forbidden you from praying over the hypocrites?!'

The place of evidence here is this behavior with the Messenger of Allah! And there is much of the likes of this behavior with the Messenger of Allah. **He pulled the Prophet, shouted at the Prophet, and acted with bad manners!** As if the Prophet, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, did not know that! And as if the Prophet was opposing the command of Allah, and Umar was more careful than the Prophet about the command of Allah!

And in some books, this incident is mentioned with more detail: **that he pulled him from his shirt until it left a mark on the body of the Prophet from the force of the pull!** This is the dealing with the Messenger of Allah, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family.
Another place. This is **Tafsir al-Tabari**. And this is the thirtieth volume... in Surah al-Takathur. When the speech comes to the verse: "Then you will surely be asked that Day about pleasure."

So the Messenger of Allah and his companions ate, then he called for water, and drank cold water. So he said: 'You will surely be asked about this on the Day of Resurrection.' ... So Umar took the branch, and it had dates on it, and struck the ground with it in front of the Messenger of Allah! Until the unripe dates scattered. Then he said: 'O Messenger of Allah! Are we going to be asked about this?!'

Of course, this narration present here [in al-Tabari] has a phrase deleted from it! The phrase is present in Hilyat al-Awliya' by Abu Nu'aym... I checked Hilyat al-Awliya' and found the narration with another text: **"He struck the ground with it until the unripe dates scattered towards the face of the Messenger of Allah!"** This phrase is deleted here from Tafsir al-Tabari.

Is this not a clear bad manners?! He takes the branch and strikes the ground with it! ... So if he deals with the Messenger of Allah, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, in this way... then what do you think of how he deals with others?!
Segment 3: The Companions' Verdict - A Harsh and Rough Ruler

Sheikh al-Ghizzi shifts to how the senior Companions viewed Umar, noting that their perspective was built on years of direct association (Mu'asharah). Reading from the prominent Sunni hadith collection Kanz al-'Ummal, he details the events surrounding Abu Bakr's deathbed appointment of Umar as the next Caliph. Senior Companions explicitly objected, confronting Abu Bakr and demanding to know how he would answer to Allah for giving authority to a man notoriously "harsh and rough" (Fazzan Ghalizan). The Sheikh points out the logical conclusion of the Companions: if Umar was already brutal without total power, his cruelty would only magnify once he became the absolute ruler.

Let us take a look at the view of the Companions regarding Umar ibn al-Khattab. And definitely, the view they have is the result of association (Mu'asharah); they associated with Umar and lived with him, and they know him. They lived with him, some of them before Islam, and lived with him after Islam in Mecca and in Medina... So they know him and know his nature.

This is **Kanz al-'Ummal** by Al-Muttaqi al-Hindi... the fifth volume, page 269, hadith 14,171. The point of evidence: "And some of the companions of the Prophet heard of Abd al-Rahman's entering... and Uthman's entering upon Abu Bakr and being alone with him in his final days of his life."
So a speaker among them said: 'What will you say to your Lord when He asks you about your appointing Umar over us?! While you see his harshness (Ghilzatah)!'

This is the speech of the Companions, the senior Companions, while they converse with Abu Bakr in the last moments of his life. Definitely, it is not from the common people; these are the senior Companions, his companions, the companions of Umar and the companions of Abu Bakr.
On page 270, the text numbered 14,174. From Zayd ibn al-Harith: "When death approached Abu Bakr, he sent for Umar to appoint him as successor. So the people said:"

You appoint over us Umar, harsh and rough (Fazzan Ghalizan)! And if he rules over us, he will be even harsher and rougher! What will you say to your Lord when you meet Him, having appointed Umar over us?!

The point of evidence here is the view of the Companions, and they are the senior Companions who enter upon Abu Bakr in the last moment of his life and discuss the matter of the Caliphate with him... The text is clear: "You appoint over us Umar, harsh and rough! And if he rules over us, he will be even harsher and rougher!" Considering the government will be in his hand.

**And the harsh, rough person cannot be of good morals after that if the absolute authority is in his hand!** The nature of the harsh, rough person, if he finds an opportunity, his harshness and roughness will increase. This is human nature, and I do not think two people would disagree on this issue.
Segment 4: The Broker, the Marketplace, and the Legacy of Coarseness

Sheikh al-Ghizzi examines Umar's profession as a marketplace broker (Dallal) and his rough treatment of people and animals. He recounts a story from Kanz al-'Ummal where Umar violently kicks a Bedouin's camels to test them, prompting the owner to call him an "evil man." Imam Ali has to intervene to resolve the dispute fairly. The Sheikh then reads another disturbing narration from the same source detailing how Umar's son, Abdullah, would physically grope and harass female slaves he intended to buy, arguing that this legacy of inappropriate and coarse behavior was inherited directly from his father.

For example, Umar ibn al-Khattab, if a questioner asks: What was his profession? He was a broker (Dallal)! And this is known in the books of biographies and in the books of history; he was a broker striving in sales transactions in the market set up in Baqi' al-Gharqad.
This is **Kanz al-'Ummal**... the fourth volume, page 58. From Anas ibn Malik: "A Bedouin came with his camels to sell them, so Umar came to him bargaining."

So Umar began to strike (Yanfus) them camel by camel! He struck each camel with his foot to make the camel stand up! ... So the Bedouin began to say: 'Leave it, may you have no father (Khalli la aba lak)!' But Umar would not be stopped by the Bedouin's words from doing that. Camel by camel! ... So the Bedouin said to Umar: 'I surely think you are an evil man (Rajul Su')!'

When he finished with them, he bought them... While they were disputing, Ali approached. Umar said: "Are you satisfied with this man between me and you?" The Bedouin said: "Yes." So he told Ali their story.

So Ali said: 'O Commander of the Believers! If you stipulated to him its saddle-cloths and wooden saddles, they are yours. As you stipulated. Otherwise, a man adorns his merchandise with more than its price.' So he took off their saddle-cloths and wooden saddles, the Bedouin drove them away, and Umar paid him the price.

The point of evidence here is in the method of dealing. It is expected of a broker to have beautiful speech, good manners, and a certain taste... But you notice the story! How he deals with the animals and the owner of the animals, such that the owner of the animal starts saying: "Leave them, may you have no father! I don't want you to buy from me! What are you doing?!"
It seems that Abdullah ibn Umar ibn al-Khattab took his method from his father! In the exact same place, page 59, hadith 9911. From Nafi':

When Ibn Umar ibn al-Khattab (meaning Abdullah) wanted to buy a slave girl, and agreed with them on a price, he would place his hand on her buttocks, her belly, and her private parts (Qubuliha)... and he would uncover her leg!
When he wanted to buy a slave girl, this is what he used to do! Meaning, this rough behavior he took from his father, just as Umar acted in his dealing with this Bedouin and with his camels.
Segment 5: Violence Against Trees - The Tree of Allegiance and the Tree of Mourning

Sheikh al-Ghizzi reads from Ibn Hajar's Fath al-Bari to show that Umar's roughness extended even to inanimate objects of immense historical and emotional value. When Umar learned that people were praying near the Tree of Allegiance (where the companions had pledged loyalty to the Prophet), he threatened them and ordered the sacred tree chopped down. The Sheikh draws a direct parallel to a deeply painful event for the Shia: when Lady Fatima was forced out of Medina to mourn her father, she sought refuge under an Arak tree in Baqi'. Umar maliciously cut down that tree as well, denying her even the shade to weep.

And the matter goes even further than that. This is **Fath al-Bari fi Sharh Sahih al-Bukhari** by Ibn Hajar... This is the seventh volume. Page 361, in the saying of Ibn Hajar while he speaks about the tree at which the known Allegiance of the Tree occurred... What does Ibn Hajar say?

With an authentic chain from Nafi': It reached Umar that a group of people were coming to the tree... praying at it. So he threatened them (Tawa'adahum)! ... then ordered it to be cut down, so it was cut down!

He cut the tree that the Muslims used to seek blessings from, and remember the Messenger of Allah, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, by! **And that is not far from the Arak tree which Fatima, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon her, used to shade herself with!**

After they demanded of Fatima not to remain in her house receiving the women who came to console her and share in her tragedy... So they said to Ali: "Either Fatima remains silent, or if she wants to weep, she must leave the house!" ... And she used to go out to an area near Al-Baqi'. And there was an Arak tree there. So she would shade herself with this tree with Al-Hassan and Al-Hussein and Zaynab... **So Umar stood up and cut down the Arak tree!**
And the cutters of the Arak tree! Sheikh Salih al-Kawwaz, may Allah the Exalted's pleasure be upon him, says:
And the cutters of an Arak tree, so she would not find shade, Under its leaves and branches!

In this exact same direction that he cut the tree where the pledge to the Messenger of Allah took place, he cut the Arak tree where Fatima sat! But it is violence even with trees! Violence with the tree of the Messenger of Allah! Just as there was violence with the Messenger of Allah! Just as there was violence with the Companions!
Segment 6: The Invasion of Privacy and the Poetic Confession

Sheikh al-Ghizzi highlights a well-known historical incident to demonstrate that invading private homes without permission was a normalized behavior for Umar. While roaming Medina at night, Umar heard men drinking wine, scaled their wall, and broke into their home. However, the men humiliated him using the Quran, pointing out that while they committed one sin, Umar had committed three: spying, climbing over walls instead of using doors, and entering without permission. The Sheikh quotes the famous Egyptian poet Hafez Ibrahim, who ironically recorded this severe breach of Islamic law as a "virtue" in his Al-Umariyyah poem. The Sheikh concludes that for a man accustomed to scaling walls and raiding homes, violently attacking the house of Fatima was entirely within his established character.

And a known incident... this incident is mentioned in numerous sources, and perhaps many of you have heard of it. When Umar was out one night, roaming the streets and alleys of Medina, he heard a sound. He heard something that aroused suspicion in him in one of the houses, **so he scaled the wall of the house (Tasawwara)!**

Meaning this issue of scaling walls into houses, and raiding houses, is an issue that passed upon us... So he scaled the wall into the house of those people, and they were drinking wine. And an argumentation took place between Umar and those people.

So Umar said to this man... 'O enemy of Allah! Did you think that Allah would conceal you while you are upon His disobedience?!' So he said: 'O Commander of the Believers! Do not rush. If I disobeyed Allah in one thing, you have disobeyed Allah in three! He said: And do not spy, and you spied! And He said: And enter houses from their doors, and you scaled the wall! And He said: Do not enter houses other than your own until you ascertain welcome and greet their inhabitants, and you entered my house without permission!'
Therefore, this meaning... the Egyptian poet Hafez Ibrahim in his Al-Umariyyah poem versifies this story in these verses. He says:
