Can you imagine with me this moment? A moment of bewilderment. A moment of pain, agony, and intense anxiety. When did this moment clear? **It was only moments, and suddenly, the neighing of the horse!**

The horse approached, and the children ran out, expecting that Al-Hussein had returned to bid them a third farewell. But suddenly, the horse was devoid of its rider! Covered in blood... Imam al-Baqir says: The horse was saying:
The injustice! The injustice! The injustice! The injustice! From a nation that killed the son of the daughter of its Prophet!

My master, O Owner of the Time! O you continuous connection between the earth and the heaven! ... I adjure you, O son of Muhammad, by this moment! By the anxiety... by the anxiety of your aunt, the Lady [Zaynab]... do not deprive us of serving Al-Hussein! I ask you all for supplication.

title: The File of Ashura - Episode 6: The Husseini Incubator and the Cosmic Secret
speaker: Sheikh Abdul-Halim al-Ghizzi

content.text: Overview: Episode 6

In the sixth episode of the File of Ashura, Sheikh al-Ghizzi delves into the profound, unseen reality of Imam Hussein and the inescapable magnetic pull of the **Husseini incubator**. Moving beyond historical events, the Sheikh explores the deep cosmic philosophy of the "Arc of Ascent and Descent," explaining that Imam Hussein is the ultimate earthly manifestation of the Greatest Divine Image. By analyzing the stories of Prophet Adam and Prophet Noah, he demonstrates how the tragedy of Ashura was engineered into the very subconscious and **Fitrah** (innate nature) of humanity centuries before the event occurred. The episode reaches a devastating climax with the heart-wrenching accounts of the slaughter of Abdullah the infant and the young orphaned sons of Muslim ibn Aqil, illustrating the unparalleled cruelty of the Umayyad forces and cementing the absolute necessity of the Mahdawi vengeance.
Segment 1: The Luminous Bond with Al-Hussein and the Distortions of History

Sheikh al-Ghizzi opens the sixth episode of the File of Ashura with a profound poetic elegy expressing deep devotion and longing for Imam Hussein. The poem reflects on how political forces and historical distortions have attempted to paint the tragedy of Karbala with colors that suit their own oppressive agendas. The Sheikh explains that only by stripping away centuries of deception and approaching the pure, unadulterated reality of Imam Hussein can the true luminosity of his mission be understood. He concludes the introduction by invoking the heartbreaking physical sufferings of the Imam, specifically referencing the crushing of his teeth by the cane of Yazid, as mentioned in the Ziyarat of the Sacred Sphere (Ziyarat al-Nahiyah al-Muqaddasah).

May Allah magnify your rewards, and make excellent your mourning, and grant us and you success in serving Hussein and the family of Hussein. Enliven your gathering with prayers upon Muhammad and the family of Muhammad.

O Zahra... In the name of Allah, the Beneficent, the Merciful. Peace be upon the resting places of your sanctity, O Hussein. Peace be upon the brilliance of your purity. Peace be upon every valley and highland that leads me to you, So that from within the folds of the hearts, the light of your splendor and your beauty shines forth. Peace be upon your spilled blood. Peace be upon your dyed gray hair. Peace be upon your dust-covered cheek. Peace be upon you, O beloved... O most beloved of all beloveds, O Hussein.

I pictured your day in my mind... And returned your voice in my hearing. And I scrutinized your matter, I was not intimidated by the transmission of narrators, nor was I deceived. And I said, perhaps the echoing of the years... With its reverberations... Perhaps politics in what it committed against those attached to you, or claiming to be... A hand in dyeing the narrative of Al-Hussein with a color desired for it to be swallowed!

And when I removed the paint of the centuries, and the veil of deception from the deceived... I found you in a picture, O Abu Abdullah... You stormed my chest, just as the mountains of iron stormed on the day of Al-Taff... So you illuminated the dark parts of my thought, O Hussein, and straightened what was crooked in my ribs.

Peace be upon your parched lips. Peace be upon your sunken eyes. **Peace be upon your mouth, struck by the bamboo cane!** In some of the books of the Maqatil: "They broke the front teeth (Thanaya) of Al-Hussein." The Thanaya are these teeth that are at the front of the teeth. So how severe was the striking with the bamboo cane on the mouth of Al-Hussein? Otherwise, how could the teeth be broken?!

Therefore, our Imam, the Owner of the Command [Al-Mahdi], when he sends peace upon his grandfather in the Ziyarat... in the **Ziyarat of the Sacred Sphere** (Ziyarat al-Nahiyah al-Muqaddasah): "Peace be upon the mouth struck by the cane (Al-Qadhib)!"
Segment 2: The Meeting Point of the Two Arcs and the Divine Image

Sheikh al-Ghizzi resumes his explanation of the Husseini "incubator" from the previous episode, introducing its second unique aspect: the profound, cosmic reality of Imam Hussein himself. He quotes the profound poetry of Sheikh Muhammad Hussein al-Isfahani, describing the Imam as the ultimate convergence point of the "Arc of Descent" and the "Arc of Ascent" (the flow of divine grace down to creation, and creation's journey back to God). To explain this, the Sheikh uses the concept of the human as a "microcosm" reflecting the "macrocosm," and explains the profound hadith that "Allah created Adam in His image," referring not to a physical form, but to the Greatest Divine Image—the First Word, the Muhammadan Light. Imam Hussein is the most perfect earthly manifestation of this Divine Image. He concludes by quoting Imam al-Sadiq, revealing that Imam Hussein was not nursed by any woman, but fed directly from the Prophet, physically manifesting this cosmic reality in his very flesh and blood.

I return to my speech which I did not complete last night. And the speech is continuous in answering a question mentioned repeatedly: What is Ashura? And the speech was about a near goal, an intermediate goal, and a distant goal, which is the most complete and perfect goal: **the Mahdawi project.**

And I said that this incubator has two aspects of specificities. A specificity relating to us... and the second specificity which I promised to speak about tonight is the specificity of this incubator from the side of Al-Hussein, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him.
I will cite two verses from the poem Al-Anwar al-Qudsiyyah... by Sheikh Muhammad Hussein al-Isfahani. What does he say in the place where he speaks and composes poetry about the Master of Martyrs?
If the veil were lifted from you, you would not see / Other than him as a center and axis for it. And do you see for the meeting of the two arcs / A firmer dot than Al-Hussein?

He refers by the "two arcs" to a term known among the people of wisdom and the people of gnosis (Ma'rifah): **the Arc of Descent and the Arc of Ascent (Qaws al-Nuzul wa Qaws al-Su'ud).** "And do you see for the meeting of the two arcs... a firmer dot than Al-Hussein?" Al-Hussein is the dot at which the arcs of ascent and descent meet!

When the Prophet, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, informs us that **"Allah created Adam in His image,"** there is the Divine Image! "Allah created Adam in His image"... did He create him in the image of Adam? How is that possible? "Allah created Adam in His image," in the image of Allah! There is the Divine Image. The Divine Image is the Greatest Image, the intended meaning of it is the Muhammadan Reality, the intended meaning of it is the First Light!

I will bring the matter closer with another example. You and your personal ID card. What is your reality with the government? The reality of the human in state departments is a collection of papers. Where will you be in this card? Your picture! When they want to deal with you, they deal with this picture. So this picture... if I wanted to say that it is created, it is created upon your true image! It suits the passport. Is it possible for you to be personally present inside the passport? It is impossible! Because the nature of the passport is this limited size.

"Allah created Adam in His image." There is the Greatest Divine Image, which is the First Word. **"The first of what He created was them, then He created things from their lights."** And this meaning is clear in the words of the Ahlulbayt, and it is axiomatic to whoever is knowledgeable of the hadiths of the Household of Infallibility.

"If the veil were lifted from you, you would not see / Other than him as a center and axis for it." Because Al-Hussein is the summary of the Image! **Al-Hussein, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him, is the most perfect manifestation, the most beautiful manifestation of that Picture!**

A narration transmitted from our Imam al-Sadiq, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him, in **Kamil al-Ziyarat**. The Imam speaks about the nursing period of Al-Hussein. The Imam says: "Indeed, Al-Hussein was not breastfed by Fatima nor any female."

Rather, he used to be brought to the Messenger of Allah... so he would place his thumb in his mouth... and he would be satisfied by that for two or three days! And from here, the flesh of Al-Hussein grew from the flesh of the Messenger of Allah, and his blood from his blood!
Segment 3: The Fitrah and the Weeping of Adam

Sheikh al-Ghizzi explains that the Fitrah (innate nature) is the deepest seed of the human soul. To illustrate how this Husseini grief was cosmically engineered into the human subconscious, he recounts the story of Prophet Adam. When Gabriel taught Adam the Five Holy Names for his repentance, Adam’s heart broke and his eyes wept uncontrollably upon hearing the fifth name: Al-Hussein. Gabriel then prophesied Hussein's thirst and slaughter. This profound sorrow was permanently imprinted on Adam's subconscious and passed down to his spiritual descendants, embedding the tragedy of Ashura into the very DNA of humanity.

Al-Hussein, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him, is the gathering point of Mercy, and he is the gathering point of the Divine Beauty... And here I go to the hadiths in which our Prophet and the Imams, prayers of Allah be upon them all, inform us that **the human is born upon the Fitrah (innate nature)!**

When they ask Imam al-Sadiq about the meaning of the Fitrah, he said: **"The Fitrah is: There is no god but Allah, Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah, Ali is the Wali of Allah!"** The human creature is an image of that Great Image. And the Fitrah is the first seed in the formation of the human.

The human self has two layers: there is consciousness, and there is the layer of the subconscious (La-Shu'ur)... **And psychological inheritances only appear in the subconscious layer!** Most of the behaviors the human acts upon... stem from the subconscious. And the subconscious layer is an area where images gather, where influences gather, without the human paying attention to them.

Where do I want to reach? I want to reach this result. To which result? **I want to reach the Husseini influence in the human composition! In the origin of the human composition.** Perhaps the example will become clearer when I go to two segments from the history of humanity. Humans have two fathers. The first father is our father Adam, and the second father is our father Noah.
Adam descended to the earth, and there was the repentance... When Gabriel descended with the Five Names. Adam asked Gabriel, he said:

My brother Gabriel! What is it with me that whenever I mention the Fifth Name, my heart breaks? What is it with me that whenever I mention the Fifth Name, my eyes shed tears? What is it with me that whenever I mention the Fifth Name, sorrow strikes me?

You notice this segment? It is a very important segment in the life of Adam. The subconscious layers capture and are influenced by exceptional events... Normal circumstances do not influence the fetus. But if she is put under intense pressure... these exceptional circumstances leave their effect on the subconscious layer in the fetus!

Adam, peace be upon him, in this moment, in the moment of repentance... **When the door of relief was opened for him by these Five Names, when the Fifth Name passes, his state turns upside down!** Gabriel informs him:

The owner of the Fifth Name will be killed thirsty! And there will be between him and the sky, from the intensity of the thirst, something like smoke from the intensity of the thirst. ... and he will be slaughtered from the nape.

**The Master of Martyrs was slaughtered while he was alive!** The rest of the martyrs, after they died, their heads were severed. But Al-Hussein's head was severed while he was alive! And he was severed from the nape!

This narration and its likes are symbols speaking about the enfolding of the human self upon this meaning! Even if it were a historical issue, this immense sorrow that strikes him at the Fifth Name... this is imprinted in Adam, and when it is imprinted in Adam, it transfers to his sons!
Segment 4: The Trials of Noah and the Five Nails of the Ark

The Sheikh transitions to humanity's "second father," Prophet Noah. He details the horrific physical torture Noah and his followers endured for centuries, and the repeated tests of patience they faced before the Flood. When the terrifying, mountain-like waves of the Flood finally arrived, the Ark was saved not by its wood, but by the Five Nails brought by Gabriel. When Noah struck the fifth nail, it bled—another cosmic imprint of Al-Hussein's tragedy onto the subconscious of the surviving believers, embedding this sorrow deep within the human psyche.

When we move to the stage of the second fatherhood, to the stage of Noah, peace be upon him. Our Imam al-Sadiq tells us: The first three hundred years, Noah suffered with his people! "And his companions were few. **They used to beat him until he fainted! And he would remain thrown in the streets for three days, blood flowing from his ears!**" If they were doing this to the Prophet, what were they doing to his followers?!

He remained with them in this state for three hundred years... When the 900 years were completed... He prayed against his people, and he prayed for relief (Faraj). The angels descended upon Prophet Noah and said to him: "O Prophet of Allah, take this date seed (Nawa). Take this date seed and give it to your companions, let them plant it, until it bears fruit and the dates come out, the relief will come."

The point of evidence here is that the companions of Noah were eighty in number, those who boarded the Ark! Therefore it is said about Prophet Noah that he is the second father of humanity. You imagine what the psychological state of these people was! This long number of years, and these trials, then came the flood. The flood was terrifying!
And it sailed with them through waves like mountains!

The rain was pouring from the sky with pouring water! And where did the beginning start? "The oven boiled over (Fara al-Tannur)." The oven, which is the place of fire, the oven in which bread is baked. If the oven boils over, that was the sign for Noah: that if the oven boils over... you all run to the Ark, board it!

The Ark. This group, which went through all those exceptional circumstances, and after that the Ark is saved. And the question: How was the Ark of Noah saved? The answer in our narrations, in the Bihar of Sheikh al-Majlisi and others: **Gabriel descended with the Five Nails!** And these Five Nails which Noah hammered and struck into his Ark were the reason for its salvation!

**And when he struck the Fifth Nail, a fluid came out of it!** So Noah asked Gabriel... Noah asks Gabriel: "What is this?" He speaks to him with the story of Al-Hussein, peace be upon him! And those are symbols! Those are symbols speaking about the enfolding of the human self upon this meaning! ... The companions of Noah, these events will leave their impact in the subconscious layer!
Segment 5: The Hidden Secret of the Husseini Pull and the Cosmic Curse

Sheikh al-Ghizzi explores the inexplicable, magnetic pull that Imam Hussein exerts over millions of people worldwide, noting that it defies rational explanation. He quotes a hadith from the Prophet stating that Al-Hussein possesses a "hidden knowledge" within the hearts of believers. He also shares Lady Fatima's account of her pregnancy, during which the unborn Hussein acted as a guiding light and emanated the glorification of Allah from her womb. The Sheikh concludes this section by contrasting the followers of the Husseini incubator with those outside of it, quoting Imam al-Sadiq to show that the heavens themselves cursed the Ummah that slaughtered Hussein, rejecting their prayers and fasting until his vengeance is fulfilled.

And this is what I meant in the past nights when I said that **there is an attractive force in Al-Hussein! This force attracts souls to him.** And therefore, this is the most important characteristic in this incubator... You find there are groups of people ready to offer everything for Al-Hussein! And they feel that they have offered nothing. And if you ask them why, he doesn't know!

**There is something present that I do not know, and you also do not know! There is a secret!** And this secret, it is not I who calls it a secret; Muhammad Al-Mustafa, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, calls it a secret!
The traditionist Al-Karajaki... in his book Al-Khara'ij wa al-Jara'ih, transmits a very beautiful narration. Al-Miqdad asks the Prophet about Al-Hussein. What does the Prophet... say? He says to him:
Indeed, for Al-Hussein in the inner beings of the believers, there is a hidden knowledge (Ma'rifah Maktumah)!
Miqdad goes to the Mother of Al-Hasan and Al-Hussein, so he asks Fatima. She says to him:

Indeed, my father said to me: I see in the front of your face a glow and a light! ... When the sixth month came... Then I, when it became the dark night, I did not need a lamp! And when I secluded myself in my prayer place, I would hear declaring of purity (Tanzih), exaltation (Tasbih), and sanctification (Taqdis) in my inner being!

This is the lamp of guidance! This is the hidden knowledge whose meaning we do not know. **This is the heat in the hearts of the believers!** "A heat," the narrations say, "for the love of Al-Hussein, that never cools down!"

The Master of Martyrs laid down the dividing line. The Master of Martyrs seeks reform in this incubator. Not outside the incubator. A narration from our **Imam al-Sadiq**, mentioned in the noble Al-Kafi, in Al-Faqih by Sheikh al-Saduq. He says: "When Al-Hussein fell to the earth... and they rushed towards him and slaughtered him..."
A caller called out from the depths of the Throne! ... O you bewildered, astray Ummah after its Prophet! May Allah not grant you success for an Adha (sacrifice) nor for a Fitr (breaking fast)!
Then Imam al-Sadiq comments and says: **"Undoubtedly, by Allah, they were not granted success, and He will never grant them success, until Al-Hussein's vengeance is exacted!"**

Therefore, we have a narration from our Imam al-Baqir... What does our Imam al-Baqir say? ... "Your religion this will remain turning away (Muwalliyan)... scrambling in its blood (Yafhasu bi-Damihi)!" Meaning like the wounded person who is about to die, and has been covered in a pool of blood, tossing and turning in it!
Then no one will return it to you except a man from the Ahlulbayt!
Segment 6: The Slaughter of the Infant and the Anguish of the Heavens

As the night of the ninth of Muharram arrives, the Sheikh shifts to the tragic events of Karbala, focusing on the harrowing martyrdom of Abdullah the infant (Ali al-Asghar). He vividly recounts the dialogue between Al-Mukhtar and the archer Harmalah, describing how the infant’s neck shone like silver before the arrow severed his jugular vein. The Sheikh details the heartbreaking moments of the infant's blood being thrown to the heavens, his tiny arms embracing Imam Hussein's neck in his death throes, and the ultimate indignity of the Umayyad army exhuming the infant's grave just to sever and parade his head on a spear.

This night is the night of Al-Hussein, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him... This night is the night of bidding farewell to the family. This night is the final night for the Master of the Youth of Paradise in the land of Al-Taff. Today is the ninth day... I do not want to prolong for you. It is my custom on such a night to mention the nursing infant. This night I mention the nursing infant, I make him a door of intercession (Tawassul) before the Master of Martyrs, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him.

I want to transport you to the time of Al-Mukhtar. When Harmalah was brought and stood before Al-Mukhtar. Al-Mukhtar asked him: "Tell me about what you did." So Harmalah began to speak... Al-Mukhtar asks him, says to him: "Woe unto you! Did your heart not soften? While he is carrying a nursing infant in his hands, looking like a piece of the moon?" And he says: "No."
He said: "When Ibn Sa'd, Umar ibn Sa'd, said to me: 'O Harmalah, cut the dispute of the people!' I took the arrow out of its quiver, placed it in the belly of the bow, and aimed it at the infant."

He says in this moment the wind blew. And this is in addition to the meaning of mobilizing emotions... He says the wind blew, lifting the garment from his neck! **So his neck appeared like a silver jug!** Abdullah the infant. So it appeared like a silver jug. He says in this moment I seized the opportunity, directed the arrow, and it fell into his neck, slaughtering him from jugular vein to jugular vein!
The arrow struck something of a silvery neck, Abdullah's blood overflowed, And dyed every happiness with blood, Rubbing the fountain into the face of Hussein!

He said: "Woe unto you, when did your heart soften?" He said: "My heart softened at this state." Which state? "When the arrow fell into the neck of the infant, and slaughtered him from jugular vein to jugular vein, and his blood overflowed like a fountain upon the chest of Al-Hussein... this infant took out his hands and embraced Al-Hussein!" He said: **"I, in this moment, my heart softened! When his blood was spilled, and from the heat of the arrow and the heat of the wound, the infant took out his hands and wrapped them around the neck of Al-Hussein!"**

Therefore, Al-Hussein, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him, did not return the infant to his mother. He returned the infant to the Lady [Zaynab]. "My sister, take the child from me so I can cover him." He gave him to the Lady... She did not want Rabab to see her child slaughtered from jugular vein to jugular vein while she was expecting him to be given water!

The Master of Martyrs after that dug a grave between the tents... When the battle ended, and the heads were divided... Umar ibn Sa'd said: "I will solve the problem." He examined the heads, he did not see the head of the infant! He said: "Go to the grave, dig it up."

They dug the grave of the infant, took out the infant, cut off his head, raised it on a spear, and entered Kufa! And this tribe from the tribes, joyful, raising the head of the infant on the head of a spear stripped of its blade!

And when Al-Sajjad, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him, came to bury the bodies... When he wanted to exit, suddenly a voice from the noble throat: **"My son Ali! Place my nursing infant beside me!"** So a nursing infant is buried with Al-Hussein!

Therefore, in some reports, when the Imam of our Time... arrives in Karbala... And the one reciting the mourning is Al-Hujjah ibn al-Hasan al-Askari! Imagine these numbers... at the shrine of Al-Hussein, millions upon millions of humans... And the pulpit, where does he lean? He leans exactly on the grave of Al-Hussein!
He brings out the infant! He says to his companions: 'If the adults had a sin... then what was the sin of this one?!'
Segment 7: The Orphans of Muslim ibn Aqil and the Final Supplication

In the final segment, the Sheikh recounts the tragic fate of Muhammad and Ibrahim, the young orphaned sons of Muslim ibn Aqil. Escaping from prison, they are captured by a ruthless killer who ignores their pleas and their connection to the Prophet. The Sheikh highlights the heart-wrenching moment when the older brother offers his own life to spare his younger sibling, and the younger brother rubs his own face in his slain brother's blood. The episode closes with their bodies reuniting in the river and a tearful, impassioned supplication to Imam al-Mahdi, begging for his reappearance by the agony of the children of Al-Hussein.

Al-Taff presented children and children. And there were children and children and children. I will point to the children of Muslim ibn Aqil briefly... Muhammad and Ibrahim, young children, their ages between six years and four years. Placed in prison, and they fled until they fell into the hands of that criminal. And he took them out of his house next to the river. For what reason? He wanted to slaughter them, to cut off their heads!

They pleaded with him, they said: 'O uncle, have mercy on our kinship to the Messenger of Allah.' He said: 'You have no kinship to the Messenger of Allah, and there is no mercy in my heart for you.' ... They said: 'Take us to Ibn Ziyad, let him judge us.' He said: 'I must cut off your heads.' They said to him: 'Then let us pray some rak'ahs.' He said: 'Pray if prayer will benefit you.'

After they prayed some rak'ahs... he grabbed the younger one, wanting to slaughter him. **The older one threw himself upon the younger one, saying: "Slaughter me before him!"** When he wanted to slaughter the older of the two, the younger one threw himself upon his brother, saying: "Slaughter me before him!" After that, he dragged the older one by his head, and struck him with the sword, cutting off his head. You notice? This small child, four years old, looking at his brother being slaughtered! Imagine the state with me.

This child, do you know what he did? **He threw himself into his brother's blood! And he smeared himself with his brother's blood! And he said: "Thus I shall meet Allah, smeared in my blood and the blood of my brother!"**

This criminal took the head... What did he do with the body? He threw the body into the river... The books of biography say the body did not move! It remained still in its place, even though the water was flowing... The body remained standing! What is this body waiting for? Until the criminal grabbed the younger child and cut off his head, and placed his head in the saddlebag, and threw his body into the river... this [first] body was waiting for the second body! The books of biography say: **So the two bodies embraced each other! And they went with the flow of the river.**

My masters, O Family of Muhammad. Peace be upon your desolate homes. O Allah, we ask You by the agony of the Family of Muhammad. By the agony of the nursing infant of Al-Hussein. We ask You by the agony of the children of Al-Hussein. By the agony of the orphans of Al-Hussein. That You hasten the relief of our Imam, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him. And that You do not deprive us of serving Al-Hussein.

I ask you all for supplication. And our final call is that praise be to Allah, Lord of the Worlds. And may Allah send prayers upon our Master and our Prophet, Muhammad, and his family, the most good and most pure.

title: The File of Ashura - Episode 7: The Cosmic Covenant and the Awaited Avenger
speaker: Sheikh Abdul-Halim al-Ghizzi

content.text: Overview: Episode 7

In the seventh and final episode of the File of Ashura, Sheikh al-Ghizzi concludes his profound investigation into the ultimate purpose of Imam Hussein's sacrifice. He reveals that the "hidden knowledge" of Al-Hussein is a **cosmic reality embedded in the inner beings of the believers** and witnessed by the Prophets—who exist beyond linear time—as seen in the grief of Prophet Zakariyya. The Sheikh illustrates the irresistible magnetic pull of the Husseini incubator through the transformations of companions like Zuhayr ibn al-Qayn and Wahb the Christian. He explains that this eternal incubator was designed to **cultivate the specific ideological and emotional base required for the awaited Mahdi**, whose ultimate slogan is "O Vengeance of Al-Hussein!" The series culminates with a harrowing, detailed report of the Imam's final physical agonies submitted to the Awaited Avenger, followed by a deeply moving, original three-chapter elegy dedicated to the tragedy of Karbala.
Segment 1: Introduction and the Hidden Knowledge of Al-Hussein

Sheikh al-Ghizzi opens the final episode of The File of Ashura with a sorrowful poetic elegy honoring Imam Hussein, specifically lamenting the atrocities committed by Shimr and the trauma inflicted upon the women and children. He recaps the overarching theme of the previous episodes—answering the question "What is Ashura?" through the lens of its immediate, intermediate, and distant goals. Resuming his analysis from the previous night, the Sheikh focuses on a profound hadith from the Prophet stating that there is a "hidden knowledge" (Ma'rifah Maktumah) of Al-Hussein embedded in the inner beings of the believers, setting the stage for a deeper exploration of this mystical connection.

May Allah magnify your rewards. May Allah hasten the relief of your Imam, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him. May He bring joy to the Mother of Al-Hasan and Al-Hussein, by prayers upon Muhammad and the family of Muhammad.

In the name of Allah, the Beneficent, the Merciful. Peace be upon the resting places of your sanctity, O Hussein. Peace be upon the brilliance of your purity... Peace be upon you, O beloved... O most beloved of all beloveds, O Hussein.

How can we forget... from Hussein, the severing by the accursed Shimr? How can we forget his radiant head, gifted on spears to the wretched prostitutes? How can we forget... the terror of the children from the night, and from the fire, and from the pain? How can we forget the anguish of Al-Zahra... O Remainder of Allah! How can we forget the anguish of Al-Zahra... A fire... O fire, O light!

Our final night. And my speech began in the first gathering... with the question: What is Ashura? And I said that the answer will be in two horizons... The speech in the past gatherings was in the first horizon... where the speech was about the goals of this colossal Divine project: a near goal, an intermediate goal, and a distant goal.

The conclusion of the speech was in the past night. I reached the prophetic hadith narrated by our traditionist Al-Karajaki... in his book Al-Khara'ij wa al-Jara'ih, with his chain from Al-Miqdad ibn al-Aswad, from the Greatest Prophet:
Indeed, for Al-Hussein in the inner beings of the believers, there is a hidden knowledge (Ma'rifah Maktumah)!

A hidden knowledge... meaning the human cannot delve into its deep secrets. It is hidden! ... So when our Prophet, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, says: "Indeed, for Al-Hussein in the inner beings of the believers, there is a hidden knowledge," we cannot fathom the depths of this knowledge because it is fundamentally hidden!
Segment 2: The Empathy of the Prophets and the Secret of Kaf-Ha-Ya-Ain-Sad

Sheikh al-Ghizzi builds upon the concept of the "hidden knowledge" of Al-Hussein embedded within human nature. He recalls the stories of Adam and Noah, whose spiritual grief over the Fifth Name (Al-Hussein) was cosmically imprinted on humanity. To further illustrate this, he recounts the profound story of Prophet Zakariyya (Zechariah). When Zakariyya learned the Five Holy Names, he felt overwhelming joy at the first four, but was struck with an agonizing, choking sorrow at the fifth. Gabriel then revealed to him the tragic fate of Imam Hussein, cryptically encoded in the Quranic letters Kaf-Ha-Ya-Ain-Sad (Karbala, the Halak/destruction of the Progeny, Yazid, 'Atash/thirst, and Sabr/patience). The Sheikh emphasizes that such divine interactions are not mere storytelling, but profound cosmic engagements with the reality of Ashura.

But we revolve around it! ... I pointed to the issue of the Fitrah (innate nature) in the human composition, and to the layer of the subconscious (La-Shu'ur) and the conscious layer (Shu'ur) in the human structure.

I also pointed to what came in the story of our Prophet Adam, and what used to afflict him of sorrow and breaking of the heart when the Fifth Name is mentioned. And what also came in the story of Prophet Noah... regarding the Five Nails which were the cause of the salvation of his Ark.

I will strike a single example: what came in the story of Prophet Zakariyya... The report is transmitted from the Imam of our Time [Al-Mahdi]. Sa'd ibn Abdullah al-Ash'ari al-Qummi transmitted it from Imam Al-Hujjah... regarding the meaning of the beginning of Surah Maryam: Kaf-Ha-Ya-'Ain-Sad.
**Kaf: Karbala. Ha: Halak (destruction) of the Progeny. Ya: Yazid, the killer of Al-Hussein. 'Ain: 'Atash (thirst) of Al-Hussein. Sad: Sabr (patience) of Al-Hussein.**
And this was a remembrance (Dhikr) for Zakariyya... So when Allah disclosed to him the Five Names, he asked Gabriel. He said:
My brother Gabriel! What is it with me that if I mention the four names, my worries are dispelled... but if I mention the Fifth Name, my heart breaks, my tears flow, and the 'Buhrah' seizes me?!

The Buhrah, what is it? It means he almost faints from the intensity of sorrow and weeping! ... So Gabriel informed him. And the summary was in this symbol: Kaf-Ha-Ya-'Ain-Sad... **What was his remembrance? Reading the Maqtal (narrative of martyrdom) of the Master of Martyrs!**

Therefore, he asked Allah... to provide him with a child, so he would love him with the utmost truth, and that he be tested by his killing! Just as the Master of Martyrs is killed. And Yahya (John) was indeed killed as well.

The point of evidence here is that if the Fifth Name was mentioned, his heart broke, softness struck him, and his tears flowed! And the issue is the exact same issue that was in the story of the first fatherhood... and in the story of the second fatherhood... And with all the Prophets!
Segment 3: The Prophets' Cosmic Vision Beyond Linear Time

Sheikh al-Ghizzi challenges the superficial, linear understanding of time when analyzing the Prophets. He explains that for the Prophets—who possess cosmic vision and insight (Basirah)—the past, present, and future are a single point. Using the analogy of a chicken trapped in an artificially lit coop versus the farmer who operates outside that illusion, the Sheikh demonstrates how the Prophets exist outside the confines of earthly time. Thus, when Prophet Abraham looked into the stars and said "I am sick," or when Prophet Adam wept bitterly at the Fifth Name (Al-Hussein), they were not reacting to a "future" event, but witnessing the reality of Ashura as a present, unfolding cosmic truth imprinted on the subconscious of humanity.

These meanings are not to be understood in the historical shape! ... For the Prophets, time is equal! The reality of time, the human senses it through events. **For the people of insight (Basa'ir), the past, present, and future are equal.** Even from the physical point of view, physicists see that the past, present, and future are a single point.

The Prophets, Allah the Exalted gave them insight and gave them intellects that transcend the world of nature. Therefore, when we read these hadiths, we must not deal with them with this naive dealing, as if it were a matter for blessings! It is not a matter for blessings... This is an issue of real interaction.

Therefore, in the story of Abraham: "And he cast a look at the stars, and said, 'Indeed, I am sick.'" The narrations from the Imams say: "He cast a look at the stars," the intended meaning is not these stars in the sky... The narration says: **"He looked into this science, and saw the killing of Al-Hussein, so he said, 'Indeed, I am sick' because of what will happen to Al-Hussein!"**

For the Greatest Prophet, the past, present, and future are present with him at the same time! "No Prophet was harmed like I was harmed." Among the overarching meanings of this hadith is his sensation of every harm that occurs in the world, in the past, present, and future!

If we say that the Prophets are in the highest ranks of wisdom, intellect, knowledge... then these matters must not be understood at this level of naivety! They must be understood with the utmost depth... **The major problem present in the general Shii culture is that there is superficiality in understanding what the Ahlulbayt want.**

And that image, when it is imprinted in the inner beings of the Prophets... the Prophets represent the supreme human model. And this supreme human model leaves its impressions on those around it from humanity... **And this is the meaning of the 124,000 Prophets visiting Al-Hussein on the eve of Friday!**
Segment 4: The Magnetic Pull of Al-Hussein and the Stories of the Companions

The Sheikh emphasizes the inexplicable "magnetic pull" of Imam Hussein. He explains that Hussein is the ultimate convergence of Divine Mercy and Divine Beauty. To prove that this magnetic force was active during Ashura, he highlights the stories of three companions: Zuhayr ibn al-Qayn, who was initially anti-Hussein (Uthmani) and tried to avoid him; Wahb the Christian, a newlywed who had no prior connection to Islamic politics; and 'Amr ibn Junadah, a young boy who proudly identified himself solely through Hussein. None of these individuals joined Hussein due to complex theological arguments or tribal loyalty; they were simply captured by the irresistible cosmic gravity of the "Husseini incubator."

The issue in its entirety, and its orbit, returns to the specificity found in the Master of Martyrs... This specificity: that he is the gathering point of the Divine Beauty! Our Imam al-Sadiq is the one who says: "We are the Most Beautiful Names."

But in this worldly realm, if we want to touch upon examples in the event of Al-Taff... all the circumstances point to this truth! To what truth? **To the truth of the Husseini attraction! There is a Husseini force of attraction unique to the Master of Martyrs.**

For example, Zuhayr ibn al-Qayn. Zuhayr ibn al-Qayn was Uthmani in his inclination! ... He said: "I will not live in a city where Ali lives!" And when the Master of Martyrs departed from Mecca towards Iraq... Zuhayr would try to slow down.

Until the matter became compelling. Zuhayr ibn al-Qayn was forced to stop in the exact same place where the Master of Martyrs stopped! ... When Zuhayr went and returned... he returned with a different face! What transpired between Zuhayr and Imam Hussein is mentioned, but the core of the issue is not this! **The issue is the Husseini attraction!** Zuhayr returned saying: "I am departing with Al-Hussein, protecting him with my life, with my wealth, and with all I have!"

The story of the Christian... A Christian man, his family is Christian... He has no connection to what is happening in the lands of the Muslims! ... What does he care about the hadith of the Prophet, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family? He also joined Al-Hussein!

And his story is even more impactful... When he went out the second time, and he is in the middle of the battle, his wife—the very one who clung to his garments a short while ago so she wouldn't lose him—she herself went out urging him on! ... When he returned, he said: "What changed you?" She said:
The wailing of Al-Hussein broke my heart! I heard him calling: 'Is there a protector to protect us? Is there a helper to help us?'
And like these pictures, there are many other pictures. 'Amr ibn Junadah al-Ansari... His boast was his affiliation with Al-Hussein! "My Emir is Hussein, and what an excellent Emir!"

All of this... point to this truth. Which is: there is an attractive force in Al-Hussein! This attractive force is what preserves this Husseini incubator. **This Husseini incubator, which is the preserver, and it is the origin, and it is the shelter... for the multitudes from which the supporters of the Imam, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him, will emerge.**
Segment 5: The Eternal Banner and the Covenant of the Mahdawi Vengeance

The Sheikh explores why the slogan for the final Appearance of Imam al-Mahdi is "O Vengeance of Al-Hussein" (Ya Latharat Al-Hussein), explaining that such a slogan requires a dedicated, historically prepared base to answer its call. To prove this, he quotes a profound narration from Kamil al-Ziyarat where Lady Zaynab comforts Imam Sajjad on the morning of the 11th of Muharram. She reveals a divine covenant: despite the endless efforts of the leaders of disbelief to erase Ashura, Allah has pledged that a specific group of people will eternally raise the banner of Al-Hussein, and its legacy will only grow taller and brighter. The Sheikh asserts that the ultimate purpose of Ashura was to forge this indestructible Husseini incubator, from which the Mahdi's movement will inevitably emerge.
Why is the slogan "O Vengeance of Al-Hussein!"? ... **Logic says that this slogan must launch from a place, from a group that interacts with this slogan!**

When this is the primary slogan for the Mahdawi project... is it conceivable that the Imam, peace be upon him, raises this slogan, for example, in a medium that has no connection to Al-Hussein? ... **This slogan is raised in this Husseini incubator! In the Husseini medium.**

A single narration explains many of these meanings... A narration found in **Kamil al-Ziyarat**. ... Sheikh Ibn Qulawayh transmits this narration from the Houri of the Household of Muhammad, from the Lady [Zaynab], while she is narrating to Imam al-Sajjad.
She tells him about an "established covenant" from the Messenger of Allah. What does this established covenant say?

Allah has taken a covenant from a people from this Ummah... That they will set up for this Taff a banner for the grave of your father, the Master of Martyrs, whose trace will not be erased, and whose shape will not be wiped out across the passage of nights and days! ... And the leaders of disbelief and the followers of misguidance will exert their utmost effort to erase it and obliterate it! ... Yet its trace will only increase in appearance, and its affair will only increase in elevation!

This is the Husseini incubator which I have been talking about these nights! And this is the Husseini group which I was talking about in the past nights... The words of the Ahlulbayt strike the core of the truth!

The conclusion I reach is that the furthest goal for this colossal Divine project, the project of Ashura, is for the Reform to be realized in the Husseini group! And not in the Ummah! ... Therefore, Reform, and Enjoining the Good and Forbidding the Evil... **all of this is in the Husseini incubator! And outside the Husseini incubator, there is absolutely no trace.**
Segment 6: The Final Report to the Awaited Avenger

Sheikh al-Ghizzi shifts the focus to the brutal physical reality of Ashura, stating that he will not simply recount the Maqtal (narrative of martyrdom), but rather submit a formal "report" to the Awaited Mahdi—the "Organization for Defending the Blood of the Slain in Karbala." Analyzing the words of Ziyarat al-Nahiyah, the Sheikh explains that Imam Hussein suffered two distinct types of slaughter: being pierced in the chest (Nahr) and having his jugular veins severed (Dhabh). He then details the agonizing sequence of the Imam's final moments, listing the specific, successive strikes by Abu al-Hutuf, Harmalah, Salih ibn Wahb, Sinan ibn Anas, and finally, the twelve brutal blows delivered by Shimr to the back of the Imam's neck to shatter his spine.
I do not want to prolong for you. I point to this narration also from **Kamil al-Ziyarat**... Muhammad ibn Muslim al-Ta'ifi transmits the narration, transmits the hadith from our Imam Baqir al-'Ulum:
If people knew what merit there is in visiting the grave of Al-Hussein, they would have died of longing!
A narration of the Truthful of the Progeny [Imam al-Sadiq] also in Kamil al-Ziyarat:
There is no one on the Day of Resurrection except that he wishes to be among the visitors of Al-Hussein! For what reason? For what he sees of the merit for the visitors of Al-Hussein!
In the **Ziyarat of the Sacred Sphere (Ziyarat al-Nahiyah al-Muqaddasah)**, there are two phrases while we send peace upon the Master of Martyrs:
Peace be upon the one pierced in the chest (Al-Manhur) in the open! Peace be upon the one whose jugular vein (Watin) was severed!

Meaning that the Imam, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him, was subjected to two types of slaughter! As this Ziyarat says: "Peace be upon the one pierced in the chest in the open," the Imam was pierced. "Peace be upon the one whose jugular vein was severed," and the Imam's jugular vein was severed, his veins were severed.

I, in this night, do not want to recite a mourning (Ta'ziyah). I want to submit a complaint... I submit a report to this organization, to this section: **"The Seeker of the blood of the slain in Karbala."**

The first picture. A cursed man named Abu al-Hutuf. What did Abu al-Hutuf do? ... The first crime: a sharp, poisoned arrow he aims at the forehead of your Imam! ... The second crime: after the arrow, a stone! To the exact same spot.

Harmalah aimed a three-pronged arrow at the heart of the Master of Martyrs! And the books of the Maqatil show us that the Imam extracted it from his back! ... and the blood gushed out like a spout! And with all that, Al-Hussein remained standing!

When did Al-Hussein fall? When that criminal, Salih ibn Wahb, came and stabbed him with the spear in his waist! ... And the arrows in the armor of the Imam, as the narrations say, were like the quills of a hedgehog!

But the most severe strikes directed at the Master of Martyrs were when? When Sinan ibn Anas approached him... he stabbed him in the collarbone! ... Then he struck him with an arrow in his throat! ... Then he shot him with an arrow in his upper chest (Nahr)! ... And he stabbed him in the Bawani of his chest, breaking them!

Then they attacked him, striking him from every direction! ... Until it was the turn of Shimr ibn Dhi al-Jawshan! ... The first thing he did when he reached the Imam, he kicked the Imam with his foot! Then he spat on the Imam! And cursed the Imam! And after that, he struck the Imam with his sword twelve strikes! ... **he was slaughtered from the nape (Qafa)! These twelve strikes... were to break the spinal column. In this manner the Master of Martyrs, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him, was slaughtered!**
Segment 7: The Final Elegy - The Card Upon the Wreath of Sorrows

Sheikh al-Ghizzi concludes the entire File of Ashura series with a powerful, original poetic elegy structured in three chapters. The first chapter is addressed to the slaughtered infant, Abdullah, questioning the cosmic reason why his tiny neck had to be severed. The second chapter addresses Imam Hussein, praising his unparalleled majesty and the eternal power of his banner. The third and final chapter reflects on the cosmic grief of Ashura, where nature itself—sand, iron, fire, and the Euphrates—weeps for the tragedy, drawing a profound parallel between the crushing of Lady Fatima’s ribs and the crushing of Imam Hussein's chest. The Sheikh concludes the program with a heartfelt prayer to the Awaited Mahdi.

This is the concluding gathering. And if people present wreaths of roses on occasions, and write a card on their wreaths of roses... I present these gatherings, these gatherings of sorrows, I present them as wreaths of sorrow! And I write upon them a card. A card upon the wreath of sorrows. Three chapters.
**The First Chapter:**

To you, O whom I have loved from all my heart... To you, O angel of Al-Taff, the slaughtered one! ... To you, O nursing infant of Al-Hussein... a thing, and no thing! And I, though my days have passed to widen our livers... an ember of a question that has long cut into our paths along the stretch of time: Why did they slaughter you, O son of the Broken-Ribbed One? From jugular vein to jugular vein... between the chest of your father and his most sacred neck?
**The Second Chapter:**

Abu Abdullah... I see glory with all its fringes and the bounds of its expanse bowing with folded wings, out of shame from your overarching, towering glory. And I see you a knight who was not defeated, and will never be defeated! ... Abu Abdullah, O lofty Alawi stature! O banner that wraps in its folds the fragrance of Muhammad! ... O father of the free... my mount is cut off, and the journey has exhausted me! The provisions have run out... and the days have nothing left but to extend them to you, so take me, may Allah protect you.
**The Third Chapter:**

O Hussein, the sands, the blood, the iron, the fire, the thirst, and the pain... all weep for you without hypocrisy, O Hussein! The Euphrates wept for you, and even the thirst wept! ... Indeed, horses that Ibn Sa'd called out to: "O horses of Allah, ride!" And the chest of Al-Hussein was displayed to you, for you are the ascension of existence! You are the meeting point of the arcs of descent and ascent! But they lost the way. They thought they were ascending to the Throne, so they broke the Door of Heaven when they broke those ribs! ... O Hussein, my eye weeps for you not for the sake of reward, but my eye is weeping for your sake. The signature: A thing that wishes to be the dust of your feet.

O Allah, O Lord of Al-Hussein. By the right of Al-Hussein. Heal the chest of Al-Hussein with the Appearance of Al-Hujjah, peace be upon him. O Allah, we adjure you by Al-Hussein and Al-Hussein, do not deprive us of serving Al-Hussein until the end of our lives, by the right of Al-Hussein and Al-Hussein.

I ask you all for supplication. And our final call is that praise be to Allah, Lord of the Worlds, and may Allah send prayers upon our Master and our Prophet, Muhammad, and his family, the most good and most pure.

series: The File of Al-Mukhtar: The Loyal Husseini Avenger
title: Episode 1: The Methodology of the Ahlulbayt
speaker: Sheikh Abdul-Halim al-Ghizzi

content.text: Overview: Episode 1

In the premier episode of the series on Al-Mukhtar al-Thaqafi, Sheikh al-Ghizzi establishes that this study is not merely historical, but a deep intellectual exploration applying the **exclusive epistemological methodology of the Ahlulbayt**, known as the **"Melody of Speech" (Lahn al-Qawl)**. To contextualize this, the Sheikh traces the history of human philosophical and scientific methodologies—from Pythagoras to Aristotle to modern physics—demonstrating how human intellect constantly requires refined tools to uncover truths. He contrasts these human endeavors with the primitive, linguistic-based Bedouin culture of the early Arabs during the era of "Revelation" (Tanzil). The Sheikh argues that to grasp the perfected religion, one must transition to the **stage of Interpretation (Ta'wil)**, which Imam Ali famously fought to protect. He issues a profound critique of both opponent scholars like Al-Shafi'i and modern Shii scholars who have tragically abandoned the divine principles (Usul) of the Ahlulbayt in favor of flawed Aristotelian logic and literal linguistics, thereby distorting the true understanding of the Quran and the Imams.
Segment 1: Introduction - Al-Hussein, The Only Truth

Sheikh al-Ghizzi opens the first episode of the series focusing on Al-Mukhtar al-Thaqafi. He begins with a profound declaration that Al-Hussein is the only true reality in the lives of the believers, and everything else is merely a mirage. He explains that this program is not just a historical narration, but an intellectual study. It serves a dual purpose: first, to apply the specific epistemological methodology of the Ahlulbayt, known as the "Melody of Speech" (Lahn al-Qawl), to the study of Al-Mukhtar's revolution; and second, to explore the unique, unparalleled status that Al-Mukhtar holds within the "Husseini Shii conscience."
O Zahra... In the name of Allah, the Beneficent, the Merciful. The Loyal Husseini Avenger: Al-Mukhtar al-Thaqafi. Episode One.

Peace be upon you all. If we have a story in life... our story is Al-Hussein! For a simple and very clear reason, at least to us: **Al-Hussein is the only truth in our lives, and the rest is all a mirage!** "O 'Nun' of the texts!" And all of us, what is around us, what is with us, and what is with others of truth or falsehood... are in the margins of the margins. O Hussein... The Loyal Husseini Avenger. Al-Mukhtar al-Thaqafi.

This program is not a purely historical program. It is an intellectual program, more than it is a historical program... This program is important from two aspects. From one aspect, **it is a practical application of the methodology of the 'Melody of Speech' (Lahn al-Qawl) in studying the personality of Al-Mukhtar!** And accurately, in studying the revolution of Al-Mukhtar.

And the second aspect which points to the importance of this program is the importance of the personality of Al-Mukhtar in Shii history, and precisely in the Husseini Shii conscience. **For this man has a station in the Husseini Shii conscience that is distinguished from all other stations of the Husseini Shia!**

The beginning will be from this point. Which point? It is the point that must be pointed to when the speech is about the human being, about knowledge, about science, and about perception. An introduction through which I will reach the methodology of the 'Melody of Speech' (Lahn al-Qawl)...
Segment 2: The Essence of the Human Being and the Historical Quest for Methodology

Sheikh al-Ghizzi delves into the epistemological foundation of human thought. He posits that a human being is essentially a collection of convictions and emotions, and the primary tool for shaping these is the intellect (Fikr). To illustrate the historical human quest for a correct methodology to reach the truth, he outlines the evolution of Greek philosophy: from Pythagoras's mathematical universe, to Socrates' dialectic questioning and mystical inclination, to Plato's synthesis of math and mysticism (Illuminationism), and finally to Aristotle's Peripatetic school, which relied heavily on physical observation and logical deduction.

The human being in his reality is **a collection of convictions and emotions!** This is the human being. The difference between the sane and the insane, what is the difference? It is the difference between the convictions and emotions of the sane, and the convictions and emotions of the insane... The problem is in the path through which he arrives at those convictions, and to those ideas and emotions.

So the human is thought. Convictions return to thought. And even emotions—true, emotions are internal and intuitive, but their roots stem from thought. **There is originally a thought that moves the human, and this thought that moves the human leads to interaction within the hidden core of his conscience, in the flutterings of his soul, from within which emotions will be born.**

Our station is thought. The thought of the human. Because the human cannot perceive things except through thought! ... How can the human, through thought, arrive at the correct information? There are paths through which the human arrives at information.

Humans since the beginnings have experimented, felt around, and searched for the paths in which they employed thought. And they tried to employ thought through these methods, through these paths, through these means and mediums to reach the information which they at least feel reassured by...

Perhaps one of the oldest names we have in human experience... is Pythagoras... He walked the methodology of mathematics to reach the correct information. He became convinced that the world, that the universe in its reality is mathematical laws, mathematical equations!

And after Pythagoras came Socrates. And Socrates had a mystical inclination (Naza'ah Sufiyyah). **And the mystical inclination here is the immersion of the human mind beyond the senses, beyond matter, and drowning far away!** He used a method to reach the perception of information that was called the Socratic Dialectic (Al-Jadal al-Suqrati).

And after him came his student Plato... And Plato came with a method in which he combined Pythagoreanism—between mathematics—and the mystical inclination he took from Socrates. And his theory, the Platonic Ideals (Al-Muthul al-Aflatuniyyah)... Which was adopted by the Illuminationists (Ishraqiyyin) among the mystics of various religions.

And after Plato came Aristotle. **The master of the Peripatetic school (Al-Madrasah al-Masha'iyyah), which differs in its approach from the Illuminationist school, the school of his teacher [Plato].** He relied on researching correct propositions. He gathers correct propositions... and on this basis, he established the methodology of research in the natural sciences.

So he relies on the logical method that many use to this day! Especially in religious schools. **In the Christian religious school, Aristotelian logic is the ruler. And in the Sunni or Shii religious school, Aristotelian logic is the ruler!** This is the method: gathering correct propositions, and based on the correct propositions, we reach the correct results.
Segment 3: The Evolution of Scientific Method and the Limitation of the Bedouin Mind

Sheikh al-Ghizzi traces the evolution of scientific methodology from Ptolemy and Copernicus to Einstein and Superstring Theory, illustrating that human knowledge constantly requires refined tools and methodologies to uncover deeper truths. He contrasts this with the primitive, linguistic-based Bedouin culture of the Arabs during the Prophet’s time. Because the Arabs were culturally simplistic, the Prophet delivered the Quran during the stage of "Revelation" (Tanzil) using their basic linguistic framework. However, the deeper reality of the religion required a new methodology for "Interpretation" (Ta'wil). The opponents of the Ahlulbayt rejected this deeper methodology, sticking strictly to literal linguistics, which resulted in a naive, superstitious theology and an ignorant understanding of the Imamate.

Here came after him Ptolemy... And he established upon the premises he brought, and based on his observations, the first rules of astronomy, which remained ruling for centuries and centuries. And it became part of religion! ... Why did they try Galileo the Italian? Why did the Church put him on trial? Because he came with a thought that contradicted Ptolemaic thought!

The major shift that happened was in the time of... Nicolaus Copernicus... In his time, the way of thinking changed. Nicolaus Copernicus came with a new proposition and a new methodology. And after him came Galileo Galilei, whom Einstein calls the father of modern science, because he changed the methodology of research. Then after that came Newton... Then Einstein came... And his theory, Special Relativity and General Relativity... changed the methodology of research and thinking.

Until we are now in the beginnings of the twenty-first century... The Superstring theory, which says that the atoms of matter are composed of strings. I do not want to list names, terminology, and details for you. Therefore, I quickly presented these demands.

Where do I want to reach? I am saying that when a human wants to reach a piece of information, his tool to reach the information is thought! This mental and intellectual activity will not yield a result unless there is a path to employ it. ... What concerns us here is not the discussion about laboratory sciences. The discussion is about human science. Human sciences that deal with the spiritual/abstract aspect in human life.

When our Final Prophet, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, was sent... he was sent among the Arabs. **The culture that governed them was a Bedouin culture. The primary element in it was the word, speech, language.** And dealing with the language was a Bedouin dealing. The Bedouin is characterized by simplicity.

So the era of the Prophet was the era of Revelation (Tanzil). **The Arabs who entered Islam dealt with the Quran and the hadith of the Prophet with a Bedouin dealing. So the understanding relied on language, and language only!**

Therefore, when did the Prophet... express that the religion was completed? When he opened the door of Interpretation (Ta'wil) at the pledge of Al-Ghadir! **"You will fight them over the Ta'wil."** Ta'wil is a new stage. In the stage of Ta'wil, there must be a new path for understanding, a new method for research.

**It is inconceivable that the Prophet, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, comes to the Arabs with a new religion, and wants to convince them of this new thought, and asks them to change their way of thinking to a new method...** So he had to come to them from their own way of thinking, trying to convince them using the very same method.

So it was the Tanzil. Those who opposed the Ahlulbayt remained upon this exact same state. The Umari methodology: "The Book of Allah is sufficient for us." According to the stage of Tanzil, relying on language. **The methodology of the Ahlulbayt is something else!**

And days passed. The opponents of the Ahlulbayt do not possess a specific methodology! They rely on the normal method that was present back then... And the methodology was language, and the method they were upon in the time of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah). **From here came their concept of Monotheism (Tawhid): an ignorant Tawhid! And from here came their understanding of the Imamate: they understood it as the head of a tribe or a prince in some location.**
Segment 4: The Aristotelian Void and the Methodology of Ta'wil

Sheikh al-Ghizzi explains how the opponents of the Ahlulbayt, lacking a divine epistemological framework, eventually adopted Aristotelian logic, spearheaded by Al-Shafi'i. This deductive, language-based methodology contradicted the deeper, intended reality of Islam. The Sheikh highlights the famous hadith stating that Imam Ali would fight the Ummah over the "Interpretation" (Ta'wil) of the Quran, just as the Prophet fought over its "Revelation" (Tanzil). Because literal revelation was no longer sufficient to grasp the perfected religion, the Ahlulbayt established their own exclusive hermeneutic methodology for Ta'wil known as the "Melody of Speech" (Lahn al-Qawl).
And the days pass. Until the time of Al-Shafi'i. Al-Shafi'i represents a clear, specific point in the opposing methodology to the Family of Muhammad, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon them all.

What is Al-Shafi'i's methodology? **Al-Shafi'i's methodology is the Aristotelian methodology!** The exact same methodology. What is the Aristotelian methodology? It is gathering propositions he believes to be correct. And after gathering these propositions that are correct in his view, he extracts the results from them.

And the propositions that are correct in his view, he deals with them in the exact same way the Arabs dealt in the era of Revelation (Tanzil) in understanding the Quranic text, and in understanding the Prophetic text: language! Language, language is the foundation.

The hadith of the Prophet which the Shia and Sunnis narrate: that Ali will fight them over the Interpretation (Ta'wil)... It points out that the Ta'wil is different from the Tanzil (Revelation). The Prophet, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, says to him that you will fight them over the Ta'wil just as I fought them over the Tanzil! **And the hadith here is speaking about an infallible fight!**

So was the Tanzil sought after in the time of Ali? The Tanzil was not sought after; if the Tanzil was sought after, he wouldn't have fought them over the Ta'wil! ... **Rather, the Wilayah of Ali is faith.**

What is required is the Ta'wil, not the Tanzil! ... "This day I have perfected for you your religion." When is this perfection? **The religion is incomplete, but when was it perfected? It was perfected when the stage of Ta'wil began!** Meaning if you enter the stage of Ta'wil, then your religion is perfected. But if you remain upon the stage of Tanzil, this religion is a religion of deficiency!

The stage of Ta'wil is what the words of the Household of Infallibility spoke about! And what is termed as the methodology of the 'Melody of Speech' (Lahn al-Qawl). It is the methodology of Ta'wil! It is that we understand the Book, and the hadith of the 'Itrah (Progeny), and that we understand the religion, and understand faith... according to the methodology of Ta'wil, which I title the methodology of Lahn al-Qawl!
We do not consider a man among our companions to be a jurist (Faqih), intelligent, and wise until he is spoken to in melody (Yulhan lahu fil-Qawl) and he understands the melody in speech!

The melody in speech is the style! It is the style followed in the stage of Ta'wil, and this is Lahn al-Qawl. And it differs completely from the Aristotelian methodology! From Al-Shafi'i's methodology! ... But this methodology of Lahn al-Qawl, according to what I claim, is the methodology of Ta'wil! It is the methodology extracted from the truths of the Book and the 'Itrah.
Segment 5: The Prohibition of Interpreting the Quran by Personal Opinion

Sheikh al-Ghizzi presents several foundational hadiths from the Ahlulbayt unequivocally forbidding the interpretation of the Quran through personal opinion (Tafsir bil-Ra'y). Imam Ali warns that while the Tanzil (revelation) of the Quran resembles human speech, its Ta'wil (interpretation) does not, and treating it as such leads to destruction. The Sheikh quotes subsequent Imams explaining that the Quran has an outer and inner reality (Zahr and Batin), and that it employs the rhetorical device of "I mean you, but listen O neighbor," proving that relying purely on literal, linguistic methodologies—like those used by the opponents—is fundamentally flawed.

From Abu Ma'mar al-Sa'dani, that a man was told by the Commander of the Believers, Ali ibn Abi Talib, peace be upon him. The Emir says to this man: **"Beware of interpreting the Quran with your opinion!"** Interpreting the Quran with his opinion... how? Relying on language and resting upon the inherited culture! This is interpretation by opinion: relying on language and resting upon the inherited culture. And this is what occurs with the opponent.

And I shift to our scholars, our jurists, our Maraji', and our exegetes (Mufassirun). "Beware of interpreting the Quran with your opinion, until you seek its understanding from the scholars ('Ulama')." The intended meaning of the "scholars" here is the Infallibles, prayers of Allah be upon them. They say: **"We are the scholars, and our Shia are the learners."**

Beware of interpreting the Quran with your opinion, until you seek its understanding from the scholars! For there is many a revelation (Tanzil) that resembles the speech of humans, while it is the speech of Allah! ... And its interpretation (Ta'wil) does not resemble the speech of humans!

This Tanzil in its words, in its verbal structure, in its letters, resembles the speech of humans. Otherwise, how could humans understand the speech of Allah if it were not like the speech of humans? This is according to the stage of Tanzil. But according to the stage of Ta'wil, the reality differs!

Now, do the exegetes not deal with the Quran as if it is the speech of humans? And they deal with the Quran in the exact same way they deal with the speech of the rest of humans! ... In the way the Arabs understood the Quran in the time of the Prophet, in the stage of Tanzil, over which the Prophet commanded Ali to fight the Companions of the Prophet if they remained upon it! ... So why do we, while we are the Shia of Ali, follow the exact same methodology that the enemies of Ali followed, and over which he fought them?! Why?

From Jabir al-Ju'fi, he said: Abu Abdullah [Imam al-Sadiq], peace be upon him, said: "O Jabir, **indeed the Quran has a belly (Batin), and the belly has a back (Zahr).**" Then he said: "O Jabir, and there is nothing further from the intellects of men than it! Indeed, the verse descends: its beginning is regarding one thing, its middle is regarding another thing, and its end is regarding another thing!"

The unity of context (Wahdat al-Siyaq) falls here! If the unity of context falls within a single verse, what remains of the rules of understanding the Quran operated by the opponents, and operated by our scholars and exegetes out of following or imitating the opponents?
From Hisham ibn Salim, from Abu Ja'far [Imam al-Baqir], he said: 'Whoever interprets the Quran with his opinion, if he hits the mark, he is not rewarded! And if he errs, he has sinned!'
From Al-Qasim ibn Sulayman, from Abu Abdullah [Imam al-Sadiq]... My father [Al-Baqir] said: 'No man has struck the Quran, one part against another, except that he committed disbelief (Kafar)!'

From Al-Fudayl ibn Yasar, he said: I asked Abu Ja'far about this narration: **"There is no verse in the Quran except that it has a back (Zahr) and a belly (Batin)..."** "What does he mean by his saying it has a back and a belly?" He said:
Its back is its revelation (Tanziluha), and its belly is its interpretation (Ta'wiluha)!

And the narrations from them, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon them all, are numerous. That the Quran was revealed by: **"I mean you, but listen, O neighbor (Iyyaki a'ni wasma'i ya jarah)!"** This is the tone of the narrations: the entire Quran was revealed in this tone!
Segment 6: The True Principles (Usul) of the Ahlulbayt vs. The Scholars of Misguidance

Sheikh al-Ghizzi draws a sharp contrast between the divine epistemological principles (Usul) established by the Ahlulbayt and the flawed, Aristotelian-inspired methodologies currently dominating Shii seminaries (Hawzas). Quoting Imam al-Askari, he highlights a severe warning against corrupt Shii scholars who blend true knowledge with the lies of the opponents, misleading the vulnerable Shia. The Imam explicitly describes these scholars as being more harmful to the Shia than Yazid's army was to Imam Hussein. The episode concludes by asking whether the Ahlulbayt would have ever left the Shia without a distinct methodology for Ta'wil.
What do they say? This is 'Uyun Akhbar al-Ridha by our Sheikh al-Saduq. What does our Imam al-Ridha say?
Upon us is casting the principles (Usul) to you, and upon you is the branching (Tafri').

The principles are these! So have we acted upon these principles? Has our scientific seminary (Hawzah) acted upon these principles? Is the Shii culture built upon these principles? These narrations that passed talk about the principles of understanding the Quran. Why do we take our principles from the opponents and branch upon them, then falsely say that this was taken from the Ahlulbayt?!

From our Imam al-Sadiq, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him: "A hadith you comprehend (Tadrih) is better than a thousand you narrate (Tarwih). **And a man among you is not a Faqih (jurist) until he knows the hidden meanings (Ma'arid) of our speech!**" Ma'arid means the speech from the verbal side is one thing, and the meaning is another thing!
What does our Imam al-Ridha say?

Indeed, in our news there is Mutashabih (ambiguous) like the Mutashabih of the Quran! And Muhkam (clear/decisive) like the Muhkam of the Quran. So refer its ambiguous to its clear, and do not follow its ambiguous without its clear, lest you go astray!
These are the principles! This is the hadith of the Ahlulbayt! And this is their methodology.

I am reviewing before you these models from their words... In order to supply you with these givens: that the stage of Ta'wil, that the Quran, and that the hadith of the 'Itrah have specificities that differ from the specificities of the speech of the general people! And that the stage of Ta'wil has conditions that differ from the stage of Tanzil. And consequently, we cannot use the exact same methodology in research, thinking, and studying with such givens. It is not suitable for us to use the Aristotelian methodology.

But I say: let us try to be in a place as close as we can be to them, prayers of Allah upon them! So where are the results I take from a methodology laid down by Aristotle, and walked upon by Al-Shafi'i according to a Bedouin Arab culture in a stage that the Prophet rejected, and commanded Ali to fight whoever remains upon it?! ... Why do we not search for a methodology within their hadith?
The narration in **Rijal al-Kashshi**. Imam al-Sadiq:
Know the stations of our Shia... according to the measure of what they master of narrating from us. For we do not consider the Faqih among them to be a Faqih until he is spoken to (Muhaddathan).
Understanding branches out from them! The rules of understanding are taken from them! Not from the Aristotelian methodology, nor from Al-Shafi'i's methodology.

These narrations all point to the fact that the Ahlulbayt have a specific methodology that differs from the Aristotelian methodology, differs from Al-Shafi'i's methodology, differs from the rest of the methodologies.

As for the first question: Do our scholars possess a single proof? They do not. But I ask this question: **Do they possess a single proof from the hadith of the Ahlulbayt, from the Noble Book? Do they possess a single proof for the correctness of the methodology they are upon?** This Aristotelian and Shafi'i methodology... they possess no proof for it!

The second question: **Is it conceivable that the Ahlulbayt... after establishing the stage of Ta'wil, which differs entirely from the stage of Tanzil, is it conceivable that they left us without a methodology?!** It is impossible to conceive that. So where do we find their methodology? Their methodology is in their hadiths! **"Your speech is light."**

The speech continues, God willing. I will meet you, God willing the Exalted, upon the affection of Al-Hujjah ibn al-Hasan, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him. Our meeting is renewed tomorrow. And I conclude my speech: Peace be upon your bleeding neck, O Hussein. In the safety of Allah.

series: The File of Al-Mukhtar: The Loyal Husseini Avenger
title: The File of Al-Mukhtar: The Loyal Husseini Avenger - Episode 2: The Anatomy of Thought and the Evolution of Methodology
speaker: Sheikh Abdul-Halim al-Ghizzi

content.text: Overview: Episode 2

In the second episode of the File of Al-Mukhtar al-Thaqafi: The Loyal Husseini Avenger, Sheikh al-Ghizzi continues to lay the essential epistemological groundwork required to understand Shii history and the revolution of Al-Mukhtar. He explains that human beings are fundamentally driven by convictions and emotions, which are entirely shaped by the methodology through which their intellects process information. He traces the evolution of human thought—from Greek philosophy to modern physics—to demonstrate that all intellectual frameworks are fallible and subject to evolution. Turning to Islamic history, the Sheikh delivers a profound critique of the early Muslim reliance on the **primitive, Bedouin linguistic methodology of Revelation (Tanzil)**. He critically analyzes early Shii scholars like Al-Kulayni, arguing that while their preservation of hadith was miraculous, their personal analytical methodologies were rooted in mere "submission" rather than the **deep, interpretive "Melody of Speech" (Lahn al-Qawl)** intended by the Ahlulbayt. The Sheikh traces the eventual dominance of the flawed, Aristotelian-influenced methodology established by Sheikh al-Tusi over the modern Shii Hawza, setting the stage to apply the true Ahlulbayt methodology to dissect the complex distinction between Al-Mukhtar's personal character and his historical revolution.
Segment 1: Introduction and the Trajectory of Human Thought

Sheikh al-Ghizzi begins the second episode by reiterating that Al-Hussein is the ultimate, central truth in the life of a believer. Addressing requests for more details on the philosophical methodologies mentioned in the previous episode, he clarifies that while he will not turn the program into a history of philosophy, he will expand upon these ideas to clarify his main objective. He explores the fundamental nature of human existence—rooted in thought, convictions, and emotions—and contrasts Descartes' "I think, therefore I am" with a more accurate philosophical premise: "I exist, therefore I think," emphasizing that knowledge requires a specific path or methodology.
O Zahra... In the name of Allah, the Beneficent, the Merciful. The Loyal Husseini Avenger: Al-Mukhtar al-Thaqafi. Episode Two.

Peace be upon you all. If we have a story in life... our story is Al-Hussein! For a simple and very clear reason, at least to us: **Al-Hussein is the only truth in our lives, and the rest is all a mirage!** O 'Nun' of the text of texts. And all of us, what is around us, what is with us, and what is with others of truth or falsehood... are in the margins of the margins. O Hussein...

The speech passed in the previous episode regarding the atmospheres of the methodologies of human thought. And I spoke about models from the methodologies of human thought across history in a brief manner. There are some brothers and sisters who request more detail in this demand. The truth is that if I wanted to detail the speech, the program would distance itself from its subject, and it would turn into a history of philosophers and thinkers, and into a discussion and study of their methodologies in thinking and in scientific research, and to what branches out from that of various demands.

For example, if I wanted to speak about the Platonic methodology, I do not believe that two or three episodes would suffice! And if the speech were, for example, about the Aristotelian methodology, I do not imagine that five episodes would suffice me for the speech, and consequently, the research would branch out... And I primarily did not convene the program to speak about these topics. I only cited what I cited in the past episode by way of examples to bring the picture closer. Yet still, I will clarify the idea more.

Fundamentally, where do we begin the speech? Or from where do we begin? The speech in the past episode, in this episode, and at every time... **the human seeks knowledge! The human wants to learn. The human wants to reach the truth as much as possible.**

The well-known Cartesian methodology: "I think, therefore I am." Now the American philosophical methodology has changed: **"I am, therefore I think." And it is more accurate. More accurate than the Cartesian methodology.** "I am." And that is a truth established by "Knowledge by Presence" ('Ilm Huduri). So the known (Ma'lum) is present by itself, so there is no need for me to search for a premise to perceive it. "I think, therefore I am." "I am," because this truth is established by itself for itself. And it is a clear application and a clear rank from the ranks of Knowledge by Presence. Here the known attends by itself with the knower, not the image of the known. The known attends by itself; I am present with myself, and perceiving myself by myself. "I am." And from the effects of this existence is that I think. "I am, therefore I think." Therefore I am a thinker.

The human in his movement is ascending, heading towards knowledge! And he attains knowledge by means of thought!
