Let us assume that the Prophet, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, on some Fridays did not pray for one reason or another. So let us say the number of Fridays in which the Prophet did not pray Friday was around 100 sermons... the Fridays around 200 sermons. Meaning 100 Fridays, let us assume so. How much remains? 840 remains. 840 sermons! **Where are these sermons?!** We do not blame the opponents. The opponents altered the Prophet's hadith. We ask: Our Imams, why did they not transmit to us even a tenth of these sermons? Why? Because they were in the era of Tanzil! They were in the era of Tanzil, this is the reason. Otherwise, why? Even if the Imams transmitted them and they were lost, it is not reasonable that these hundreds of sermons were lost! At the very least, there should remain, for example, 10 sermons. Do we have 10 sermons? We don't have them. Do we have 5? 1? We do not have even 1 from the Prophet's Friday sermons! Where did they go? If the opponents are not to be relied upon, how about our Imams? O people, the matter is clear! The logic is clear logic.

The speech is long, truly. I do not know which demand to leave and which demand to take up, but the program's time is nearing its end. I will meet you tomorrow upon the affection of the heart of Al-Hussein, the door to Al-Hussein: Al-Hujjah ibn al-Hasan, the Imam of our Time, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him. Peace be upon your bleeding neck, O Hussein. In the safety of Allah.

title: Our Shia Beliefs - Introduction Part 1: The Intuitive Proof for Divine Knowledge
speaker: Sheikh Abdul-Halim al-Ghizzi

content.text: Overview: Episode 1

In this foundational episode of Our Shia Beliefs, Sheikh al-Ghizzi provides a profound introduction to the necessity of true knowledge and belief. Bypassing scriptural texts temporarily, he relies on **pure human conscience (Wajdan)** to demonstrate the absolute value of knowledge in human life. He masterfully categorizes all human sciences into two branches: those that bring **welfare** and those that **organize** life. He then elevates the discussion, proving logically and intuitively that human existence **does not end at death**, which necessitates the pursuit of divine, eternal sciences. By establishing that divine knowledge is superior and immutable—unlike flawed, evolving human theories—the Sheikh lays the groundwork for understanding the constant roots of the Twelver Shia creed.
Segment 1: Approaching Knowledge Through Conscience (Wajdan)

Sheikh al-Ghizzi begins by explaining that before delving into the specific beliefs of the Twelver Shia sect, it is essential to establish the value of knowledge itself. Surprisingly, he chooses not to use Quranic verses or Hadiths initially, not out of disrespect, but to prove the necessity of knowledge purely through human conscience and lived reality (Wajdan). He argues that humans inherently possess an internal capacity to distinguish between truths, and observing life proves that survival and progress are impossible without basic, practical knowledge.

Praise be to Allah, and blessings be upon the Messenger of Allah, and upon his family and curses be upon the enemies of Allah. Today the speech is regarding the details of belief. According to the true religion, and according to the foundations of the Twelver sect, which came in the Book of Allah and in the speech of the Prophet and the Legatees, prayers of Allah upon them. And the speech in the chapter of belief is a result and a strike from the strikes of knowledge. The chapter of belief is clear: gnosis and science.

We pause for a short while in explaining the virtue of knowledge. I want here to not rely on the verses of the Book in explaining the virtue of knowledge. And I do not want to rely on the noble wisdom texts, the many that came from the infallibles not from the aspect that they do not indicate the meaning of the virtue of knowledge, nor from the aspect that we do not rely upon them! As I say, I want the speech about knowledge, its virtue, about the virtue of the scholar, and about the virtue of learning for the life of the human, to not revolve around the verses of the Book, nor on the basis of the noble texts. Not out of non-reliance, nor out of questioning their responsibility and precision.

Perhaps at a time, we will take up the speech about knowledge and its virtue based on the verses of the Book. Rather, we do not know the reality of knowledge nor the virtue of knowledge except through the verses and the words in the texts of the infallibles, prayers of Allah upon them. But my speech here, considering that this text precedes belief from this aspect, because belief branches out from the Book and the Sunnah, we must address an introduction that serves as an incubator for our study of this dialogue, and these chapters that I previously mentioned in the days.

We do not want to know the virtue of knowledge from the verses, nor do we want to know the virtue of knowledge from the infallible texts. And we do not want to hear the speech of the wise men, philosophers, scholars, and people of virtue and wisdom. These also their speech is considered from the sources from which we know the virtue of knowledge, all of them are from the masters of knowledge, because they are the people of expertise. On any account, the philosophers, the speech of the wise men, the speech of the procedural scholars.

Rather, we want to look at the life around us. And we want to look at this conscience (Wajdan). And the intended meaning of conscience, in a precise phrase according to the philosophers and logicians this word, the term 'conscience', which reflects the view of the philosophers and the view of the logicians in the theory of the origin of the intelligible (Asl al-Ma'qul), the source of the sources of knowledge, perhaps. But in launching the reality before addressing the sources from which human knowledge springs in this role I am speaking of: **The intended meaning is this state of sensation inside the human, which senses within himself, between him and his internal reality, between the fluctuations of his soul, that he has the ability to distinguish between things!**

This internal psychological ability present in the living human who lives the structural life inwardly, overall he is capable of distinguishing between things, even if just by approximation. Now, look at this life that we live. Look at the conscience that I mentioned, regardless of the verses of the Book, regardless of the texts of the infallibles, regardless of the sayings of the wise men and philosophers. Look at this life, as a result we are existing in truth. Meaning there is something resembling a truth. We are existing truths, we are created beings existing on this earth. And we are in this human system. And we live upon it.

And this life around us, whether the present life—I do not say the future, because the future is in the realm of unseen matters, and the research does not address the issues that are taken from before—but the present life, the existence which we witness, or the past life lived by human generations, whether in the present, or harvested by history books whether inside the presence of events or the history books regarding the ancient past. This present life, and that past life lived by advanced generations of humans who lived on this earth: **There is a reality, and there is a truth by which the human was created: life cannot continue without knowledge!**

It should not come to mind that the intended meaning of knowledge here is the science of mathematics, the science of jurisprudence, or absolute knowledge. This architecture if the human did not have knowledge of mathematics, this architecture would not have been built in this coordinated, beautiful manner. Noticing the tall, the short, the thick, the thin. If the human did not have knowledge of a thing and do not let the Western theories say that the human, by virtue of experience, as they say, that "necessity is the mother of invention," this is false speech!

For example, this tailoring, who taught people the tailoring of clothes? Our life, or building? Building, another prophet upon our Prophet and him be peace, he taught them tailoring, and writing, and measuring. Idris, as in the narrations. A multitude of those who taught people were prophets. Who taught people the management of states? And many of the communities that the Prophet, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, taught the people in life. The previous prophets were suited for it, that there are some moral issues, so he came with the methodology, to teach people how to live, how a methodology to take people out of the life of animals.

And the Imam of our Time, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him, will come to verify upon Ali, to take the human out to the human life, to the Husseini life which the Lord desires, upon any difference in the hadith regarding the subject. We want to look at the virtue of knowledge through looking at life, through looking at the human conscience, regardless of whether there are intellectual, rational proofs, or proofs from rational premises.

Look at this life naturally, while we live in this life this life without knowledge, the human cannot enjoy it. It is a natural issue, the human scholar leaves his house in the morning and enjoys life. Regardless, the Quran says whether he is a Muslim or a polytheist, without a scholar, it is impossible! Yes, now without the knowledge of carpentry, we would not have been able to benefit from these tools manufactured without the knowledge of medicine. Without the knowledge of herbs, we would not have been able to perform surgeries without the knowledge of this brain. We would not have been able without the knowledge of cooking! The intended meaning of knowledge is anything related to the human, such that the ignorant one who cooks food is a scholar compared to this city-dweller who does not know how!

If the human did not learn cooking, if the human did not learn cooking, he could not manage this system. If the human did not learn the issue of challenging and the first one who taught the human And thus, if he did not learn cooking, the first who established it and therefore, from among the legislative preparations, from among the legislative sciences, it is not permissible to eat food without it. But from among the recommended legislative sciences, in which the narrations came regarding the stability of people, the preparation of food and building.

On any account, if he did not learn if the human did not learn how to build houses, these things around us would remain a knot in all aspects. Otherwise, if the human did not have the knowledge to transform this stone into iron, to transform this element into this man this stone would remain. And it would remain such that the human does not benefit from it. But there is a scientific issue through which the human managed to preserve life. And now, scientific development, technical development in the countries the countries where there is a lot of advanced contemporary technical development, general progress, we find these countries where there is this progress are different from other countries. Why in these countries more than others? We find the welfare in these countries is returning to progress, knowledge.
Segment 2: The Two Branches of Science - Welfare and Organization

Sheikh al-Ghizzi deepens his analysis by demonstrating that knowledge is the true root of all prosperity; even wealth is useless without the knowledge to manage it. He then categorizes all human sciences into two fundamental branches. The first branch consists of sciences that bring "welfare" or comfort to life, such as physics, chemistry, and medicine. However, he warns that if left unchecked, these sciences inevitably lead to conflict and destruction as people compete for resources. This necessitates the second branch: the "organizing" sciences, such as law, ethics, and sociology. The Sheikh establishes that without the organizing sciences to govern human behavior, the welfare-bringing sciences would ultimately destroy humanity.

Why in these countries more than others? We find the welfare in these countries is returning to progress to knowledge. The modern devices that eased the hardships of daily life, their reference is to knowledge. The ease of travel today, compared to the old difficulty if the human did not have knowledge that these animals are prepared, he would have chosen the horse or the camel. Why did he choose the camel? Why did he differentiate between the camel for riding and carrying loads? Because of knowledge. The advanced sciences taught them these matters. The contemporary sciences have changed life and changed these matters completely.

But the result is that the one who possesses knowledge, whether he is an individual or has information, little or much, can enjoy life to the extent of that knowledge he possesses. Yes, perhaps in the general street it is said that money is the cause money is the cause of welfare in his life. **But money without knowledge, the human loses it!** When he does not have the know-how, does not have the expertise and knowledge in managing this financial wealth Knowledge remains the primary tool. This wealthy person benefited from the money because he knows how to manage it.

This doctor, perhaps he enjoys health in his life more than others because the doctor has knowledge of managing his life, the methods of microbes entering the human body, the causes of some diseases And likewise, the manager who manages the sale more than others moves in matters he knows, while perhaps others commit mistakes why? Due to his lack of knowledge and lack of understanding. In all of this, the summary we reach is that knowledge for any human brings benefit to him. And the human does not want to search for fatigue; he wants the causes that assist him in leading life to be a scholar. Therefore, the human, if he wakes up in the morning needs to traverse life, and the human cannot live the good life that suits him except through knowledge.

Now, any person look at the ignorant human who does not possess a craft. Even the Arabs say: "So-and-so has a craft." Why doesn't he use it? Because he has the knowledge of the craft. But this one who does not know why doesn't he work? Because he does not possess the knowledge of the craft. And this issue is clear to humans. So we are in the virtue of knowledge. And we notice that knowledge has an essentiality in the life of the human, in the life that accompanies them, that faces them, that establishes stability.

And we must direct ourselves to the circle of knowledge into the sciences. We find that sciences are divided into religious and worldly sciences, regardless of the research But we find that the human who possesses it—whether Christian, Jewish, Buddhist, or Muslim, every religion has its beliefs—every human seeks a sound, dignified life. We find that the basic things that lead to this the human who is immersed in this life needs an introduction, and the introduction is knowledge. So it is necessary the sciences are large and of various kinds, but generally, we can divide them into two parts.

We find sciences that bring welfare to life. Economics comes to treat a problem around a group, a country, or the world, to move this country from an economic crisis, placing solutions, and the economy of the country transforms into a good state. This economics moved the country from a state of distress to a state of welfare. Sociology when the sociologist comes, he identifies the social problem and places a treatment for this problem. If the social problem is known in the working class a social problem Sociology comes and diagnoses this problem, is it a problem of the elderly, a problem of the youth? When he diagnoses it with a treatment, this treatment moves this group [to a better state].

And likewise, no science among the sciences causes harm You might say these military sciences, these military sciences cause harm, cause terror! Even these weapons, when used by this party to strike that party these weapons, as a result, were produced to get rid of an enemy to achieve peace. Now, regardless of the legislative rulings, we are dealing with the human as he lives on earth, regardless of everything, because this research is an introduction. Even the one who strikes true, striking the opponent [causes harm], but as a result, the one who used the weapon seeks to raise the welfare of the country, seeking to achieve victory.

As a result, sciences generally—whether used in the aspect of evil or good—the intended meaning of knowledge is that it has an impact. We do not want to enter into the moral aspect of using knowledge, the goals of knowledge. But as a result, we find sciences that cause welfare in the life of the human, from these worldly sciences And these sciences that cause welfare, without other sciences, would actually be a cause for their mutual killing! Because humanity learned the sciences that cause welfare in human life: like the science of physics, chemistry, and astronomy and the other sciences were learned. And if it wanted to use this science to acquire the benefit of others, there will be conflict!

Therefore, there must be something other than welfare and to build this welfare that resulted. So the second group of sciences are those that organize! Regardless of the divine laws, looking at the human the problems of the world: Law organizes life. Psychology, ethics. I do not mean Islamic ethics, the ethics considered by countries, European or Western, making ethics an absolute good, whether for the European or the Westerner. Law, psychology, ethics, and the sciences that branch out from these sciences sociology. These sciences organize human life.

The supposed the supposed is that the human adheres to this power, considering positive laws, economic laws. But the rational requirement is that if the human adhered to these laws the laws of sociology, the laws of law, the laws of psychology **if he adhered to their conditions, the conflict that occurs due to the welfare brought by the first group of sciences would be lifted from the world!** But people by their nature need to be organized and therefore, difficulties and governments are the ones that try to apply the laws whether their application is sound or unsound. We are looking at the issue from its root.

So there are sciences that bring welfare, and sciences that organize. But these sciences that brought welfare to life, if they were alone they would cause destruction. **So they are in need of organizing sciences. And from here, the organizing sciences have the greatest merit in organizing!** And the organizing sciences are indispensable meaning if the human did not possess the sciences that bring welfare, but possessed the sciences that organize life these sciences would work in the absence of the sciences that bring welfare to human life.

But in the absence of psychology, sociology, and law, to organize the life of humanity—regardless of the presence or absence of religion—the benefit of the welfare sciences in the life of people because the material motive will direct the force of these first sciences, and the fame, and the control of these sciences. Otherwise, these sciences are beneficial to the human. Therefore, looking at the conscience: which sciences benefit people the most? The organizing sciences are needed. **And if all other sciences were lost, these sciences would become the most beneficial sciences in every aspect.** The sciences that organize.

So we found knowledge upon two bases: a basis that brings welfare to human life, and a basis that organizes these sciences. We find that these sciences that organize human life, when they were and up to today, if we wanted to look at them, we also find them to be human-made, positive sciences. We are humans and have sciences. And from the sciences we find that these sciences are human sciences. Its owner makes them psychology, sociology, these sciences positive law to organize life. **And there are Divine Sciences!**
Segment 3: The Flaws of Human Sciences and the Rational Proof for the Hereafter

Sheikh al-Ghizzi compares human-made organizing sciences with divine sciences. He notes that human sciences constantly change their foundational principles based on eras and interests, whereas divine sciences are immutable. Crucially, human sciences only address worldly life and assume existence ends at death. He argues logically that since materialists cannot definitively prove that human existence ends at death—just as a broken chair's matter still exists in another form—the mere probability of an afterlife requires a rational person to prepare for it. He further proves this innate accountability through the human conscience (Wajdan) and the "reproaching soul" (Nafs Lawwamah), citing how even the most hardened tyrants and executioners suffer from inner terror and psychological torment.

If we study the characteristics of the sciences created by humans, we find that the sciences created by humans aside from the change in their foundations (Usul) as a result of general sciences, sociology, and human psychology. If you read into the foundations followed by scholars in their methodologies, for example, the advanced classes and the contemporary classes, you will find a great shift in the foundations upon which they were built. We do not want to discuss everything, but we say: aside from the change in their foundations—considering we have two groups of sciences organizing humanity: human sciences and divine sciences—we find that there is a change in their scientific foundations.

And even the change of time after that I do not want to delve into this with them, but we find that **these human sciences claim that they organize the worldly life of the human.** Meaning the assumption that accompanies them is the human being alone in this world. Therefore, the human sciences presented recently in this worldly life there is a change in their foundations. Even according to the interests of the world, a change in stance occurs! As for the foundations of the divine sciences, they do not change. There is a vast difference with the science whose foundations change.

We do not want to debate the scientific value of human sciences they share the goal with divine sciences in organizing human life. But the crucial stance is that the scope of these human sciences ends when? At death! Now, if the human is annihilated after death if the human is annihilated after death, then even if he chose human sciences, it might be an alliance if he is annihilated. And if we believe in this reality, then humanity has benefited from leadership. But if the human does not know? And here he will transition to human sciences based on the second probability In the case of the human, there can be an understanding between human science on one hand, and the divine science regarding worldly life on the other.

Otherwise, if we wanted to enter into the comparison, human life ends at death. We find that the laws of human science for worldly life And if we wanted to conduct a scientific comparison, according to scientific rules, between the rules laid down by the human in the sciences that organize human life, and the rules present in the divine science to present human choices **we find that the scientific laws deposited in the divine science have no equal in scientific debate!** It is an intuitive, conscience-based (Wajdani) issue, as we said from the beginning to the end regarding this matter.

The human proceeds upon this probability. With that, he can take the divine science. But the divine science and the human science are equal in the case that the human is annihilated after death; they evaluate ideas and domains of human science in presenting this goal. **But what human possesses definitive proof of the annihilation of the human after death?** Becoming dust does this mean evidence of non-existence? This does not indicate the annihilation of the human into dust. The existence of the human body in the dust does not indicate annihilation; rather, this indicates that the human exists with us in another form or state!

If the human was only matter, we would have found Why? Because we have not seen annihilation even for, say, a chair. For example, if you break a chair, it is just broken it remains wood or matter. As for us, the body of the human tears apart, it may tear apart from a previous season This does not indicate exception. The Arab sage, when he dies if the human was only matter As the naturalist or materialist says If the human was in a state where the mountain and nature produced this body The mountain does not end by crumbling, and there is no proof for the annihilation of the mountain. **So there is no definitive proof of the annihilation of the human after death!**

He who ponders deeply in this life, and finds this management in the affairs of life he who ponders inevitably reaches this conclusion. The human must contribute, because there is an inner issue. And this issue deposited inside **it is the message of the reproaching soul (Nafs Lawwamah), shared approximately in the conscience of the human!** Especially if he remembers mistakes against life. Considering the speech about the religious human, even if he commits sins, his conscience remains alive.

And therefore, we find steps Even this matter it says that whoever starts his soul debating against history it is clear from this sort. Many of those in Iraq who practice execution, who entered prisons, who practice torture and execution, especially in the beginnings they hate to fall into it, they anticipate torture or sharp operations. This issue is known because there is something the human reaches the issue of the conscience remains! Therefore, the one who drops the bomb merely upon seeing the injury of the soldiers And these phenomena in the lives of criminals throughout history are clear.

Therefore, in history, many of the butchers in the recent history of the world the issue of torturing the conscience moved many streets. Therefore, Ali, peace be upon him, when he says the human can see truth as falsehood and falsehood as truth. If he says this matter, then Allah will not give him the ability to distinguish between these two matters And therefore, Al-Hajjaj bin Yusuf al-Thaqafi. Al-Hajjaj believed and mentioned that Abd al-Malik bin Marwan is better than the Messenger of Allah! He stands before the people in Mecca, saying: "O people, a messenger and his deputy is not the deputy of the man better than the messenger? And Abd al-Malik is better than the Messenger of Allah." He who believes in the general worldview **but what happened with Sa'id bin Jubayr in the final moments of his life?**
These testimonies many of the opponents of the Ahlulbayt **Therefore, when the human is created, he is given the ability through which he distinguishes between truth and falsehood!**
Segment 4: The Necessity of Divine Sciences and the Roots of Religion

Sheikh al-Ghizzi concludes the episode by demonstrating that because human sciences cannot disprove the afterlife, a rational person is compelled to prepare for it. This realization forces humanity to transition from limited, human-made sciences to Divine Sciences. He explains that these Divine Sciences, specifically within the religion of Islam, are structured into branches (Furu') and constant foundations (Usul). These foundational constants are what we term the "Roots of Religion" (Usul al-Din) or our Core Beliefs ('Aqa'id). The episode ends with the Sheikh promising to explore the different methodologies for acquiring this vital religious knowledge in the next episode.

And these choices for the human there is that which they believe, or at least **The speech inevitably transitions from the circle of human sciences to the circle of Divine Sciences!** As for this one who remains in a state of focus, at the very least he must acquire it. So he needs to traverse the path of Divine Sciences, to learn. Considering that the best thing in life is knowledge! And knowledge by human means is for the welfare of life, and the best of it are the sciences that organize life. But the sciences that organize life, when made by human hands the best sciences from these two groups are the Divine Sciences! So the human needs the Divine Sciences.

These Divine Sciences, like other sciences, have foundational principles (Usuliyyat), necessities that in their totality form that science, and branches (Furu') of the science. All evidences, all Divine Sciences in every religion—in our religion, in all religions—are necessary, not compulsory. Others see no religion and no need for the soul, those who put an end to religion the issue is clear, we do not need to pause and say that our religion is the true religion and other religions are false religions; we have a general discussion regarding this.

With divine knowledge, if one adorns himself in the Divine Sciences, all of us in the demands of divine knowledge the premises have led us to the fact that the religion of Islam is the true religion. If we enter into the circle of Divine Sciences and search within the circle of sciences and the fluctuations of religion—regardless of human sciences—considering the Divine Sciences, **our religion consists of roots (Usul) and branches (Furu'). The branches emerge from the roots. The roots are the constants (Thawabit), and they are what, in their totality, preserve the religion!**

We call these foundations of the divine science the "Roots of Religion" (Usul al-Din). And this is what we name the intellect, and this is what we Muslims call belief (I'tiqad)! And since the roots are obligations, then the knowledge of them must be a scientific knowledge. However, there are different paths (Masalik) in acquiring this information. God willing, I will explain them to you tonight or they said perhaps the coming night, because in that we will complete the speech. This coming night, the truths regarding time Now, God willing, next time I will complete the speech regarding the paths that differed in acquiring the information of the human in his belief, in the roots of the Muslim human. We suffice with this extent. And our final prayer is praise be to Allah.

title: Our Shia Beliefs - Introduction Part 2: The Methodologies of Acquiring Belief
speaker: Sheikh Abdul-Halim al-Ghizzi

content.text: Overview: Episode 2

In the second introductory episode of Our Shia Beliefs, Sheikh al-Ghizzi continues to explore the rational necessity of seeking Divine Sciences. Building upon the previous episode's conclusion, he emphasizes that since human sciences cannot disprove the afterlife, humanity must turn to Divine Sciences to secure both worldly welfare and eternal salvation. The core of this episode focuses on dissecting the various **methodologies (Asalib)** used throughout history to acquire and prove core Islamic beliefs (Usul al-Din). He systematically critiques the **Theological Method (Kalam)**, the **Philosophical Method (Falsafah)**, and the **Sufi Method (Tasawwuf)**, highlighting the flaws, shifting definitions, and primary sources of evidence each group relies upon. He sets the stage to discuss further methodologies, including Mysticism ('Irfan) and the Scripturalist (Muhaddithin) approaches, leading up to the true methodology derived exclusively from the Ahlulbayt.
Segment 1: Recap - The Rational Need for Divine Sciences

Sheikh al-Ghizzi begins by summarizing the previous episode's argument. He reiterates that purely through human conscience and observation—without initially relying on religious texts—we recognize that knowledge is essential for a safe and prosperous life. However, since human-made sciences only address this temporary world and cannot disprove the existence of an afterlife, a rational person is compelled to adopt Divine Sciences to guarantee safety and prosperity in both realms. Thus, understanding the "Roots of Religion" (Usul al-Din) becomes a logical necessity.

Last night the speech arrived at the Roots. However, the Prophet and the Infallibles, prayers of Allah upon them, quickly stated that the knowledge of belief and the knowledge of the future is among the types of knowledge. And it is among the types of gnosis. We began the introduction without relying on the Book, nor on the infallible texts, nor on the sayings of the wise men regarding the benefits and specifics in explaining the virtue of knowledge.

The intended texts address the other aspect, considering that we are tackling this problem based on our reliance upon the noble verses and the honorable narrations. When I mention the path that the human takes by himself, regardless of other factors, we must therefore cast a glance at past human expertise. And this conscience (Wajdan) which everyone possesses must be the judge, so we find that knowledge has a major role in human life, and it is what brings welfare and safety to his life through this surpassing view of life.

And when it became clear that knowledge has a major role in the safety of life—regardless of whether this knowledge is religious or knowledge in general—therefore, through worldly examples, the human should look at the sciences as a group of fields and a group of sciences. And after research, it became clear that the sciences that advance the human are better because they construct the human in the presence of the organizing sciences. In their absence, the sciences that bring welfare to human life without the organizing sciences might be devastating. As for the organizing sciences, therefore, at the very least, the sciences that organize are essential, so it was found that they organize human dissemination.

Whenever we consider knowledge, we find that it is the path of Allah, delivered through the prophets and the Awliya (saints). We found that human sciences only address the life of this worldly span. Conversely, we found that the Divine Sciences address human life, the worldly span, and what is beyond. Here it arises that if the human cancels the highest afterlife, the research in this issue is built upon the fact that the human, as a result of researching this issue in the surrounding circumstances, does not possess a single proof that he is annihilated after death. Regarding this view he holds, as for the human and the non-human, do not say annihilation! Otherwise, if death meant the human is annihilated, meaning the afterlife is non-existent, Allah is above this speech.

I want to bypass the line of many. After that day, realizing that there is a world after, or at least taking the path that believes or demands from the living to hold onto it. Knowledge and the sciences that enrich life came from Allah through His Awliya. When we come to the substance of the sciences, they must undoubtedly be distinct from the sciences found among the Christians or the fighting, as the issue is clear.

Islam made the Infallibles the standard, but when we came to the Divine Sciences, we found that these divine sciences have foundations (Usul) and branches (Furu'). In asking about the sciences, all sciences have definitive foundations. In truth, **the foundations of the science of religion represent the true picture of the religious science.** This is the rewarding picture we have, which the believer holds, and therefore studying the foundations is the greatest endeavor. He is the one who was told that we want to study the foundations of the religious science. This is because it provides worldly benefit and otherworldly benefit hereafter.

We provided this introduction without relying on a scholar or an infallible transmission, but rather through looking at life. Starting from there, we must realize a sentence because the ancient sciences that were a cause for leadership in this world act conversely in the next world. Therefore we proceed.
Segment 2: The Theological Method ('Ilm al-Kalam)

The Sheikh shifts to the core topic: the different methods used by scholars to establish these religious foundations (Usul al-Din). He begins with the Theological Method ('Ilm al-Kalam), primarily used by the Mutakallimun (theologians). He explains that theologians define the "Roots of Religion" (Monotheism, Prophethood, Imamate, Justice, and Resurrection) and attempt to prove them using primarily definitive rational proofs (Dalil 'Aqli Qat'i) and some textual evidence. However, he critiques this method, pointing out that what they consider "definitive reason" is often disputed, and their reliance on textual evidence (Dalil Naqli) is inconsistent, leading to severe disagreements even within their own ranks.

If we look at the board, the paths, the theories, the books, or the intellectual feelings, we find there are multiple different teachings in the course of the current year. If we had the time, we would have spoken about each method for perhaps a segment or more than that. The first method in knowing beliefs and detailing the truths is the theological method (Uslub al-Kalam). This is the method relied upon by the scholars of theology, the Mutakallimun, from the early Mutakallimun and the science of Dirayah, who rely on the theological method in reaching the Hereafter.

We evaluate the path upon some methods like the theological method. As a result of every science, the subject of theology (Kalam) and its core issues in our view deals with the study of the known, famous foundations of the world.

The general populace imagines that this issue is agreed upon in a way, such that among the established things with them is that the foundations are five: **Monotheism (Tawhid), Prophethood (Nubuwwah), Imamate (Imamah), Justice ('Adl), and the Return (Ma'ad).** But there is no agreement that these are the only ones. Rather, there are some scholars who limit the foundations of religion into Monotheism, the angels, and the unseen. Meaning they say, "in Allah and His angels" as mentioned in the honorable verses that spoke about considering belief.

Some scholars make the foundations of religion solely Monotheism and Prophethood. They argue that Justice is included within the result. Justice is included in the issue of Imamate. What remains is Prophethood and the issue of the Return (Ma'ad). We do not know the Return except through Prophethood, and Imamate is a branch of Prophethood. Therefore, the foundations of religion stem from Monotheism.

This difference is merely a difference in numbers. Otherwise, the final result remains the same. He says belief in Imamate, Prophethood, and the Resurrection is required. Despite this difference, we have a clear narration that says its foundations are five, affirming the Justice of Monotheism.

This is what he extracted through the texts regarding the fear of the Infallibles and the hadith in details. In the first method, the theological method, considerations of a science during adopting theology aim at reaching the knowledge of human truths. Belief and all theology in the view of the Imamiyyah represents knowledge in these matters.

Therefore, what the scholars of the Imamiyyah wrote in the science of theology is based on placing the administration of Monotheism, Prophethood, Imamate, Justice, and the Return. Under this title, there are other sub-titles that enter the record of truth. The foundation which theology relies upon asks: what is the proof in the things they establish proofs for? **The definitive rational proof (Al-Dalil al-'Aqli al-Qat'i)!** They wanted the reasonable definitive rational proof, meaning the necessary and intuitive proof. The definitive rational proof is that upon which all rational people agree! This is the first type of evidence.

The second proof is the Book and the texts that indicate what the people said. Regarding the linguistic use of the word Wajdan and the use of the text in the science of theology or the science of the foundations of jurisprudence (Usul al-Fiqh), they establish the first proof as the major rational proof. The second proof is intuitiveness. The third proof is the hadith, specifically the widely transmitted (Mutawatir) text. They agreed upon the widely transmitted hadith in the locus of theology and accept the Mutawatir.

Regarding the textual proof (Dalil Naqli), disagreement occurred! Because the textual proof relies upon the countries of the Messenger, the regions and the Arabs differed among themselves. Therefore, the evidences that theology relies upon today are the definitive rational proof and conscience. They may rely on the textual proof in some matters, but generally, the method relied upon today in studying beliefs considers the definitive rational proof, intuitiveness, and reliance upon the widely transmitted hadith. Researching the Mutawatir is an external report reviewing Dirayah, which enters into Dirayah after verifying the Mutawatir.

In the science of unseen theology ('Ilm Ghayb al-Kalam), they agreed that the science of theology has doors of success. The science of theology evolved in a broad manner through an experience from the time of Sheikh Al-Mufid to the time of Sheikh Al-Tusi in this period. According to the Islamic scholars, the nature of the chapters, the foundations, its rules, and its terminologies led up to the time of the luminaries in Baghdad. Al-Allamah triumphed much in this science, meaning it did not exist before Islam in the Pre-Islamic era (Jahiliyyah). Therefore I said that the subject of the science of theology is these five foundations.

In the narrations, we find that the Imams say the outcome of this affair does not cancel out. This meaning has been repeated in several narrations and the concept is clear. The narration says that the science of theology is divided upon the decision of the sons of the earth and upon the opinions of others. **This is a science which does not return in its laws, foundations, or chapters to what Allah says!** This is a quick picture of the method of the Mutakallimun in reaching the truth.
Segment 3: The Philosophical Method (Falsafah)

Sheikh al-Ghizzi moves on to the second method: Philosophy (Falsafah). He traces its origins to Greek thinkers, noting that true wisdom originally came from divine prophets, but was later distorted by philosophers. Unlike theologians who define specific religious roots, philosophers search broadly for the reality of existence and the "First Cause." The Sheikh highlights a critical difference in their reliance on evidence: philosophers elevate the rational proof (Dalil 'Aqli) to the absolute highest tier, valuing their own intellectual deductions over any scriptural text (Dalil Naqli). Because philosophers rely primarily on abstract reasoning, they frequently misinterpret or dismiss clear divine texts that contradict their theories.

The second method is the philosophical method in reaching the knowledge of the Hereafter. The first method is the method of the Mutakallimun, the method of theology. The second method is the philosophical method. It existed from before Islam. The philosophical methods which originated from the Greeks. Before the luminaries of the well-known things among the philosophers, the sect of the Greek philosophers as it is said that this philosophy, its origin is from Allah. And in a terminology of those who are named the Wise of the Wise (Hakim al-Hukama) meaning in philosophy. And he was trained upon our Prophet and upon him be the best of prayers and peace. Because the existence of wisdom is an infallible issue and he is the one upon whose policy they rely first that from the students of the prophets.

Before Islam it existed in rules, and it was not only in the Greek world, but rather before Islam in peace the external Indian philosophy and the Persian, and the Indian Persians. And whoever is in the Greek work due to what it was distinguished by from Arabs, and from the Indian and the Persian.

Segment 4: Mulla Sadra and the Flaws of Attempting to Merge Philosophy with Religion

Continuing his critique of the philosophical method, Sheikh al-Ghizzi specifically addresses Sadr al-Din al-Shirazi (Mulla Sadra), a prominent Shii philosopher. He argues that Mulla Sadra attempted to forcefully merge Greek philosophy with Islamic texts, effectively twisting religious scriptures to fit philosophical molds. Segment 5: The Sufi Method (Tasawwuf) and the Illusion of "Unveiling" (Kashf)

The Sheikh introduces the third method: Sufism (Tasawwuf). He explains that Sufism revolves around the concept of divine love ('Ishq) and attempting to achieve spiritual "unveiling" (Kashf) through rigorous ascetic practices. He notes a critical flaw in this method: the absolute submission required by a student (the Murid) to his spiritual master (the Shaykh), comparing the student to a "corpse in the hands of the washer." The Sheikh emphasizes that the spiritual visions or "unveilings" claimed by Sufis are entirely subjective and lack objective, textual proof. He warns that what a Sufi perceives as a divine vision could easily be a satanic illusion, making this method highly unreliable for establishing concrete religious truths.

The Sufi method in knowing the truths is a third method that differs from the theological method and the philosophical method. In Sufism, they might differ, and the sects disagreed in determining its subject. But it can be said in sum that the intended meaning is the love of the Divine. Segment 6: The Mystical Method ('Irfan) and Conclusion

In the final segment, Sheikh al-Ghizzi outlines the Mystical Method ('Irfan), carefully distinguishing it from philosophy and Sufism, though acknowledging they share some terminology. He divides 'Irfan into two distinct halves: Theoretical 'Irfan (intellectual frameworks) and Practical 'Irfan (spiritual exercises and self-purification). He criticizes those who merely memorize theoretical mystical terminology without undergoing the actual spiritual purification required to possess the "faculty" of true understanding. title: Our Shia Beliefs - Introduction Part 3: The Methodologies of Acquiring Belief (Continued)
speaker: Sheikh Abdul-Halim al-Ghizzi

content.text: Overview: Episode 3

In the third introductory episode of Our Shia Beliefs, Sheikh al-Ghizzi continues his comprehensive breakdown of the various methodologies used by scholars to acquire and establish Islamic beliefs. He briefly recaps the flaws of the **Theological Method (Kalam)** and the **Philosophical Method (Falsafah)**, before diving deeper into the distinctions between **Sufism (Tasawwuf)** and **Mysticism ('Irfan)**. He heavily critiques those who merely memorize mystical terminology without undergoing the rigorous spiritual purification required for true gnosis. Finally, the Sheikh introduces the method of **Sheikh Ahmad Al-Ahsa'i (the Shaykhi school)** and the method of the **Traditionalists (Muhaddithin)**, touching upon the esoteric sciences of letters and numbers (**Jafr / Numerology**) used to extract deeper meanings from the Quran and Hadith.
Segment 1: Recap of the Theological and Philosophical Methods

Sheikh al-Ghizzi begins the third episode by briefly reviewing the discussions from the previous two sessions. He reiterates that knowing one's religion and beliefs is the fundamental cause for the safety and prosperity of human life. He then recaps the theological method ('Ilm al-Kalam), which relies on definitive rational proofs, widely transmitted (Mutawatir) hadiths, and consensus (Ijma'), though he heavily critiques the reliability of consensus. Following this, he recaps the philosophical method (Falsafah), noting that philosophers prioritize their own abstract rational deductions over the scriptural texts, leading figures like Mulla Sadra to improperly philosophize the religion.

Praise be to Allah, and blessings be upon the Messenger of Allah. In the previous two sessions, I first spoke about the introduction to belief and religion. The outcome reached a specific result, which is that **the cause for the safety of human life is knowing his religion and knowing his beliefs.** Secondly, there are multiple methods to reach the knowledge of the truth through these paths. I pointed out this issue briefly because there are other aspects of the discussion, and I am confident that time does not permit us to delve into all of them. If we wanted to go into all their details independently, we would try as much as humanly possible to limit the speech to the core of the subject in the second degree.

I will point to a summary of what I mentioned regarding the multiple methods. The first method is the theological method (Uslub al-Kalam), which relies on the science of theology to know the truth. This is the science of theology according to the Imamiyyah. Regardless of the general masses, the science of theology according to the Imamiyyah revolves around the five famous foundations alongside the roots of religion. We spoke about this issue. The science of theology took a clear scientific character in the late canonical era, defining its boundaries completely, especially during the time of Sheikh Al-Mufid, Sayyid Al-Murtadha, and Sheikh Al-Tusi, may Allah have mercy on them, and those who came after them.

The evidence varied. The first was the rational proof, distinguishing between the proof of the intellect and the heart in theology. The intended meaning of the text is the Book, the scriptural text, and absolute texts. The science of theology uses the Book as a weapon employed by scholars of theology and scholars of the soul. It is a clear book that does not need this proof. The second proof is the widely transmitted hadith (Mutawatir). The widely transmitted hadith also has detailed discussions, but the intended meaning of a widely transmitted hadith involves the naming of the wording and meaning. It can be found in specialized books that detail the issue of consensus (Ijma').

Consensus is agreed upon by the people of knowledge and practice. Some scholars of theology consider it a proof among the proofs returned to in determining beliefs and reaching the knowledge of the truth. However, there is major debate regarding consensus. Firstly, consensus might cancel the general masses regarding the supreme beliefs of the truth and the methods used to look into this issue. Beyond that, many scholars of theology have multiplied the types of consensus and its definitions regarding its authoritative proof. In our present time, scholars vary in accepting or rejecting it. Otherwise, consensus is considered a proof.

Perhaps in this regard, it is among the necessary issues, even if contemporary scholars limit it to this extent. The early jurists and scholars stated that an issue is necessary among the necessities for the theologians who divide the ideological necessities clearly. Among the necessary issues that jurists agree upon are the necessities, considering that the consensus of jurists leads to the necessity of the issue. From here also comes the speech that they consider consensus as leading to the necessity of the religious issue without it. When we say an issue is necessary in religion, it means if it were removed, it is said that people would fall.

But the general public at the present time focuses on this issue, considering that social issues were general. So if it is based on this foundation, it is a great country. The technical consensus spread in scientific studies requires one to act upon the detailed segments. How can those preoccupied with internal scientific acquisition from a young age rely on this crisis, and then the consensus? This issue has details. Therefore, I said in the beginning of my speech that I am speaking positively because if we wanted to go into all these details to clarify the basic subjects, it would be another issue.

I repeat to the brothers that they should not imagine that scholars of theology rely on innovation or rely on the definitive rational proof and the widely transmitted hadith. Perhaps they respect the true widely transmitted hadith, considering that the widely transmitted hadith in the view of theologians is definitive. However, there is the definitive hadith which establishes the definitive truth from the omitted council of scholars. This means it is definitive in its content and meaning, and the benefits of the issue have established its issuance from the infallible. This, even if it is widely transmitted, is relied upon in theological demands, especially in legislation. Many theologians rely on this.

If we want to review Allamah Al-Hilli, the Sheikh, or the Shia books, the good scholars who wrote in the science of theology have a large number of discussions. Perhaps they respect the truly widely transmitted reports according to the terminology. Without the scientific terminology, it does not resolve transmission. They rely on reports that we can call, according to the terminology of Dirayah, as definitively issued from the infallible. This is a difference in the space of theology.

We spoke about the second method, the philosophical method (Uslub al-Falsafah). We discussed the ancient practical, Greek, and other philosophical schools. We spoke about the subject of the states of existence according to the philosophers. There is the rational proof. **The rational proof in its first rank is the definitive rational proof present in theology.** The same definitive rational proof present in theology is a proof in the second rank for them. And the proof is nothing but the intellect from the philosophical intellect.

As we transmitted, this proof is closer to the rational people. The philosophical rational proof is the intellect of the philosophers, meaning the intellects of the general people, but the intellects of philosophers. We clarified that the one who speaks and there is a successive meaning the unity of Iraq. And he looked at the prophets. And upon it are the problems and the defenses against these problems and so on. Philosophers have a path of theories. As for the one who possesses the faculty, he has the ability to comprehend what is beyond nature. The ability to conduct what is said in the proverb regarding the rest of the country's reform in what is beyond nature. From three sources, the rational proof is accepted, suitable and advancing upon the rational proof present in theology. The rational proof is in the second path, the proof which harms the municipal intellect which you decide.

Secondly, the meditations of the philosopher who possesses the faculty in highlighting what is beyond nature. This speech at its core needs great detailing. The context of detailing when we say an ideological path intended is the Greek period, the ancient philosophy which was noble. Otherwise, the philosophy can establish a path to reach the truth as Sadr al-Muta'allihin said. As he said, to say religion and the religion of cost. Meaning upon the different jurisprudential demands upon the narrations and goals, and making the unseen demands legitimate by the nominal demands is clear. The schools which can be a proof can be a path to reach the knowledge of the intellect. Philosophy is not absolute, before and forever. But the philosophy which means philosophy, as they say, by way of the Mutahallihin, may Allah have mercy on him, leads this team to the philosophical method.
Segment 2: Sufism and Mysticism

Sheikh al-Ghizzi outlines the Sufi method, noting that it focuses on divine love ('Ishq) and spiritual unveiling (Kashf). He distinguishes this from Mysticism ('Irfan), which is divided into theoretical and practical branches. He emphasizes that true gnosis ('Irfan) is rooted in spiritual states and rigorous self-purification, not just memorizing terminology. He also critiques how some mystics rely heavily on the words of their spiritual masters (Shaykhs) over clear scriptural texts.

The second reason is the Sufi method. It was said that mysticism differs from Sufism, and Sufism revolves around this speech. We also discussed the evidences of the Sufis and the mystics. The intended meaning of the mystics is the media upon the truth. There are mystics who claim to be from the illiterate and immediately go from falling upon the truth to falsehood, and he is upon the law of the Imams, peace be upon them. These people we envision from a country of anger. This speech is according to decisions through the role model who reached his Lord.

The proof for that is the evidence which encompasses the greatest ideological proof, considering he reached the international level. As for he who did not reach, these are the ones who do not have these details. The first is the Book for the Sufi. Meaning there is Sufism which accesses the technical texts and other benefits generally through scriptural and infallible texts. Firstly, the conclusion of the speech of the Sheikh and how the Sheikh in my view is the great scholar professor to others. That is the early student in the view of the general mystics. Therefore his speech is the reality.

I say firstly the Book and the Sunnah, the speech of the Sheikh. And thirdly, high ranks. As for he who did not reach, it does not mean that the Sheikh considers this unveiling as correct. And there is a difference in belief. As a result, there is also other speech. He calls for the beginning of Sufi loyalty as a necessity in the search for falsehood as a part of the parts. But this preparatory decision is the one mentioned, and unveiling according to them is what is produced in the human after strong spiritual exercises.

Here we even spoke about the theological method in reaching the belief. We spoke about the philosophical method in reaching the belief. And we spoke about the Sufi method in reaching the belief. Last week we entered into some habits to generalize the benefit. The fourth method is the mystical method (Uslub al-'Irfan). The mystical method is based on the science of mysticism. Some prolonged their answers and wrote, perhaps considering the greatest in the university in the issue of mysticism, Sufism, and jurisprudence, and made the generalities one thing. And this honorable clash is a thing, and equalization and memorization. Perhaps even some scholars enter into the study of matters as one, judging logic and Sufism and the external.

Notice a little while ago that the subject of philosophy is the states of the existent. The subject of Sufism is love. The subject of mysticism is knowing the Truth (Al-Haqq). The subject of the human is the Truth, these matters. Yes, perhaps they are not Arabs. And the sciences sometimes have resignations from the rest of the world. Otherwise, some jurisprudential issues will not exit through the science. Sometimes it relies on the science of mathematics and science in general, contemporary sciences, words about in general.

The scholar buried the science of electronics because it is from the contemporary worldly sciences unless it is connected to the science of electronics. This deterioration which occurred in antibiotics would not have happened if there was no development in the science of electronics. This overlap and decision between the sciences exists, but the correlation of sciences from the days of the union some say the direction means the subject, the goals, and the purpose. The science of electronics is never a subject, nor does it mean that mathematics meets the science of medicine in a blockade in a large number of experiments conducted by various scholars. He needs the science of mathematics in the permissibility of the issue of calculating probabilities in Iraq. And this issue in its path is a mathematical issue to what drops the woman or when the roles occur inside mathematics. It relies on mathematics, and so on.

He said if the sciences are from a certain aspect, it does not mean we have unity. That physics meets with the science of mysticism, and meets with Sufism. In this relationship, the three meet. Perhaps these sciences, the meeting of Sufism, philosophy, and mysticism, meet in some aspects. And sometimes they do not meet with the other science. These are distinct and different sciences. There is a result present or to approximate the knowledge to the minds in some others.

Therefore, the three scholars of the country say we have existing details. Now there are chapters of legislations in the science of mathematics, chapters of legislations of theories by the advent of the month of sciences. These legislations and these exercises are for the human mind. Why establish necessity upon the human mind? Because these sciences cannot reach the minds in the true sense of the word unless the sciences are shaped this way. These sciences will not be meaningful to the human if he does not learn the definitions in knowing the basics, and knowing the alternatives in every science from the sciences of his country.

From here, wisdom is not from the word, meaning if not for the dimensions. But to produce the true outputs which are the killing, whether this science was in earthly matters or this religious science which customarily flowed to the syndicate of the festival to present. content.text: Overview: Episode 4

In the fourth introductory episode of Our Shia Beliefs, Sheikh al-Ghizzi concludes his evaluation of the various ideological methodologies. He emphatically warns against the **dangers of adopting Greek philosophy and Sufism**, contrasting their speculative theories with the pure, divine teachings of the Imams. By utilizing explicit examples—such as the philosophical misunderstanding of Allah's Will and Volition—he demonstrates how relying on human intellect over divine revelation leads to fundamental theological errors. The Sheikh cites a powerful prophecy from Imam al-Askari to highlight that the most corrupt scholars of the end times are those who abandon the Ahlulbayt in favor of philosophy. Ultimately, he establishes that **true belief is inseparable from conforming action**, and that the only safe refuge for a believer is absolute adherence to the intertwined authorities of the **Quran and the Prophet's progeny ('Itrah)**.

He emphasizes that the Theological Method ('Ilm al-Kalam) remains indispensable for the general Muslim public to grasp the basic, necessary beliefs. However, he critiques theologians for straying into abstract Greek philosophy instead of grounding their arguments strictly in the teachings of the Ahlulbayt.

And he points to a house in which, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him, the truth exists unless you exited. Meaning in his pointing to the house, the house of the Ahlulbayt, and the house of stones. The concept is clear from the word 'the house', meaning the true interior, the true watcher upon him. The Imam of truth, the hands of Allah. And what corresponds to it, and he made so-and-so and so-and-so the path of the Ahlulbayt, prayers of Allah upon them all.

And this is what we accept. And he says as he said, for that is the nourishment, rather the spring of building. And casting it regarding many things unless he exits conversely. But another time, God willing, taking off on a day from the objective conditions, the objective truths, the blackness, the change, the jealousy, God willing. Details, but there is a destiny to it. And these issues and the meeting of the narration, even if the position of speech was greatly expansive. But I point out that the wave, perhaps in the general gatherings or in another time, an overall hadith of the sermon, these demands which firstly are the narrations of Allah upon him.

**And I believe that it is in a narration we find in his narrations. By Allah, the belief came, the truth without the action conforming to the Ahlulbayt, peace be upon them.** And this is what it is, and realizing the belief is a terminology with a proof. This is not valid unless there is a heavy reality upon it. Therefore, narrations other than this, the narration of the leaders of the ten, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him:
Indeed, Allah does not accept an action without knowledge, nor knowledge without an action.
Then we define knowledge upon action, and whoever does not act now the Imam is a part of the belief.

The important thing is, regarding the Imam, some of them do not accept without knowledge, and whoever acts the belief is a part. Therefore, in the hadith which I mentioned a short while ago, it supports this exact meaning: that knowledge to action is a process upon it. The law of correlation is that studying the belief is the conforming action. That knowledge revolts in the external reality of the year. And the human acts upon acting with knowledge, causing him abundance. Indeed, Allah does not give a scientific bestowal without action before, as preceded for us in the past week. And he bequeathed knowledge to other than the knowledge of the scholars of wisdom. Wisdom to other than the knowledge. And the truthful ones bequeathed, and it is whoever acts upon it in the external reality. Meaning that whoever acts is capable of being granted success. That the human, the Creator, the Exalted and Majestic, gives the human the strong memory, gives him the purity. It may be from those methods but knowledge as a result leads to action. Then we know from it, and whoever acts upon it, and knowledge aims for action in the answer.

And action is knowledge. But studying the belief is ruling without action. Perhaps during the necessity and clarifying the praiseworthy from the action which is said inside a great study in giving lessons, this third point which we find in his speech emphasizes the nurturing of the hearts upon the truths, and this is clear. The proof for that is their disciplining in supplication, and their disciplining for the sake of, and their disciplining in the visitation of the King, which is achieved in this huge number. These which are daily segments for the name if they say, meaning it is supposed these supplications are placed daily for them. These visitations are placed either as actions for specific occasions and supplications like specific daily ones, or the waters which contribute. Nurturing the horses upon the belief is sufficient for the government. And if it needs nurturing the government, it does not mean that the intellects do not need nurturing, but nurturing the hearts is the truest nurturing upon the belief. And from here, in our studies of beliefs, we emphasize this aspect more than the other aspect because it is clear in the narration, prayers of Allah upon them. And this meaning we find clearly in their honorable narrations, prayers of Allah upon him.

The third point which also becomes clear through the demands of the government. Therefore the truth is the issue of deterioration. The issue of disintegration. Meaning the human when he realizes the intellect, when he performs the conforming action, the truth is he is also in need of pondering, in need of memorization. But not in the manner of the philosopher, in the manner which He desires: "Do they not ponder the Quran?" Upon other than in a reading, that worship is not the abundance of prayer, the command of Allah if the place is with drawing near, pondering, and thinking in the scriptural, in the honorable texts.

And also a method that aids the human upon achieving the belief and learning the services he mentioned in order to extract the belief from it, because these issues are clear upon the sayings of the hadiths of the Ahlulbayt. We find these meanings and remembrance in every narration. Then the fourth issue, and this is our beloved almost. The completion of the hadith about it remains the final refuge. The first refuge, the final refuge for the human. **The Book and the Prophet, may Allah bless him and his family, the resources, and success, and not by Sufism.**

By imitation (Taqlid) and another issue which is from the Arab, that he is in a rank whose Book of Allah is morning, and the Book of control is not left, he does not wake up. There are no periods there, and therefore from the meanings, and this hadith is not penetrated by the one who wants the Quran without the path of my family, it is clear. This and the Prophet, the two sides gathered between the obstacle and to what they establish as an alternative for the truthful ones in the earth. There are no two honors, the individuals of iron.

The Quran, he says, peace be upon him. He separated between them, he separated between the Book and between the one who separates between the Book and between the periods in a circle if he did not say in numbers. As decided. Otherwise, the first and second politicians separated between Ali and the Quran and rejected the Quran of Ali. The one who presented, and I am the one who wrote it with his influence, he rejected it. Why did he reject Ali? Because he does not desire logic. He does not reflect another politician. And they also want, they want to take the Quran in the Commander of the Faithful, who caused the closing to be accepted, but they did not want there to be a connection between Ali and the understanding. They separated between Ali, and whoever wants to separate between the families and the Quran, he is the one who became Ali.
And the path, our method, our approach, our lives, the great ten in our narrations:
Indeed, this religion, if men enter you into it, other men will take you out of it. But whoever enters into this religion through the Book and the Sunnah, the mountains will vanish before he does.

But whoever entered into this religion through the Book and the Sunnah, whoever entered into this religion, all the men are sayings, he heavily weighed with the saying of the philosopher, for the heart of the distant, the fear. If he entered into this religion with his saying, and this, and this religion does not accept the human leaving it to other men.

As for if this truth in the Book and the Sunnah. Then what? That he separates between and does not exit. And he said you enter as he said, and a slight lesson, meaning murdered, you say with this imitation (Taqlid), from the meanings of imitation. Or from the refutation, from the swearing in this abundance from the renting of the hadith. Imitation was not for the sane to initiate the article of imitation from what is consequently a crime. But from his state, meaning naturally, a piece from the pregnancy by the amount of a kilo, a piece from the iron by the amount of a kilo is weak. There is a resemblance between the throat and the many from the aspect of placement, but from an increase, truly a weapon. The likes of it in the shape, this is the intended Majid, some now in the club, this media, and the observers of the boldness when the external human carries it or the boldness. So he will develop upon the party and not be like that pointing, may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace.

That he is integrated, whoever entered into it, the men, the men of my region, this religion, the Book, did not exit without us saying that we differ with the Book and the Sunnah. As for this and the sayings of theology which if it was conforming to the speech. And sanctified it, estimated it with the value of the human in the honorable narrations, the hadith from the Commander of the Faithful, peace be upon him:
The value of every man is according to what he knows well.
From his sake, in from his sake, according to what they kill. And whoever stands with him, they obtain, he does not kill, so it is the beginning and the end. Allah is the Greatest.

May Allah keep you, Allah. And accompanying the end of Allah, there is no end beyond Allah. In not in the Hereafter.
