And this picture behind me on the TV screen, a picture from the Speicher massacre in Iraq, is in the series of these crimes.

Whoever sets a bad precedent bears its burden and the burden of whoever acts upon it until the Day of Judgment. I do not know how many shackles will be placed on the neck of this criminal, the great devil, that is Hasan al-Banna. I will not talk in this point, which is the fourth point, about Hasan al-Banna, because I will talk to you tomorrow about this criminal whose crimes still continue to this moment in every place, in the east of the earth and its west.

I have no concern with the opponents of the Ahlulbayt. But the Shia, up until now, still call him Imam and ask for mercy upon him and title him Martyr! While he is a criminal with perhaps no equal in the later eras, the religion of criminals. Since 1928, when the first cells of this ominous organization were formed, the devil began opening the doors of killing, bombing, and slaughter, up to our present day. And this matter will continue until the time of the Sufyani. There is no salvation from this terrorism and this criminality except at the hands of the Imam of our time, whether this is in our era or in coming eras, that is a matter we do not know; it is known by the Imam of our time, Al-Hujjah Ibn Al-Hasan, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him.

The Demonic Plot. A title I can place for this brief idea which I present before you, and in a summarized manner. As I said, this episode serves as main points; I do not want to detail them much. When did the devil's war with the Messenger of Allah, may Allah bless him and his family, begin? It began since the Saqifah! Rather, even before the Saqifah, when the people wrote the document. And Al-Hussein was killed the day the document was written, as is the culture of the family of Allah, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon them all.

And when the Saqifah came, what did it raise? It raised the slogan of the Caliphate. And this title remained as an alternative to the methodology of the Book and the Itrah. It is the methodology of the document titled with the title of the Caliphate. In our hadiths in the book of Sulaym ibn Qays: **The first Caliph, the first to pledge allegiance to him was Iblis.** The narrations say this, it is not I who am saying it; the Commander of the Faithful says the first to pledge allegiance to him was Iblis. From here started the Iblisi program and the demonic plot.

And the Caliphate was to mislead the Ummah from the methodology of the Book and the Itrah, and which raised its slogan, The Book of Allah is sufficient for us. And it broke the pledge of Al-Ghadir, which took in its conditions that the interpretation of religion, the interpretation of the Quran, and the understanding must be from Ali and from Ali only. That is the methodology of the Book and the Itrah. And this game continued, the game of the Caliphate, until its hateful, cursed, ominous page was folded when the Nasibi Ottoman State ended, and that page was folded.

It was possible for the project of the Book and the Itrah to breathe if men had carried this project and had not melted into Nasibi thought. But the project that walked struttingly was another demonic project! It is an alternative to the project of the demonic Caliphate: it is the project of the Muslim Brotherhood group, which spread in all regions. You know that the Muslim Brotherhood group, thus they claim, that they are present in more than a hundred countries. Of course, they count even if there are only one or two people from them, they consider that a presence for themselves. But we, in reality, find them clearly and at the level of politics and governance in most Arab countries and in many Muslim countries. And even in countries not described as Islamic, they are present in their institutions, their societies, and their headquarters.

This demonic project came as an alternative to the methodology of the Book and the Itrah, and the Shia ran behind this project! **So they found in it an alternative to the Imam of their time.** The Shia did not go searching for a project that connects them to the Imam of their time, or a project that paves the way for the Imam of their time. They ran behind Nasibi projects! These Nasibi projects replaced the previous demonic Caliphate, which Satan originally brought as an alternative to the methodology of the Book and the Itrah.

This is the tale and the story. Believe it or do not believe it, this matter is up to you. I am not addressing the Sunnis, I have no connection to them fundamentally; they reject all these premises upon which my idea is built. Rather, I am addressing you. You who say that we are the Shia of the Book and the Itrah. O Shia of the Book and the Itrah, was not the Caliphate an alternative to the methodology of the Book and the Itrah? Did not this group, the Muslim Brotherhood, come as an alternative to the Caliphate? Why do you cling to it? Why do you run behind it?

Why is all this Brotherhood and Qutbist thought on our pulpits? You may say, How? This is what I will explain to you in the coming episodes so you know that the malignant Qutbist cancer has gnawed your intellects and destroyed your cultural reality. And I am among you, I am one of you.

This demonic plot expanded widely. It expanded with the soft style they pretend to have, and it expanded at the level of the international organization; they have what is called the International Organization. And they have what is called the Secret Organization, the Special Apparatus. And they have what is called the Financial Organization; they have banks in Europe and outside Europe. This financial organization and that massive financial empire of this group, which the leaders enjoy, yet they impose on the followers to pay money, or to pay subscription fees, while they live the life of emperors. Those are facts present in the books and on the ground. And I emerged from among them.

And all of this manifests clearly in all those terrorist groups that emerged from under its cloak. **All the crimes that fell upon the Shia of Iraq are from under the cloak of Hasan al-Banna, and from the thought of Sayyid Qutb, and from the tails of this ominous organization!** But what do we do about our leaders, our scholars, and our Marjas, while they pant after them! The problem is they don't take money from them; **they** give them money! And I will talk about this issue also, and with documents. For I do not slander anyone. Everything will come to you with documents, facts, and precise details. We go to a third dramatic break. And after the break, I will return to you.
Segment 10: Dramatic Reenactment: The Brotherhood's Financial Hypocrisy

The Sheikh presents another dramatic scene from the series Al-Gama'a. This sequence exposes the financial opportunism and ideological hypocrisy of the Muslim Brotherhood. It depicts the Brotherhood's leadership secretly accepting a hefty financial donation (500 pounds) from the foreign-owned Suez Canal Company—the very imperialists they publicly claimed to oppose. When confronted about the ethics of taking money from foreign occupiers, Hasan al-Banna twists the logic, arguing that since Egyptian workers dug the canal, the money rightfully belongs to the Egyptian people, thereby justifying the acceptance of the funds.
[Dramatic Scene]

I am Al-Baradei, manager of the Canal Company. Is it possible to speak with you in English or French? I speak the language of the Quran. In this case, the government will provide a translator. Welcome, welcome. O beloved brothers, welcome. Praise be to Allah for your safety. May Allah keep you safe. We have a problem, your eminence the Guide. A very big problem. First of all regarding the topics and expenses. I heard the following speech. May our Lord protect us from the evil of discord, but we want whether it is me, or those with you, or those not with you. As for the money that reached us from the Baron, it is exactly 500 pounds, no more. And the one who received the amount is the treasurer, and he is also the one responsible for the mosque's affairs from the financial side, Hajj Muhammad Ballout. And he justified not presenting the matter to you.

I believe you shouldn't have this problem in accepting any donation! Especially since it is not the foreigners' money! The money belongs to Egypt, the property of Egypt, on the land of Egypt. **The digging was done by Egyptian workers. The fact that the Canal is managed by its owners, and the foreigners usurped it through trickery, cunning, and deceit, means that the money of the Canal rightfully belongs to the country in which the Canal resides.** It is the right of the workers who dug it. Naturally, your eminence the Guide can we consider this speech a religious fatwa? No fatwa or anything to be sad about. It's not a fatwa. It is sound logic and a correct opinion. This is the speech. By Allah, regarding what you are saying about the issue, we want we want to take the religious opinion and the logical opinion that you are talking about. Peace be upon you. We utilize the value of the gift. Is the problem solved, O group, or not?
Segment 11: The Enigma of the Brotherhood and the Thick Mask of Hasan al-Banna

Sheikh al-Ghizzi resumes his commentary with his sixth major observation. He describes the Muslim Brotherhood, Hasan al-Banna, and Sayyid Qutb as unprecedented historical enigmas due to their massive, unnatural influence across both Sunni and Shia spheres. He critiques the television series for failing to expose al-Banna's true demonic face, noting that the show only managed to portray the "thick mask" of piety he wore to deceive the masses. The Sheikh promises to dismantle this mask with scientific precision and absolute fairness, regardless of the backlash he might face from Shia admirers of al-Banna.

The Sixth Observation. An appropriate expression to express the reality of the Muslim Brotherhood group is that, for whoever wants to look at the matter from the outside, history has never witnessed an organization with this level of alignment and this level of influence!

And Sayyid Qutb is also an enigma. If we look at the vastness of the circle of his influence in the Islamic atmospheres, in the Sunni milieu or in the Shia milieu, there is a massive, clear, and sweeping influence for the ideas of Sayyid, for the methodology of Sayyid Qutb, for his authorships, his books, his exegesis, for everything that is Qutbist! We have not witnessed an intellectual in the Islamic atmosphere in the past with this level of influence. There are organizations that had influence, but to a certain limit. And there are thinkers who had influence, but to a certain limit.

The influence of the Muslim Brotherhood group in the Islamic milieu is a very strange and very vast influence, and we have not witnessed in history such an influence for any other organization! **And likewise is Sayyid Qutb. The Muslim Brotherhood is an enigma among enigmas. And likewise, Sayyid Qutb is an enigma among enigmas.** What is the secret in it? We will not be able to know the enigma of the Muslim Brotherhood until we know the enigma of Sayyid Qutb. And we will not know the enigma of Sayyid Qutb until we know the enigma of Hasan al-Banna. Therefore, my speech in the coming episode will begin with Hasan al-Banna. Because if we know his enigma and analyze his personality, and dismantle his secrets so that his demonic face appears to us poured all over the screen.

Even the series, which we are showing a part of, and which the author and the director tried in the first part where it dealt with the life of Hasan al-Banna tried to be fair, to be realistic, to be a chronicler of historical events it did not succeed in that! **The series did not highlight the demonic face of Hasan al-Banna.** The series is successful artistically, culturally, successful in the high movement of authorship, successful in the dramatic movement, taking, successful in all directions. But unfortunately, from my point of view, I have no concern with others and how they evaluate this series, this series did not show the demonic face of Hasan al-Banna, which is the true face.

The series showed the thick mask of the personality of Hasan al-Banna and contributed greatly to this thick mask. There is a thick mask worn by Hasan al-Banna, and with it, he covered his ugly demonic face. Absolutely, his lovers from the Sunnis and the Shia will reject my speech. And his passionate lovers from the Shia will rush to defend him. Congratulations to them with their beloved, and I have no concern with them; they are free in what they believe, and I am free in what I believe.

So we are before a group of enigmas: the enigma of the Muslim Brotherhood, the enigma of Sayyid Qutb, and the enigma of Hasan al-Banna. And the beginning is from Hasan al-Banna when we think and analyze his personality according to correct data. I do not want to slander Hasan al-Banna, nor anyone else. And I do not want to say something which, between me and my Lord, I do not believe to be scientifically true. I will be fair according to my point of view. Perhaps this is not considered fairness, and is considered injustice in your view. But according to my point of view, I will be fair to the utmost extent that I can be fair in research, study, investigation, and analysis.

I do not want to laugh at you, just as the religious establishment laughed at us! It laughed at me and at others. I do not want to deceive you. And the Shia religious establishment, by the way, is also laughed at; it too is deceived, it too is miserable. So I do not want to laugh at you. I do not want to deceive you. I want to place the facts before your hands, and I want you to feel them for yourselves. Listen to my speech, do not accept it; research behind it. Ensure its precision, and examine the documents that will be presented. The Seventh Point, and it is the last, with which I conclude my speech. But before I reach it, we go to a break and return to you after the break.
Segment 12: The Absolute Imamate of Al-Banna and the Freemasonry of Qutb

In his final point, the Sheikh exposes the true underlying motives of the Brotherhood's founders. Hasan al-Banna did not merely want to restore the Caliphate; he actively sought an "absolute Imamate" where he would be worshipped and obeyed unconditionally by his followers, earning titles like the "Luminous Guide." Sayyid Qutb, a contemporary who spent a period of his life as an atheist and a Freemason, initially mocked al-Banna, calling him "Hasan al-Sabbah" (the founder of the Hashashin/Assassins). However, after al-Banna's death, Qutb adopted his framework to seek his own absolute intellectual Imamate, using terrorism and bloodshed to achieve it. The Sheikh concludes that al-Banna was the seed of terrorism, and Qutb was its fruit—a bitter harvest still tearing the world apart today.

Hasan al-Banna used to wear a thick mask. This mask tells others: I am working for Islam, and for Islam only. But behind this mask, when Hasan al-Banna raised the slogan of the Caliphate, he did not want the Caliphate. **He was searching for an absolute Imamate! He wanted to be for the Ummah as the Messenger of Allah was!** He was searching for an absolute Imamate, committing to command and forbid to very far limits. Therefore, his followers believe in his infallibility. Even if they did not declare that verbally, practically they believe in his infallibility.

Sayyid Qutb was a contemporary of Hasan al-Banna. Do you know they were born in the same year? But Sayyid Qutb was a Freemason! And the hadith about this will come. Sayyid Qutb had no connection to religion. Sayyid Qutb lived a period as an atheist; he talks about himself, and the facts and documents testify to that. He used to name Hasan al-Banna when he would enter into an argument of speech with his nephew who belonged to the Muslim Brotherhood group he used to name Hasan al-Banna as Hasan al-Sabbah. I will talk about Hasan al-Sabbah.

But after Hasan al-Banna died, Sayyid Qutb began to ask for mercy upon him. Because Sayyid Qutb, too, was obsessed with absolute leadership. So when death took Hasan al-Banna and cleared the way for Sayyid Qutb, he built upon what Hasan al-Banna had built upon. And Sayyid Qutb also wanted an absolute Imamate! But an intellectual one, and there is no harm in reaching it through terrorism, criminality, and the spilling of blood. And this is what its facts will become clear in the coming episodes.

The problem here is that Hasan al-Banna wanted an absolute Imamate, a worship for himself, and a desire in subjugating the people to him, and casting a character of holiness upon him. Therefore, they used to call him the Luminous Man, or the Luminous Imam, or the Luminous Guide. These are from the names and titles of Hasan al-Banna in the circles of his followers.

As for Sayyid Qutb, the methodology is the same. But Sayyid Qutb was distinguished by his attractive literary style and his deep thought; he is deeper in thought and broader in culture than Hasan al-Banna. I will not prolong much. They express in their circles that Hasan al-Banna is the seed, and that Sayyid Qutb is the fruit. These are their expressions in their Brotherhood circles in Egypt and outside Egypt. **Hasan al-Banna is the seed of terrorism and criminality, and Sayyid Qutb is the fruit of terrorism and criminality!** And we are still plucking these demonic fruits to this day. In Iraq there are bombings, and here in the United Kingdom there are bombings, up to this hour, to your day.

The speech is plentiful. The time of the program has ended. Our meeting is tomorrow in the second episode, and we will begin the details. I will meet you tomorrow. And across this screen, across the screens of the satellite channel. Peace be upon you all.

series: The Malignant Qutbist Cancer in the Arena of Shia Culture
title: The Malignant Qutbist Cancer in the Arena of Shia Culture - Episode 2 (Hasan al-Banna Part 1)
speaker: Sheikh Abdul-Halim al-Ghizzi

content.text: Overview: Episode 2

In the second episode of The Malignant Qutbist Cancer in the Arena of Shia Culture, Sheikh Abdul-Halim al-Ghizzi conducts a profound psychological and historical dissection of Hasan al-Banna, the founder of the Muslim Brotherhood. The Sheikh exposes al-Banna as a deeply mysterious and demonic personality possessing a medium-level genius but driven by a ruthless, boundless ambition for absolute power. By tracing al-Banna's ideological roots, the episode reveals his opportunistic transition from Shafi'i Sufism and tomb visitation to Wahhabi Salafism and its methodology of bloodshed. The Sheikh highlights al-Banna's Machiavellian pragmatism, demonstrating his willingness to prostrate before corrupt kings, collaborate with Western imperialists, and establish a violent Secret Apparatus dedicated to assassination, all while hiding behind a thick mask of religious piety. Furthermore, the episode uncovers the shocking reality of the Brotherhood's infiltration into the highest echelons of the Shia Marja'iyyah, proving that al-Banna manipulated Shia leadership rather than being influenced by it. Ultimately, the Sheikh warns that this organization is a demonic farce that has brought nothing but death and ruin to the Islamic world.
Segment 1: Introduction and Dedication to Lady Fatima

Sheikh al-Ghizzi begins the second episode with the same powerful dedication to Lady Fatima used in the first episode. He quotes her sermon and the tragedy of her martyrdom, reaffirming his absolute loyalty to her and enmity toward her enemies. He reiterates that true immunity against the "Qutbist cancer" lies in total ideological and spiritual devotion to the Ahlulbayt.
In the name of Allah, the Beneficent, the Merciful. Peace be upon you.

Fatima says, and the speech is directed to me and to all of you, as it came in her well-known sermon: "What is this deficiency regarding my right, and this slumber regarding my grievance? Did not the Messenger of Allah, my father, say: 'A man is preserved through his children'?"

And our Imam, Ja'far al-Sadiq, informs us about what happened to his mother, Fatima. And the narration is in the most reliable of our books, Kamil al-Ziyarat: "And she is beaten, O Fatima! And she is beaten while she is pregnant." Until he says: "And she miscarries what is in her womb from the beating, and she dies from that beating."

And I say, and I do not know if you will say it with me or not, just as it came in her honorable visitation: "I am pleased with whomever you are pleased with, and I am enraged at whomever you are enraged at. I disassociate from whomever you disassociated from, I am loyal to whomever you are loyal to, and I am an enemy to whomever you are an enemy to. I hate whomever you hate, and I love whomever you love. O mother of Al-Hasan and Al-Hussein, I call upon the Master of the Command to bear witness to this belief of mine, in my life and in my death. And I wish, by your success, to meet you upon it in this world and the Hereafter."

My brothers and sisters, my sons and daughters. I will not hide a secret from you: **If you are looking for a vaccination or a cure for salvation from the malignant Qutbist cancer, this is the successful cure from this disastrous disease:** You ally with whomever Fatima allies with, and you show enmity to whomever Fatima shows enmity to—intellectually, heartily, in word, and in deed.

Between your hands now is the second episode of The Malignant Qutbist Cancer in the Arena of Shia Culture. May Allah Almighty protect us and you from it, and purify our intellects and yours, our hearts and yours, and our religion and yours from this filth, by the Wilayah of Fatima and the family of Fatima, prayers of Allah be upon her and upon them.
We will go to a break, and after the break, we will return.
Segment 2: Supplication to the Imam of the Time and the Three Enigmas

After the break, the Sheikh recites a beautiful supplication directed to the Imam of the Time (Imam al-Mahdi). He then recaps the conclusion of the previous episode, reminding the audience of the three interconnected historical and ideological enigmas: The Muslim Brotherhood, Sayyid Qutb, and Hasan al-Banna. He explains that to understand the first two, one must first dismantle the mystery of the third: Hasan al-Banna.

O son of the luminous lamps! O son of the piercing meteors! O son of the shining stars! O son of the clear proofs! O son of the apparent signs! O son of the perfect sciences! O son of the renowned Sunnahs! O son of the preserved monuments! O son of the existent miracles! O son of the witnessed evidences! O son of the Straight Path! O son of the Great News! O son of him who is in the Mother of the Book with Allah, Ali and Hakeem!

We reached in the previous episode of this program to a group of enigmas. An enigma titled the Muslim Brotherhood, an enigma titled Sayyid Qutb, and a third enigma titled Hasan al-Banna. **And we will not be able to dismantle the first and second enigmas until we dismantle the third enigma!** In this episode, I will shed light, according to what the context allows, and the rest of the speech will be in tomorrow's episode, God willing. I will shed light on this very mysterious personality: Hasan al-Banna.
Segment 3: The Demonic, Machiavellian Personality of Hasan al-Banna

The Sheikh begins his psychological and historical dissection of Hasan al-Banna. He describes him as a highly complex, mysterious, and demonic figure possessing a "medium-level genius" but boundless, ruthless ambition. Crucially, the Sheikh argues that al-Banna operated with a Machiavellian mindset where nothing was truly forbidden (Haram); he was willing to do whatever it took—including groveling before corrupt kings or exploiting religious rhetoric—to achieve his ultimate goal of absolute personal power.

I have read about this personality a lot. A lot, and a very lot. And if the opportunity permits in the coming episodes, I will present to you a portion of the books that I have read in the past days or in these days. Absolutely, the time is not enough to mention all of them or to bring them here on the table, for they are very many, but I will bring you many of them as well.

According to what I believe, all those who wrote about Hasan al-Banna from both sides—from those who praised him and from those who criticized him—I have not found anyone who has completely uncovered the mask from this mysterious demonic personality.

The personality of Hasan al-Banna is a compound and complex personality. Hasan al-Banna was distinguished by a genius of a medium degree, according to what I believe. And when I say of a medium degree, it is in comparison to the geniuses in history, and even in our present time, who reached very high degrees in their genius. There is intelligence in a human being that has ranks and degrees, but genius is what comes after the highest degrees of intelligence. So if a person surpasses the highest degrees of intelligence, he will be in the rank of genius. And genius has ranks. According to what I believe, Hasan al-Banna possessed a genius of a medium degree. And he had high resolve, trying to achieve his ambition which is boundless, but in a hidden manner, in a very hidden manner.

A mysterious personality wrapped in genius, high intelligence with a degree of genius of a medium limit, with high resolve and boundless ambition. And at the same time, he is ready to do everything! **So there does not exist in his dictionary what is forbidden and what is permissible**, even if he pretends to do so; it absolutely does not exist in his dictionary!

Through browsing his states and the precise details of his situations, this truth manifests itself, at least to me. I do not impose my opinion on anyone, but rather, as I said in the past episode, this program is a medical dissection, directed to the youth of the Shia of Al-Hujjah Ibn Al-Hasan; it is not directed to any other party. And at the same time, I do not demand the acceptance of my speech and believing it. They must use their intellects, and they must examine and verify all the facts that I will lay before them.

I return to this mysterious demonic personality. Between the genius, the high resolve, and the boundless ambition, there is an area of what is permissible and what is not permissible in the path of achieving his personal goals, which have no connection neither to the goals of Islam nor to the goals of religion. **He is searching for an absolute Imamate!** This area, the area of what is permissible and what is not permissible, is a hidden area. Everything is permissible in the path of achieving his goals, but he masks that with a mask containing what is permissible and what is not permissible.

If we were to browse the life of this man, we would find that when he needs to claim that he is independent of rulers and kings, he turns to Allah and holds fast to the handhold of Allah. He will prolong the speech and embellish the talk, and he was capable of speaking and oratory to a good degree. **But when he needs to kiss the hands of kings, rather even their shoes—we possess pictures of him kissing the hands of kings—but a personality like this personality is ready to kiss anything in the path of achieving its goals and reaching its interests and purposes.**

And when he needs to be in the service of corrupt kings, like the kings of Egypt at that time, he will be in the position of service, obedience, and submission. And the swords of his media, and the swords of his group, will be entirely and completely in the service of the thrones of those kings. And when he needs to kiss the hand of an ignorant Bedouin king, he falls prostrating, kissing his hand! All of that is to achieve his goals and reach his personal interests. If he could do that in secret, he did, and if the matter appeared and reached his followers, he would deceive them with his sweet words.

The story is like this all along the line. He is fully ready to serve the government at any time. Segment 8: The Brotherhood’s Penetration of the Shia Marja'iyyah

In a shocking revelation, the Sheikh details the fourth major element of al-Banna's personality network: his relationship with Twelver Shiism. He clarifies that al-Banna was not spiritually influenced by the Shia; rather, al-Banna manipulated and influenced the highest echelons of the Shia religious establishment (Marja'iyyah). The Sheikh explains that the representative of the supreme Shia authority of the time, Sayyid Al-Boroujerdi, actually lived inside the Muslim Brotherhood headquarters in Cairo. Millions in Shia religious funds were funneled by Al-Boroujerdi to the Brotherhood under the guise of "Dar Al-Taqrib" (The House of Proximity). The Brotherhood's influence over the Shia leadership was so profound that after al-Banna's assassination, an Iranian Shia Marja', Sayyid Abu al-Qasim al-Kashani, was seriously nominated to become the next Supreme Guide of the Muslim Brotherhood.

The fourth factor or the fourth source: his relationship with the Shia. His relationship with the Shia, known as the Twelver Imamiyyah, was a very strong relationship. But he was not influenced much by the Twelver Imami Shia; rather, he influenced them!

He influenced them through the Marja'iyyah of Sayyid Al-Boroujerdi in the Hawza of Qom. **The representative of Sayyid Al-Boroujerdi, the representative of the Marja'iyyah in Cairo, where did he sleep? Where did he sleep? He used to sleep in the headquarters of the Muslim Brotherhood group in Cairo!** His stay was there, and he used to spend the large sums of money sent to him by Sayyid Hussein Al-Boroujerdi, he spent them on the Brotherhood and on the institution called Dar Al-Taqrib, which Hasan al-Banna was behind. And much money was spent in this regard.

So Hasan al-Banna influenced the Shia reality through the Marja'iyyah of Sayyid Hussein Al-Boroujerdi and through the Marja'iyyah of Sayyid Abu Al-Qasim Al-Kashani. To the point that they nominated Sayyid Abu Al-Qasim Al-Kashani to be the Guide of the Brotherhood after Hasan al-Banna! After the killing of Hasan al-Banna, several personalities were nominated for the position of General Guide of the Muslim Brotherhood group. One of these personalities was Sayyid Abu Al-Qasim Al-Kashani, the Iranian Marja'. He was dual between Najaf and Qom, for he was in Najaf and was from the Marjas of Najaf, from the prominent scientific personalities in Najaf, and after that, he moved to Iran. And he had a very close relationship with Hasan al-Banna and had meetings with him in Mecca, and we will talk about this subject in what is to come when we reach the Shia arena.

As a result, these relationships will leave an impact in one way or another. But who influenced heavily? Did the Shia influence the personality of Hasan al-Banna? Never! Rather, Hasan al-Banna is the one who influenced the Shia Marjas.
We go to a dramatic break, and after the dramatic break, I will return to you.
Segment 9: Dramatic Reenactment: The Formation of the Secret Apparatus

The Sheikh presents a chilling, 30-minute dramatic sequence from the series Al-Gama'a. The scene depicts Hasan al-Banna secretly gathering his most trusted followers in an abandoned location to form a private, armed militia—the "Secret Apparatus." He extracts a terrifying oath of absolute allegiance, secrecy, and obedience from them. Al-Banna declares that the group's purpose is to fight colonialism and the enemies of the "Call," explicitly stating that anyone who betrays their secrets is permitted to be killed. He even instructs them to use the word "Quran" (Mushaf) as a code word for a gun.
[Dramatic Scene]

Of course, some of you, or most of you, are asking why the Sheikh gathered us in this strange and almost abandoned place from many days past. And I rely on isolation when I think. Yes, your eminence the Guide, but what was your eminence thinking about in childhood? Thinking about what, exactly? I am preoccupied with the issue of Islam since inception and until the end of my life, God willing. By the way, your eminence the Guide, why doesn't your eminence author books to serve Islam? Your question is in its place, brother Mahmoud. I do not have time to author books. My time is entirely for the Call. My mission is preparing men. The time that I would waste authoring a book, it is better that we utilize it in preparing the youth of the Group. A hundred young men, when they spread among the people, will be equivalent to a thousand books! That is why I chose you and gathered you. And the speech I will say is extremely secret and strictly confidential. What we agree upon will be a secret between us and Allah Almighty. And whoever divulges the secret will be a traitor to us and to Allah, the Mighty and Majestic. Your eminence the Guide, we are your followers and your disciples, and around our necks is a pledge of allegiance to your eminence.

I know. May Allah bless you, may Allah bless you all. I wanted to emphasize the extent of the importance of absolute and intense secrecy, to the degree of concealment, over everything that concerns the Group, especially the active elements. And you are the first of the active elements. Listen to me. The first assignment, we will read together the Wird of the Bond. The Wird of the Bond? Yes, the Wird of the Bond. The bond which gathers us upon sincerity, loyalty, obedience, commitment, and sacrifice. I want every one of you to close his eyes, and summon and bring forth the image of any person from his brothers in his mind and in his imagination, and feel the spiritual connection between him and his brothers in the Group, those he knows and those he doesn't know. And pray for all of them with the following supplication: O Allah, You know that these hearts have gathered upon Your love and met upon Your obedience and united upon Your Call and pledged upon the victory of Your Sharia. So strengthen, O Allah, its bond and perpetuate its affection and guide it to its paths and fill it with Your light which does not fade and expand its breast with the overflow of faith in You and beautiful reliance upon You and revive it with Your gnosis and let us die upon martyrdom in Your path You are the excellent Master and the excellent Helper. O Allah, amen. And may Allah bless our Master Muhammad, and his family and companions, and grant them peace.

I want every one of you to read this Wird every day in the manner I explained to you, once or twice, God willing. You know the extent of my interest in the students. And I found, given the importance of the matter and its gravity, it is necessary to form a committee for the students. And this committee is you, and I am responsible for it. And the activity of the committee is everything I commission you with. There is one of you who has not pledged allegiance yet. Me, your eminence the Guide. God willing, we will take the pledge of allegiance from you in the headquarters, God willing. I want you to know that this committee or branch of students is just like the ministries of defense and war in importance. And you, from this moment, are the commanders of the soldiers of the Group.

I pledge to Allah, the High, the Great, to hold fast to the Call of the Muslim Brotherhood, and to perform Jihad in its path, and to fulfill the conditions of its membership, and to have complete trust in its leadership, and to hear and obey in what I like and what I dislike. And I swear by Allah the Great upon that, and I pledge allegiance upon it, and Allah is over what I say a Disposer of Affairs! Praise be to Allah, and thanks be to Allah, and we ask Him Almighty that your presence in the Group be a goodness for it.

Welcome, O brothers. Why are you standing? Please come in. Is it good, your eminence the Guide? Good, God willing, it is good. First of all, is there anyone among you who does not know the other? Meaning, a simple acquaintance? I know you with a very solid acquaintance. That is why I gathered you together for the most dangerous responsibility concerning the Group. Our brother in Allah, Salih Ashmawi. Our brother in Allah, Hussein Kamal al-Din. Our brother in Allah, Hamid Shurd. Our brother in Allah, Abd al-Aziz Ahmad. And our brother in Allah, Mahmoud Abd al-Halim. I think like this, every one of you knows the name of his brother.

In the name of Allah, the Beneficent, the Merciful: 'Fighting has been enjoined upon you while it is hateful to you' Allah the Great has spoken the truth. Truth requires force! And we are a group of truth, but without force. Force is necessary and essential. The time has come for the Group to have its own private, trained, and armed army. An army? Is this other than the rovers, your eminence the Guide? The rovers are a public army, visible to all people, and its role is known and understood. The army that I thought of, and for which I gathered you to prepare the sound and good preparation, is a secret army. Let us not use the word army, rather it is an organization. If you permit me, your eminence the Guide. Patience, O Mahmoud, patience. I will say everything you want to hear. Patience. I am sorry, please continue.

The purpose of this organization is to wage a war on British colonialism. The time has come for the Group to have an active role in resisting the English. Allah is the Greatest! And fighting those who oppose the Call, trying to obstruct its progress. Is it clear or not clear? What is not clear now will become clear later, your eminence the Guide. God willing, everything will be clear. The third goal of the organization is reviving the duty of Jihad. Allah is the Greatest! The system of families guarantees for the individuals of the Group the spiritual and psychological formation. As for the new organization, it is the practical and executive field for Jihad. And this organization, all its affairs are secret! **Whoever divulges a secret of its secrets, it is permissible to kill him.** The organization will be based on a system of cells. Every cell has five men. And over them is an Emir. And we consider ourselves the mother cell, and you are the individuals of the cell, and I am your Emir. And before we proceed with the structural work of the organization, the conditions for choosing the individuals of the organization, and the specifications of every one of them, we read Al-Fatihah. Then you swear the oath of obedience and secrecy. In the name of Allah, the Beneficent, the Merciful.
I swear by Allah the Great upon hearing and obeying in what I like and what I dislike and concealing the secret and Allah is over what I say a Witness.

Any individual among you must call things by other than their names. Let's assume, for example, that one of you will ask his colleague and say to him, 'Send me a revolver.' Of course, the word revolver must have an alternative that does not arouse suspicion or doubt. **Meaning, for example, he says to him, 'Send me the Quran.'** In this case, he says it to him openly, he writes it to him on a piece of paper, 'You are asking for a Quran.' And so on, and so on in everything, and that is to ensure secrecy and the concealment of our affairs.
Segment 10: The Demonic Mockery of the Secret Apparatus and Conclusion

Sheikh al-Ghizzi comments on the dramatic scene, labeling the Brotherhood's "Secret Apparatus" a demonic farce. While they claimed to build an army to fight colonialists and infidels, their actual history consists almost entirely of murdering fellow Muslims, Shia, and innocent civilians. He summarizes al-Banna's complex, ruthless personality and the diverse sources of his ideology (Sufism, Wahhabism, Ismailism/Hasan al-Sabbah). He ends by teasing the next episode's focus: al-Banna's connection to Freemasonry and Mahdism.

From the farces of this terrorist organization, the Muslim Brotherhood, and the rest of the terrorist organizations that split and branched from it: they prepare military equipment and say they will fight the infidels! But if we examined their history, most of their operations, if not all the operations for many of these movements, are to kill Muslims from the sons of their own sects, or from the Shia of the Ahlulbayt, or to kill innocent people who have no power and no strength!

A clear demonic farce. A clear mockery. These people kill themselves, kill the sons of their own people, and kill those who have no stake in the matter, neither in politics nor outside of politics. All of this points clearly to the fact that the fingers of Satan are what moves these people. He moves them to the destruction of themselves and to the destruction of others! And Satan guffaws laughing at this Imam! And what an Imam? From this Imam who hides his criminality behind a mask called Islam!

This slogan they raise, "Islam is the solution." What Islam is this? It is the Islam of Hasan al-Banna! It is not the Islam of Muhammad, may Allah bless him and his family. The Islam of Hasan al-Banna is the Islam of terrorism and crime, the Islam of spilling blood and killing. This Islam which the Saqifah read, then Hasan al-Banna read it, and so it was distorted according to his ambitions and his personal desires.

Are the facts complete? Never. The summary of what has passed until now: The personality of Hasan al-Banna is a compound, complex personality. He has genius in the medium limit, and he has high resolve in achieving his goals. His ambition is boundless. He does not know what is forbidden or prohibited in his path to achieving his personal interests. He wears a thick mask of Islam, but this mask crumbles, and this dignity and arrogance crumble, when he needs a lowly or despised goal, and when he needs to flatter kings and rulers. He establishes terrorism and spills blood, and he has set a bad precedent, bearing its burden and the burden of whoever acts upon it until the Day of Judgment! Woe unto him, and woe unto his followers. There is nothing forbidden.

The sources of this personality are between Shafi'i Sufism, and between Salafi Wahhabism, and between Ismailism apparent in the personality of Hasan al-Sabbah, this mysterious personality as well. With the influences of his relations with the English and others, according to what politics and interests dictate. And with the culture he became acquainted with through his reading of communism. And through his relationship with the Shia, and he influenced them greatly, and I do not believe they influenced him except for a tiny fraction, and it influenced him with a Shafi'i Shiism as well.

There remains very important speech. Speech of extreme importance. There are two important titles: **Freemasonry and Hasan al-Banna. Mahdism and Hasan al-Banna.** These two titles are the important ones in the structure of Hasan al-Banna's personality. This speech comes to us in tomorrow's episode, God willing.

May Allah Almighty protect us and you from this malignant cancer. Purify our intellects and yours, our hearts and yours, our religion and yours from this filth. I seek refuge in Your Generous Face, my Master, O Master of the Command, from ever being a malignant Qutbist in any moment of the moments of my life, prayers of Allah be upon you, O Remainder of Allah. I ask you all for supplication, in the safety of Allah.

series: The Malignant Qutbist Cancer in the Arena of Shia Culture
title: The Malignant Qutbist Cancer in the Arena of Shia Culture - Episode 3 (Hasan al-Banna Part 2 & Masonic Influences)
speaker: Sheikh Abdul-Halim al-Ghizzi

content.text: Overview: Episode 3

In the third episode of The Malignant Qutbist Cancer in the Arena of Shia Culture, Sheikh Abdul-Halim al-Ghizzi deepens his dissection of Hasan al-Banna's ideological roots, shifting focus to the profound **indirect influence of Freemasonry** on the Muslim Brotherhood. The Sheikh systematically dismisses rumors of al-Banna's Jewish ancestry as historically unsubstantiated, arguing that a man's lineage is irrelevant compared to his ideological actions. Instead, he meticulously traces the Masonic ideological lineage from the "illuminated" Freemasonry of Egypt back to Jamal al-Din al-Afghani—revealed here as a Twelver Shia from Iran who became a documented Freemason. Through al-Afghani's disciples, Muhammad 'Abduh and Rashid Rida, the Masonic concept of the **"Unity of Religions"** was rebranded and injected into Islamic thought, eventually mutating into al-Banna's dangerous theory of "Islam without sects." The episode culminates in a fierce condemnation of the Shia religious establishment for blindly adopting the famous Masonic-derived slogan, "We cooperate in what we agree upon, and excuse each other in what we disagree upon," noting its direct contradiction to the core Shia doctrine of **Bara'ah** (disassociation from the enemies of the Ahlulbayt).
Segment 1: Recap of al-Banna's Demonic Nature and the Sources of His Thought

Sheikh al-Ghizzi begins the third episode by renewing his pledge of allegiance to Lady Fatima. He then recaps the major points of the previous episodes regarding the complex, Machiavellian personality of Hasan al-Banna. The Sheikh reminds the audience how al-Banna used a distorted version of Islam as a mask to hide his true ambition: achieving absolute Imamate over the entire world. Reviewing the influences of Sufism, Wahhabism, and Western powers on al-Banna, the Sheikh emphasizes that al-Banna is nothing short of a "tyrant worshipped besides Allah," warning the Shia who still revere him today. He then previews the next topics of discussion: al-Banna's connection to Freemasonry and Mahdism.
In the name of Allah, the Beneficent, the Merciful. Peace be upon you.

Fatima says, and the speech is directed to me and to you all, as it came in her well-known sermon: "What is this deficiency regarding my right, and this slumber regarding my grievance? Did not the Messenger of Allah, my father, say: 'A man is preserved through his children'?"

And our truthful Imam, Ja'far, informs us about what happened to his mother, Fatima. And the narration is in the most reliable of our books, Kamil al-Ziyarat: "And Fatima is beaten while she is pregnant, and she miscarries what is in her womb from the beating, and she dies from that beating."

And I say, and I do not know if you will say it with me or not, just as it came in her visitation: "I am pleased with whomever you are pleased with, and I am enraged at whomever you are enraged at. I disassociate from whomever you disassociated from, I am loyal to whomever you are loyal to, and I am an enemy to whomever you are an enemy to. I hate whomever you hate, and I love whomever you love. O mother of Al-Hasan and Al-Hussein, I call upon the Master of the Command to bear witness to this belief of mine, in my life and in my death. And I wish, by your success, to meet you upon it in this world and the Hereafter."

My brothers and sisters, my sons and daughters. I will not hide a secret from you: **If you are looking for a vaccination or a cure for salvation from the malignant Qutbist cancer, this is the successful cure from this disastrous disease:** You ally with whomever Fatima allies with, and you show enmity to whomever Fatima shows enmity to—intellectually, heartily, in word, and in deed.

Between your hands now is the third episode of The Malignant Qutbist Cancer in the Arena of Shia Culture. May Allah Almighty protect us and you from it, and purify our intellects and yours, our hearts and yours, and our religion and yours from this filth, by the Wilayah of Fatima and the family of Fatima, prayers of Allah be upon her and upon them. A break, then we return.

The speech in yesterday's episode, which is the second episode of this program, was on the dissection or analysis of the mysterious, compound, and complex personality of the Imam of the Muslim Brotherhood group, Hasan al-Banna. The speech passed on the most important specifications of his personality. Then the speech moved to the most important sources that he was influenced by, connected with, and had some relationship with in one way or another. A group of sources are what led to the formation of his final personality.

In summary, I said that Hasan al-Banna used to wear a thick mask named Islam. In the beginning, this Islam was the Islam of the Saqifah, but it gradually transformed into the Islam of Hasan al-Banna. **So he manufactured his own Islam by himself as a mask to disguise himself and hide his grand goal: his goal of absolute Imamate, not for the Muslims only, but for all people!** He aspired to be an absolute Imam over the whole earth, and he was ready to do everything to achieve this goal. I talked about this result in the previous two episodes, and it will manifest more clearly in this episode and in the coming episodes.

I talked about the first source that nourished and was nourishing the personality of Hasan al-Banna: Sufism. For the man since his tender fingernails was upon the Shafi'i Hasafiyyah order, and his father was like that as well; his father was inclined towards this direction.

And I also talked about the Salafi Wahhabism which he transitioned to, admiring it and desiring to acquire benefits through it. And I told you about what his friend and colleague Ahmad al-Sukkari relayed, and how he used to walk on his feet to visit the Awliya (saints) according to their belief, and how he was regular in visiting the Awliya in Cairo. But when he found that his interests were achieved through relationships and through connection with the Salafi and Wahhabi facades, he evaded all of that, and said to Ahmad al-Sukkari: "We do not want to be grave-worshippers, or for people to say about us that we are grave-worshippers." The speech passed on this and I do not want to repeat.

And I also talked about his relationship with the English and the Americans. And the evidences, proofs, and facts are many for that, scattered in the books. And the issue is not hidden from those informed about the conditions of the Muslim Brotherhood group, whether that was in the time of Hasan al-Banna or in our present time all along the line. I did not accuse al-Banna of treason for the English or for the Americans, never. But as I said, all parties, all gatherings that seek governance and that oppose in their country, search for a cover for themselves and for support for themselves with major countries, and this matter is well-known and I do not want to delve into it. So when I talked about his relationship with the English and the Americans, I only wanted to say that **Hasan al-Banna does not leave a small or big thing that serves his goals, regardless of his false mask which he called Islam; he searches for his interests everywhere.**

I do not want to repeat the details that passed in the previous episode, but I only point to the main points so that the speech can continue in this episode. And I pointed to his relationship with the Twelver Imami Shia, and the great influence he had on the supreme Shia religious authority in Iran. And I pointed to Sayyid Al-Boroujerdi, and likewise I pointed to the political Marja'iyyah in its time, Abu al-Qasim al-Kashani.

The detail about these issues will come when we talk about his Qutbists and about the Qutbist tide in the Shia arena. The speech will come to us in detail. He was not influenced much by the Twelver Shia, and when we examine the precise details of his positions, he shows harmony with them, but at the same time he withdraws clearly considering that he is fundamentally a Sunni. He does not care about his Sunnism; he cares about this god that erupts inside him, he cares about his personality and his personal goal and Imamate which he wants to achieve and which he could not achieve. **In a brief phrase, according to the culture of the Ahlulbayt, this man is a tyrant worshipped besides Allah!**

For everyone who calls to the Imamate of himself and considers himself a true Imam is a tyrant worshipped besides Allah. So I say to the Shia who worship him: you are worshipping a tyrant besides the Imam of your time! There are many in the Shia reality who worship this tyrant, glorify him, and magnify him up to this minute, rather up to this second of our time! And the matter exacerbates day after day. I will talk to you in the coming episodes about the fatal, toxic, and deadly effects that gnawed the arena of Shia culture, and the speech will come to us in detail.

We are with the personality of Hasan al-Banna and with the sources that formed this personality. This relationship must leave some mark, and even if it was not clear in his personality, perhaps he quoted something, but he returned to his thought opposing the Ahlulbayt, and tried to harmonize between what he quoted perhaps from the Shia. It is a natural thing when a person like Hasan al-Banna has a relationship with a party of the parties in a certain direction, he will take something from it, he will deal with this thing positively or negatively. In the end, this relationship will leave a mark.

But the big, clear mark on the personality of Hasan al-Banna is from the Ismaili reality. From the Ismaili sect, which is considered by some as from the Shia, but they are not from the Shia according to the standards of the Ahlulbayt. According to the standards of the opponents and according to the standards of the Shia scholars who talk about sects, about essays, about beliefs and schools of thought, they consider the Ismailis from the Shia sects. In the perspective of the family of Muhammad, they are not from the Shia at all.

I talked about Hasan al-Sabbah and I do not want to repeat the speech. And the reference passed to Communism, which he was not influenced by intellectually and ideologically, for from the slogans of Hasan al-Banna is fighting Communism. But because he was a reader of different cultures, there are those who say, and perhaps this speech is correct, that he was influenced by the culture of Communism in the organizational aspect, in the methods of work, and that is not unlikely.

What I am taking up in this episode, I pointed to in the past episode: a speech about Freemasonry and about Mahdism. And I begin my speech about Freemasonry, and about the relationship of Hasan al-Banna and the Muslim Brotherhood with Masonic thought. But before I talk to you, we go to a dramatic break.
Segment 2: Dramatic Reenactment: The Secret Apparatus and the Industry of Death

A dramatic scene depicts the Muslim Brotherhood's Secret Apparatus reporting to Hasan al-Banna. They discuss infiltrating political parties, strict training in weaponry and codes, and indoctrinating members with a desire for martyrdom based on al-Banna's article, "The Industry of Death." They confirm the purchase of weapons, highlighting the transition from a religious group to an armed, covert militia.

"Now let us check on our great project." "Praise be to Allah, everything is in the best possible state, your eminence the Guide. They have fought a good fight, dazzling results, your eminence the Guide." "Mahmoud, yes, speak Mahmoud, bring joy to his eminence the Guide and reassure him." "Praise be to Allah. Mahmoud, from today onwards, train yourself not to carry any papers written on them anything that can be held against the Group." "God willing, your eminence the Guide. Right now, for the Group, of course, and all of them have sworn. Look, Mahmoud, whoever is in the Wafd Party stays in the Wafd Party, and whoever is in Young Egypt stays as he is, and whoever is in the National Party stays as he is, and the Sa'dist stays Sa'dist. We want seeing eyes for the Group in all these parties, and no one should announce his affiliation to the Muslim Brotherhood group! We want men everywhere, and this indeed will provide us with very important information for your eminence." "God willing. Continue, Mahmoud." "We, your eminence the Guide, have divided the individuals into cells and families with a chain of command, just as your eminence ordered. Praise be to Allah, all members are receiving deep lessons on the virtue of Jihad and everything mentioned regarding it in the Holy Quran, and the encouragement of martyrdom. Mahmoud, there was an article by his eminence the Guide published before, if I recall, called 'The Industry of Death'. Keep it in your consideration, God willing Almighty. Continue." "Training on all exhausting and very strenuous tasks, and training on distributing flyers and methods of escape and hiding. No problem. The use of weapons of all kinds, and training on marksmanship. Great. Communicating in code. Great. Emphasizing hearing and obeying, and concealing the secret." "Praise be to Allah for what we are upon. We have the men, and all we have to do is supply them with weapons. Do you know how to buy weapons? Where do we get the bombs and the revolvers?" "We want three, most of them. Everything you want, by the command of Allah, you will find it available. You bought them, of course? Of course I bought them! Do you think I came all this way just to drink goat's milk?"
Segment 3: Freemasonry, Ancestry Rumors, and the Illusion of Neutrality

Sheikh al-Ghizzi tackles the widespread rumors connecting Hasan al-Banna to Freemasonry and claims of his Jewish Moroccan ancestry. While acknowledging the profound indirect influence of Masonic thought on al-Banna, the Sheikh rejects the rumors of his Jewish lineage due to a lack of evidence, emphasizing that a man's lineage is irrelevant compared to his ideological actions. He pivots to critique the Shia religious establishment for being deeply mired in Qutbist ideology. Finally, he outlines his methodological commitment to "fairness" over the illusion of absolute "neutrality."

Freemasonry. There are those who connect the Muslim Brotherhood group with the organization known as Freemasonry, and there are those who connect Hasan al-Banna with Freemasonry as well. I do not believe that this speech is correct at all. There is no direct link between the Muslim Brotherhood group and Freemasonry, a direct link, at least for me through a long research, there is no link between the Muslim Brotherhood group or between Hasan al-Banna with the Masonic organization.

But one thing is very clear: if we scrutinize the personality of Hasan al-Banna and his ideas, **he was greatly influenced by Masonic thought and by the goals of Freemasonry in an indirect manner!** A very great influence. And this is what I will talk about in this segment of this episode. I will address all the points raised on this topic in a summarized manner and point to the most important data.

In the 1940s, the well-known Egyptian writer, who was a teacher to Sayyid Qutb, Abbas Mahmoud al-Aqqad, raised a point in an article he wrote in one of the well-known newspapers at the time, and spoke of this content in his gatherings and in his public talks, when he said that Hasan al-Banna is not Egyptian. He is Moroccan; his ancestors came from Morocco. And he is Jewish in his origin, so his ancestors are from Morocco, and specifically from the Jews of Morocco!

A controversy erupted at that time in the 1940s, before the killing of Hasan al-Banna, between al-Aqqad and the supporters of al-Aqqad on the intellectual level, and between the Muslim Brotherhood group. Then this topic faded away, and after the events that took place in Egypt, the speech returned anew in the media. Perhaps among the first magazines and publications that talked about this topic in our current time is the well-known Egyptian magazine, and it is an ancient, old magazine, Rose al-Yūsuf. The issue in my hands is 4443, date of issuance August 16, 2013 AD.

The article is present on page 36-37 under the title: "Articles by a group of the most prominent veteran writers of Egypt: These are the Brotherhood," by Mustafa Maher. From among what came in this article, what came quoted from Abbas Mahmoud al-Aqqad, he says: "When we return to the man who established that group," talking about the Muslim Brotherhood group, this is al-Aqqad's speech, "we ask, who is his grandfather exactly? Everything said about him is that he is from Morocco, and that his father was a watchmaker." And it is known that the Jews in Morocco were many, and that the profession of watchmaking was a familiar profession among them. Until Hasan al-Banna learned this matter and was working in repairing watches as an assistant to his father, Sheikh Ahmad al-Sa'ati, his father Sheikh Ahmad Abd al-Rahman al-Sa'ati. "When we return to the man who established that group, we ask, who is his grandfather? No one in Egypt knows who his grandfather is exactly. Everything said about him is that he is from Morocco, and that his father was a watchmaker, and it is known that Jews in Morocco are many, and that the profession of watchmaking is among their familiar professions. And we here in Egypt hardly know of a watchmaker who worked in this profession a generation ago who was not Jewish," to the end of his speech.

This speech was raised by Rose al-Yūsuf magazine, then it appeared in some books. Among these books in which this speech appeared is The Secret Grip: The Burning of Education, the Muslim Brotherhood's Entrance to Control in the Gulf by the journalist Khalid al-Ubaid.
